message.content:
we'd like to add this code: #' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (!use_dual)
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)   # n × q

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B)) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg, b = b, M = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE, tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       x0 = prev_u,
                       verbose = if (verbose) 1L else 0L)
      prev_u <<- res$x                            # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = tail(res$errors, 1))
      res$x
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, ...)

  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- eig$vectors                 # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun)                     # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual)                        # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
} to genplsc.R ;https://github.com/derekbeaton/GPLS/blob/master/Package/R/plsca_cor.R which depends on https://github.com/derekbeaton/GSVD ; now our version should be less memory intensive and possible faster. let's test for accuracy, speed, under a variety of settings.

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares **Correlation** (GPLS‑C)
#' 
#' `genplsc()` extracts latent **correlation directions** between two data
#' sets \eqn{X\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times p}} and \eqn{Y\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times q}}
#' while respecting *row* and *column* metric constraints
#' \eqn{M_x,\,A_x,\,M_y,\,A_y}.  
#' It unifies the “direct cross‑product” formulation and the
#' “row/column‑likeness operator” trick in a single function:
#' 
#' \itemize{
#'   \item **`dual = FALSE`** – explicitly forms the embedded cross‑product
#'         \eqn{C = (\sqrt{M_x}XA_x^{1/2})^\top\,(\sqrt{M_y}Y A_y^{1/2})}
#'         and does (partial) SVD on the \eqn{p\times q} matrix.
#'   \item **`dual = TRUE`**  – avoids the large \eqn{p\times q} object by
#'         projecting onto row‑ or column‑space operators (à la *Allen
#'         et al., 2014*), chosen automatically or via `force_row_likeness`
#'         / `force_col_likeness`.
#' }
#'
#' Unlike `genpls()` from \code{genplsr.R}, which targets a regression setting and
#' extracts latent components that best predict \eqn{Y} from \eqn{X}, `genplsc()`
#' focuses purely on the correlation structure shared between \eqn{X} and \eqn{Y}.
#'
#' For every constraint matrix you may either
#'
#' * give a **fixed rank** (`rank_Mx = 50`) or
#' * leave it `NULL` / `NA` / `0` to let an **adaptive** scheme keep the
#'   smallest eigen‑subspace that captures `var_threshold` of its total
#'   variance (capped by `max_k`).
#' 
#' A truncated SVD is used when `svd_method = "RSpectra"` or `"irlba"` and the
#' package is installed; otherwise base `svd()` is used.
#' 
#' @section Returned object:
#' A [`cross_projector`][multivarious::cross_projector] with slots
#' 
#' \describe{
#'   \item{`vx`, `vy`}{original‑space loadings \eqn{p\times k}, \eqn{q\times k}}
#'   \item{`Tx`, `Ty`}{embedded factor scores \eqn{n\times k}}
#'   \item{`d_full`}{all singular values (length \eqn{\le k})}
#' }
#' and a class vector `c("genplsc", "cross_projector", "projector")`.
#' 
#' @inheritParams build_sqrt_mult
#' @inheritParams build_invsqrt_mult
#' 
#' @param X,Y              Numeric matrices with identical numbers of rows.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay      Row/column constraint matrices (NULL ⇒ identity).
#' @param ncomp            Number of latent factors.  If \eqn{\le 0},
#'                         uses \eqn{\min(p,q)}.

#' @keywords internal
nipals_deflation_gs <- function(Xmat, Ymat, ncomp, maxiter=200, tol=1e-9, verbose=FALSE) {
  n <- nrow(Xmat)
  p <- ncol(Xmat)
  q <- ncol(Ymat)
  
  loadX <- matrix(0, p, ncomp)  # P
  loadY <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)  # Q
  scoreX<- matrix(0, n, ncomp)  # T
  scoreY<- matrix(0, n, ncomp)  # U
  
  Xres <- Xmat
  Yres <- Ymat
  
  for(k in seq_len(ncomp)) {
    if(verbose) cat(sprintf("Extracting comp %d/%d...\n", k, ncomp))

    # initialise vectors for this component
    p_k <- numeric(p)
    q_k <- numeric(q)
    t_n <- numeric(n)

    # initialize u => col j in Yres with max var
    var_ycols <- apply(Yres, 2, var)
    jmax <- which.max(var_ycols)
    u_n <- Yres[, jmax, drop=FALSE]

    valid_comp <- TRUE
    diff_val <- Inf

    for(iter in seq_len(maxiter)) {
      # (1) p_k
      denom_u <- sum(u_n^2)
      if(denom_u < 1e-15) {
        p_k[] <- 0
        q_k[] <- 0
        t_n[] <- 0
        valid_comp <- FALSE
        break
      }
      p_k <- crossprod(Xres, u_n)/ denom_u
      
      # Gram–Schmidt p_k
      for(j in seq_len(k-1)) {
        dot_pp<- sum(p_k * loadX[, j])
        p_k <- p_k - dot_pp* loadX[, j]
      }
      norm_p<- sqrt(sum(p_k^2))
      if(norm_p>1e-15) p_k<- p_k/norm_p else p_k[]=0

1→## tests/testthat/test-genpls.R
     2→
     3→library(testthat)
     4→library(Matrix)
     5→# suppose your package is "myplspackage"
     6→# library(myplspackage)
     7→
     8→test_that("genpls handles basic numeric input and constraints", {
     9→  set.seed(123)
    10→  n <- 10
    11→  px <- 4
    12→  py <- 3
    13→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*px), n, px)
    14→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*py), n, py)
    15→  
    16→  # trivial adjacency => identity
    17→  Ax <- Diagonal(px)
    18→  Mx <- Diagonal(n)
    19→  Ay <- Diagonal(py)
    20→  My <- Diagonal(n)
    21→  
    22→  # fit with 2 comps
    23→  fit <- genpls(X, Y, Mx, Ax, My, Ay, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
    24→  expect_s3_class(fit, c("genpls", "cross_projector", "projector"))
    25→  expect_true(ncol(fit$vx) <= 2)
    26→  expect_true(ncol(fit$vy) <= 2)
    27→  expect_equal(multivarious::ncomp(fit), ncol(fit$vx))
    28→  tol <- 1e-9
    29→  nnz <- sum(colSums(abs(fit$tilde_Px)) > tol)
    30→  expect_equal(multivarious::ncomp(fit), nnz)
    31→})
    32→
    33→test_that("genpls catches non-numeric or invalid ncomp", {
    34→  X_bad <- data.frame(a=1:5, b=6:10)
    35→  Y_good <- matrix(rnorm(5*3), 5, 3)
    36→  expect_error(genpls(X_bad, Y_good), "numeric matrix")
    37→  
    38→  X_ok <- matrix(rnorm(5*2), 5, 2)
    39→  expect_error(genpls(X_ok, Y_good, ncomp=0), "positive integer")
    40→})
    41→
    42→test_that("genpls warns if ncomp > rank", {
    43→  X <- matrix(rnorm(6*2), 6, 2)
    44→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(6*3), 6, 3)
    45→  # ncomp=5 is more than min(6,2)=2
    46→  expect_warning(

## tests/testthat/test-genpls.R

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)
# suppose your package is "myplspackage"
# library(myplspackage)

test_that("genpls handles basic numeric input and constraints", {
  set.seed(123)
  n <- 10
  px <- 4
  py <- 3
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*px), n, px)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*py), n, py)
  
  # trivial adjacency => identity
  Ax <- Diagonal(px)
  Mx <- Diagonal(n)
  Ay <- Diagonal(py)
  My <- Diagonal(n)
  
  # fit with 2 comps
  fit <- genpls(X, Y, Mx, Ax, My, Ay, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
  expect_s3_class(fit, c("genpls", "cross_projector", "projector"))
  expect_true(ncol(fit$vx) <= 2)
  expect_true(ncol(fit$vy) <= 2)
  expect_equal(multivarious::ncomp(fit), ncol(fit$vx))
  tol <- 1e-9
  nnz <- sum(colSums(abs(fit$tilde_Px)) > tol)
  expect_equal(multivarious::ncomp(fit), nnz)
})

test_that("genpls catches non-numeric or invalid ncomp", {
  X_bad <- data.frame(a=1:5, b=6:10)
  Y_good <- matrix(rnorm(5*3), 5, 3)
  expect_error(genpls(X_bad, Y_good), "numeric matrix")
  
  X_ok <- matrix(rnorm(5*2), 5, 2)
  expect_error(genpls(X_ok, Y_good, ncomp=0), "positive integer")
})

test_that("genpls warns if ncomp > rank", {
  X <- matrix(rnorm(6*2), 6, 2)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(6*3), 6, 3)
  # ncomp=5 is more than min(6,2)=2
  expect_warning(
    genpls(X, Y, ncomp=5),
    "exceeds approximate rank"
  )
})

## tests/testthat/test_genplscor.R

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

test_that("genplscor works with identity constraints (basic usage)", {
  set.seed(123)
  n <- 10
  p <- 5
  q <- 3
  
  # Generate random data X, Y
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # Typically, we center for PLS
  Xc <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  Yc <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  
  # Identity constraints => Mx=I_n, Ax=I_p, My=I_n, Ay=I_q
  # We'll do no constraints => default is NULL => identity
  fit_id <- genplsc(Xc, Yc, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
  
  expect_s3_class(fit_id, c("genplscorr","cross_projector","projector"))
  # check dimension of loadings
  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vx), c(p, 2))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vy), c(q, 2))
  
  # project X -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
  Zx <- project(fit_id, Xc, source="X")
  expect_equal(dim(Zx), c(n, 2))
  
  # project Y -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
  Zy <- project(fit_id, Yc, source="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Zy), c(n, 2))
  
  # transfer X->Y => dimension n x q
  Yhat <- transfer(fit_id, Xc, source="X", target="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Yhat), c(n, q))
})


test_that("genplscor works with diagonal col constraints for X, Y", {
  set.seed(999)
  n <- 12
  p <- 4
  q <- 3
  
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)

#' Internal Utilities for Partial Eigen, Adaptive Rank, and Sqrt Transforms
#'
#' These functions are used by both \code{genpls} and \code{genplscorr} to handle
#' partial eigen expansions, diagonal/identity shortcuts, adaptive rank selection,
#' and row/column transformations for data embeddings.

#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom PRIMME eigs_sym
#' @importFrom Matrix diag isDiagonal crossprod
NULL

#' Check if constraint matrix is identity or purely diagonal with all != 1
#' 
#' @param M A matrix (often `dsCMatrix`) or `NULL`.
#' @param eps Numeric tolerance
#' @return TRUE if \code{M} is a (Matrix-based) diagonal with all or partial diag
#' @keywords internal
is_identity_or_diag <- function(M, eps=1e-15) {
  (inherits(M,"Matrix") && Matrix::isDiagonal(M))
}

#' Partial eigen decomposition up to rank k
#'
#' @param M symmetric PSD matrix
#' @param k maximum rank
#' @param which "LA" for largest algebraic
#' @param eps small numeric for safety
#' @param tol tolerance for PRIMME
#' @return list(Q=..., lam=...) with length(lam)=k_eff
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
partial_eig_once <- function(M, k=50, which="LA", eps=1e-15, tol=1e-6) {
  # clamp k
  k_eff <- min(k, nrow(M), ncol(M))
  k_eff <- max(k_eff, 1)

  # For small matrices, base eigen() is more accurate than the
  # iterative solver used by PRIMME.  This helps avoid numeric
  # discrepancies in unit tests comparing to direct SVD.
  if (nrow(M) <= 50) {
    es <- eigen(as.matrix(M), symmetric = TRUE)
    lam <- pmax(es$values[seq_len(k_eff)], 0)
    Q   <- es$vectors[, seq_len(k_eff), drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    es <- PRIMME::eigs_sym(M, NEig = k_eff, which = which, tol = tol)
    lam <- pmax(es$values, 0)
    Q   <- es$vectors
  }

  if (!is.matrix(Q) || nrow(Q) != nrow(M)) {
    stop("partial_eig_once: dimension mismatch or no eigenvectors returned.")
  }

  list(Q = Q, lam = lam)
}

#' Decide adaptive rank to capture var_threshold fraction of total variance
#'
#' @param M PSD matrix
#' @param var_threshold fraction (0..1) of variance to capture
#' @param max_k maximum rank
#' @inheritParams partial_eig_once
#' @return list(Q, lam) truncated to minimal r s.t cumsum(lam)/sum(lam)>=var_threshold
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
decide_adaptive_rank <- function(M, which="LA", eps=1e-15, tol=1e-6,
                                 var_threshold=0.99, max_k=200)
{
  out <- partial_eig_once(M, k=max_k, which=which, eps=eps, tol=tol)
  lam <- out$lam
  Q   <- out$Q
  cumsums <- cumsum(lam)
  total <- sum(lam)
  r <- which(cumsums >= var_threshold*total)[1]
  if(is.na(r)) {
    r <- length(lam)
  }
  list(
    Q   = Q[, 1:r, drop=FALSE],
    lam = lam[1:r]
  )
}

#' partial_eig_approx: unify user-specified rank or adaptive
#'
#' @param M PSD matrix
#' @param user_rank numeric>0 or NA/NULL/0 => adaptive
#' @param var_threshold fraction
#' @param max_k maximum rank
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
partial_eig_approx <- function(M, user_rank,
                               var_threshold=0.99, max_k=200,
                               which="LA", eps=1e-15, tol=1e-6)
{
  if(!is.null(user_rank) && !is.na(user_rank) && user_rank>0) {
    # direct partial
    out <- partial_eig_once(M, k=user_rank, which=which, eps=eps, tol=tol)
    list(Q=out$Q, lams=out$lam)
  } else {

#' Transfer data between domains using a cross_projector
#'
#' This method wraps [multivarious::transfer()] but allows the argument names
#' `source` and `target` instead of `from` and `to` for convenience.
#'
#' @inherit multivarious::transfer
#' @param object A cross_projector object.
#' @param new_data Matrix or data.frame to transfer.
#' @param source Source domain ("X" or "Y").
#' @param target Target domain ("X" or "Y").
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed through.
#' @export
transfer.cross_projector <- function(object, new_data,
                                     source = c("X", "Y"),
                                     target = c("X", "Y"), ...) {
  source <- match.arg(source)
  target <- match.arg(target)
  multivarious:::transfer.cross_projector(object, new_data,
                                          from = source, to = target, ...)
}

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (!use_dual)
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)   # n × q

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B)) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg, b = b, M = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE, tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       x0 = prev_u,
                       verbose = if (verbose) 1L else 0L)
      prev_u <<- res$x                            # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = tail(res$errors, 1))
      res$x
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, ...)

  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- eig$vectors                 # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun)                     # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual)                        # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B)) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg, b = b, M = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE, tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       x0 = prev_u,
                       verbose = if (verbose) 1L else 0L)
      prev_u <<- res$x                            # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = tail(res$errors, 1))
      res$x
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, ...)

  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- eig$vectors                 # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun)                     # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual)                        # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

1→## tests/testthat/test_genplscor.R
     2→
     3→library(testthat)
     4→library(Matrix)
     5→
     6→test_that("genplscor works with identity constraints (basic usage)", {
     7→  set.seed(123)
     8→  n <- 10
     9→  p <- 5
    10→  q <- 3
    11→  
    12→  # Generate random data X, Y
    13→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
    14→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
    15→  
    16→  # Typically, we center for PLS
    17→  Xc <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
    18→  Yc <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
    19→  
    20→  # Identity constraints => Mx=I_n, Ax=I_p, My=I_n, Ay=I_q
    21→  # We'll do no constraints => default is NULL => identity
    22→  fit_id <- genplsc(Xc, Yc, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
    23→  
    24→  expect_s3_class(fit_id, c("genplscorr","cross_projector","projector"))
    25→  # check dimension of loadings
    26→  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vx), c(p, 2))
    27→  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vy), c(q, 2))
    28→  
    29→  # project X -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
    30→  Zx <- project(fit_id, Xc, source="X")
    31→  expect_equal(dim(Zx), c(n, 2))
    32→  
    33→  # project Y -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
    34→  Zy <- project(fit_id, Yc, source="Y")
    35→  expect_equal(dim(Zy), c(n, 2))
    36→  
    37→  # transfer X->Y => dimension n x q
    38→  Yhat <- transfer(fit_id, Xc, source="X", target="Y")
    39→  expect_equal(dim(Yhat), c(n, q))
    40→})

   135→test_that("genplscor internal: correlation of loadings with direct SVD approach", {
   136→  skip_on_cran()
   137→  
   138→  set.seed(456)
   139→  n <- 8
   140→  p <- 4
   141→  q <- 3
   142→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
   143→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
   144→  
   145→  # let us define a quick "classical PLS correlation" approach:
   146→  # 1) center => Xc, Yc
   147→  # 2) crossprod => do SVD => top ncomp
   148→  Xc <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
   149→  Yc <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
   150→  # direct "pls correlation" => Mx=I,Ax=I, My=I,Ay=I
   151→  # => genplscor
   152→  fit_gcor <- genplsc(Xc, Yc, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
   153→  
   154→  # as a naive check, let's do crossprod(Xc, Yc) => SVD => top2
   155→  C_mat <- crossprod(Xc, Yc) # shape (p x q)
   156→  sres <- svd(C_mat)
   157→  # top 2
   158→  d_naive <- sres$d[1:2]
   159→  u_naive <- sres$u[,1:2, drop=FALSE]
   160→  v_naive <- sres$v[,1:2, drop=FALSE]
   161→  
   162→  # compare fit_gcor$vx => p x 2 vs u_naive in correlation sense
   163→  # because they can differ by sign or scale
   164→  # flatten & cor
   165→  vx_vec <- as.numeric(fit_gcor$vx)
   166→  u_vec  <- as.numeric(u_naive)
   167→  if(sd(vx_vec)<1e-15 || sd(u_vec)<1e-15) {
   168→    # check difference
   169→    dval <- sum(abs(vx_vec - u_vec))
   170→    expect_lt(dval, 1e-6)
   171→  } else {
   172→    cor_val <- cor(vx_vec, u_vec)
   173→    # we expect correlation > 0.9 or so
   174→    expect_gt(cor_val, 0.9)
   175→  }
   176→})
   177→
   178→
   179→test_that("genplscorr dual=FALSE vs dual=TRUE yield similar results on small data", {
   180→  set.seed(123)
   181→  n <- 8
   182→  p <- 5
   183→  q <- 4
   184→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
   185→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
   186→  
   187→  # (1) No constraints => identity
   188→  fit_no_dual <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp=3, dual=FALSE, verbose=FALSE)
   189→  fit_yes_dual<- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp=3, dual=TRUE,  verbose=FALSE)
   190→  
   191→  # We'll compare some basic items:
   192→  #  - singular values (these are d_full in each fit)
   193→  d_nodual <- fit_no_dual$d_full
   194→  d_dual   <- fit_yes_dual$d_full
   195→  
   196→  # Because there's an inherent "sign or orientation" ambiguity in SVD/PLS loadings,
   197→  # we can't expect exact equality. We'll check that they're not drastically different.
   198→  # We'll do a length check first:
   199→  expect_true(abs(length(d_nodual) - length(d_dual)) <= 1)
   200→  
   201→  # Then compare first 2 or 3 singular values if they exist:
   202→  K <- min(length(d_nodual), length(d_dual))
   203→  expect_true(K >= 2)
   204→  
   205→  ratio_sv <- d_dual[1:K] / d_nodual[1:K]
   206→  # ratio should be close to 1 if they're truly aligned
   207→  # We'll allow a tolerance:
   208→  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.7 & ratio_sv < 1.4)) 
   209→  
   210→  # Compare factor scores in embedded domain: Tx, Ty
   211→  # We'll do correlation check between the columns from each approach.
   212→  # Because sign flips can happen, we do an absolute correlation check.
   213→  # We'll define a function to compare columns via max corr:
   214→  col_cors <- function(mat1, mat2, tol=0.7) {
   215→    # For each col in mat1, pick best correlation in mat2
   216→    # Return min of best cor across columns
   217→    stopifnot(ncol(mat1) == ncol(mat2))
   218→    out <- numeric(ncol(mat1))
   219→    for(j in seq_len(ncol(mat1))) {
   220→      corr_vals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(mat2)), function(k) {
   221→        cor(mat1[, j], mat2[, k])
   222→      })
   223→      out[j] <- max(abs(corr_vals), na.rm=TRUE)
   224→    }
   225→    min(out)
   226→  }
   227→  
   228→  score_corX <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Tx, fit_yes_dual$Tx)
   229→  score_corY <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Ty, fit_yes_dual$Ty)
   230→  expect_true(score_corX > 0.7)  # somewhat aligned
   231→  expect_true(score_corY > 0.7)
   232→  
   233→  # Compare loadings in original space
   234→  loading_corX <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vx, fit_yes_dual$vx)
   235→  loading_corY <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vy, fit_yes_dual$vy)
   236→  expect_true(loading_corX > 0.7)
   237→  expect_true(loading_corY > 0.7)
   238→})
   239→
   240→test_that("genplscorr dual=FALSE vs dual=TRUE with constraints yield similar results", {
   241→  set.seed(456)
   242→  n <- 10
   243→  p <- 6
   244→  q <- 5
   245→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
   246→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
   247→  
   248→  # Let's define some basic PSD constraints, e.g. random cov for columns:
   249→  # We'll do it for Ax only, for demonstration
   250→  Ax_0 <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p * p, sd=0.2), p, p))
   251→  # reduce rank => set small values below threshold
   252→  Ax_0[Ax_0 < 0.15] <- 0
   253→  # ensure symmetry
   254→  Ax_0 <- 0.5 * (Ax_0 + t(Ax_0))
   255→  
   256→  # For Mx, My => identity, Ay => identity
   257→  # to keep test simpler:
   258→  Mx_0 <- diag(n)
   259→  My_0 <- diag(n)
   260→  Ay_0 <- diag(q)
   261→  
   262→  fit_no_dual <- genplsc(X, Y,
   263→                            Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   264→                            My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   265→                            ncomp=3, dual=FALSE, verbose=TRUE)
   266→  fit_yes_dual<- genplsc(X, Y,
   267→                            Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   268→                            My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   269→                            ncomp=3, dual=TRUE,  verbose=TRUE)
   270→  
   271→  # do the same checks: singular values, factor scores, loadings
   272→  d1 <- fit_no_dual$d_full
   273→  d2 <- fit_yes_dual$d_full
   274→  K  <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
   275→  ratio_sv <- d2[1:K] / pmax(d1[1:K], 1e-9)
   276→  # loosen tolerance as constraints can shift results more
   277→  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.5 & ratio_sv < 2.0))
   278→  
   279→  # factor scores correlation
   280→  col_cors <- function(mat1, mat2, tol=0.5) {
   281→    stopifnot(ncol(mat1) == ncol(mat2))
   282→    out <- numeric(ncol(mat1))
   283→    for(j in seq_len(ncol(mat1))) {
   284→      corr_vals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(mat2)), function(k) {
   285→        cor(mat1[, j], mat2[, k])
   286→      })
   287→      out[j] <- max(abs(corr_vals), na.rm=TRUE)
   288→    }
   289→    min(out)
   290→  }
   291→  
   292→  scX_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Tx, fit_yes_dual$Tx)
   293→  scY_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Ty, fit_yes_dual$Ty)
   294→  expect_true(scX_cor > 0.5)
   295→  expect_true(scY_cor > 0.5)
   296→  
   297→  ldX_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vx, fit_yes_dual$vx)
   298→  ldY_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vy, fit_yes_dual$vy)
   299→  expect_true(ldX_cor > 0.5)
   300→  expect_true(ldY_cor > 0.5)
   301→})
   302→
   303→test_that("genplscorr handles ncomp > rank gracefully", {
   304→  set.seed(999)
   305→  n <- 8
   306→  p <- 5
   307→  q <- 4
   308→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
   309→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
   310→  
   311→  # Basic constraints (identity)
   312→  # We'll choose ncomp= larger than min(n,p,q)
   313→  # min(n,p,q) = 4, so let's pick ncomp=6
   314→  ncomp_too_big <- 6
   315→  
   316→  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
   317→    X, Y,
   318→    ncomp = ncomp_too_big,
   319→    dual = FALSE,
   320→    verbose = FALSE
   321→  )
   322→  fit_dual <- genplsc(
   323→    X, Y,
   324→    ncomp = ncomp_too_big,
   325→    dual = TRUE,
   326→    verbose = FALSE
   327→  )
   328→  
   329→  # Because ncomp > rank, each method might produce fewer actual components 
   330→  # or produce degenerate columns in factor scores.
   331→  # We'll check that the code didn't crash and that the final number of 
   332→  # components is <= the requested ncomp.
   333→  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= ncomp_too_big)
   334→  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= ncomp_too_big)
   335→  
   336→  # Also check that the singular values are not huge or NA
   337→  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_nodual$d_full)))
   338→  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_dual$d_full)))
   339→  
   340→  # Quick check that at least something non-degenerate emerged
   341→  # We'll ensure they produce at least 1 component if the data isn't rank-0
   342→  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp >= 1)
   343→  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp >= 1)
   344→  
   345→})
   346→
   347→test_that("genplscorr dual=TRUE works with random sparse PSD constraints for X and Y", {
   348→  set.seed(1001)
   349→  n <- 12
   350→  p <- 10
   351→  q <- 8
   352→  
   353→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd=0.5), n, p)
   354→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, sd=0.5), n, q)
   355→  
   356→  # Build random sparse PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My.
   357→  # We'll do something like:
   358→  # Ax0: a random p x p, then threshold to sparse; symmetrize => PSD approx
   359→  # Then refine by multiplying with t(...) if needed.
   360→  # A real PSD approach might do crossprod( ) then zero out small entries.
   361→  
   362→  random_psd_sparse <- function(dimsize=10, threshold=0.3) {
   363→    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=0.2), dimsize, dimsize)
   364→    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
   365→    # symmetrize
   366→    mat <- 0.5 * (mat + t(mat))
   367→    # crossprod to ensure PSD
   368→    # for a small example, crossprod is enough
   369→    psd <- crossprod(mat)
   370→    # keep it sparse
   371→    psd <- Matrix::Matrix(psd, sparse=TRUE)
   372→    psd
   373→  }
   374→  
   375→  Ax_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=p, threshold=0.25)
   376→  Ay_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=q, threshold=0.2)
   377→  Mx_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=n, threshold=0.1)
   378→  My_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=n, threshold=0.1)
   379→  
   380→  # Just do a small number of components
   381→  K <- 3
   382→  
   383→  # We'll try dual=TRUE 
   384→  fit_sp_dual <- genplsc(
   385→    X, Y,
   386→    Mx=Mx_sp, Ax=Ax_sp,
   387→    My=My_sp, Ay=Ay_sp,
   388→    ncomp=K,
   389→    dual=TRUE,
   390→    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL, # => adaptive expansions
   391→    var_threshold=0.9,  # let's capture 90% for speed
   392→    max_k=5,           # small partial expansions
   393→    verbose=FALSE
   394→  )
   395→  
   396→  # If all is well, we won't crash or produce obviously degenerate results.
   397→  expect_true(fit_sp_dual$ncomp <= K)
   398→  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_sp_dual$d_full)))
   399→  expect_true(length(fit_sp_dual$d_full) >= 1)
   400→  
   401→  # Check that the embedding factor scores, etc., are of correct dimension
   402→  # Factor scores Tx => n x (some #), loadings vx => p x (some #)
   403→  expect_equal(dim(fit_sp_dual$Tx), c(n, fit_sp_dual$ncomp))
   404→  expect_equal(dim(fit_sp_dual$vx), c(p, fit_sp_dual$ncomp))
   405→  
   406→  # Not easily verifiable if the decomposition is "right," but 
   407→  # at least we confirm the code handles random sparse PSD constraints and 
   408→  # the partial expansions didn't blow up. 
   409→  # In real usage, you'd do deeper validations or compare with a known solution on smaller data.
   410→  
   411→  # Additional sanity checks:
   412→  # The d_full typically won't be NA or infinite
   413→  expect_true(all(is.finite(fit_sp_dual$d_full)))
   414→  # Some measure that the loadings aren't all zero
   415→  loadnormsX <- apply(fit_sp_dual$vx, 2, function(cc) sqrt(sum(cc^2)))
   416→  expect_true(all(loadnormsX > 1e-8))
   417→})
   418→
   419→
   420→test_that("genplscorr: dual=TRUE vs dual=FALSE produce closely equivalent results on moderate data", {
   421→  set.seed(222)
   422→  n <- 12
   423→  p <- 7
   424→  q <- 6
   425→  
   426→  # Generate moderately sized data
   427→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, mean=0, sd=0.6), n, p)
   428→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, mean=1, sd=0.8), n, q)
   429→  
   430→  # Let's define modest PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My:
   431→  # We'll do a crossprod approach for each, plus a small threshold to ensure some sparsity.
   432→  
   433→  make_psd <- function(dimsize, threshold=0.1, sdev=0.2) {
   434→    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=sdev), dimsize, dimsize)
   435→    # zero out small absolute entries
   436→    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
   437→    # symmetrize
   438→    mat <- 0.5*(mat + t(mat))
   439→    # crossprod => ensure PSD
   440→    out <- crossprod(mat)
   441→    # small dimension => we can keep as is for demonstration
   442→    # optionally convert to sparse
   443→    # out <- Matrix::Matrix(out, sparse=TRUE)
   444→    out
   445→  }
   446→  
   447→  # Let n=12 => Mx, My are 12x12. p=7 => Ax is 7x7. q=6 => Ay is 6x6
   448→  Mx_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
   449→  My_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
   450→  Ax_0 <- make_psd(p, threshold=0.07)
   451→  Ay_0 <- make_psd(q, threshold=0.07)
   452→  
   453→  # We choose a small-ish rank expansions but let them be adaptive:
   454→  # We'll do an example ncomp=4
   455→  K <- 4
   456→  
   457→  # dual=FALSE
   458→  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
   459→    X, Y,
   460→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   461→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   462→    ncomp = K,
   463→    dual = FALSE,
   464→    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
   465→    var_threshold=0.95,
   466→    max_k=10,  # partial expansions up to 10 
   467→    verbose=FALSE
   468→  )
   469→  
   470→  # dual=TRUE
   471→  fit_dual <- genplsc(
   472→    X, Y,
   473→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   474→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   475→    ncomp = K,
   476→    dual = TRUE,
   477→    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
   478→    var_threshold=0.95,
   479→    max_k=10,
   480→    verbose=FALSE
   481→  )
   482→  
   483→  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
   484→  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= K)
   485→  
   486→  # 1) Compare singular values
   487→  d1 <- fit_nodual$d_full
   488→  d2 <- fit_dual$d_full
   489→  expect_true(length(d1) > 0 && length(d2) > 0)
   490→  
   491→  # We'll compare up to min of lengths
   492→  L <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
   493→  ratio_sv <- d2[1:L] / pmax(d1[1:L], 1e-12)
   494→  # Because constraints can shift them somewhat, we just expect no wild mismatch:
   495→  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.5 & ratio_sv < 2.0))
   496→  
   497→  # 2) Compare factor scores or loadings
   498→  # We'll define a helper again for correlation across columns:
   499→  col_cors <- function(matA, matB) {
   500→    stopifnot(ncol(matA) > 0, ncol(matB) > 0)
   501→    K <- min(ncol(matA), ncol(matB))
   502→    out <- numeric(K)
   503→    for (j in seq_len(K)) {
   504→      # best correlation with any col in matB
   505→      cvals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(matB)), function(k) {
   506→        cor(matA[, j], matB[, k])
   507→      })
   508→      out[j] <- max(abs(cvals))
   509→    }
   510→    min(out)
   511→  }
   512→  
   513→  # Factor scores Tx
   514→  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Tx) && !is.null(fit_dual$Tx)) {
   515→    scX_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Tx, fit_dual$Tx)
   516→    expect_true(scX_cor > 0.5)
   517→  }
   518→  
   519→  # Factor scores Ty
   520→  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Ty) && !is.null(fit_dual$Ty)) {
   521→    scY_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Ty, fit_dual$Ty)
   522→    expect_true(scY_cor > 0.5)
   523→  }
   524→  
   525→  # Loadings vx, vy in original domain
   526→  ldX_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vx, fit_dual$vx)
   527→  ldY_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vy, fit_dual$vy)
   528→  # We'll want these to be decently correlated
   529→  expect_true(ldX_cor > 0.5)
   530→  expect_true(ldY_cor > 0.5)
   531→})
   532→
   533→
   534→
   535→test_that("genplscorr2 dual=TRUE vs dual=FALSE produce similar results for moderate data and PSD constraints", {
   536→  set.seed(227)
   537→  n <- 34
   538→  p <- 16
   539→  q <- 15
   540→  
   541→  # Generate moderately sized data
   542→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, mean=0, sd=0.6), n, p)
   543→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, mean=1, sd=0.8), n, q)
   544→  
   545→  # Let's define modest PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My:
   546→  # We'll do a crossprod approach for each, plus a small threshold to ensure some sparsity.
   547→  make_psd <- function(dimsize, threshold=0.1, sdev=0.2) {
   548→    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=sdev), dimsize, dimsize)
   549→    # zero out small entries
   550→    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
   551→    # symmetrize
   552→    mat <- 0.5 * (mat + t(mat))
   553→    # ensure PSD by crossprod
   554→    out <- crossprod(mat)
   555→    out
   556→  }
   557→  
   558→  # Let n=12 => Mx, My are 12x12. p=7 => Ax=7x7, q=6 => Ay=6x6
   559→  Mx_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
   560→  My_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
   561→  Ax_0 <- make_psd(p, threshold=0.07)
   562→  Ay_0 <- make_psd(q, threshold=0.07)
   563→  
   564→  # We'll do an example with ncomp=4
   565→  K <- 4
   566→  
   567→  # 1) dual=FALSE
   568→  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
   569→    X, Y,
   570→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   571→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   572→    ncomp = K,
   573→    dual = FALSE,
   574→    rank_Mx=3L, rank_Ax=3, rank_My=3, rank_Ay=3,
   575→    var_threshold=1,
   576→    max_k=100,  # partial expansions up to 10 
   577→    verbose=FALSE
   578→  )
   579→  
   580→  # 2) dual=TRUE
   581→  fit_dual <- genplsc(
   582→    X, Y,
   583→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   584→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   585→    ncomp = K,
   586→    dual = TRUE,
   587→    rank_Mx=3, rank_Ax=3, rank_My=3, rank_Ay=3,
   588→    var_threshold=1,
   589→    max_k=100,
   590→    verbose=FALSE
   591→  )
   592→  
   593→  # Basic checks
   594→  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
   595→  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= K)
   596→  expect_s3_class(fit_nodual, "cross_projector")
   597→  expect_s3_class(fit_dual, "cross_projector")
   598→  
   599→  # Compare singular values (d_full)
   600→  d1 <- fit_nodual$d_full
   601→  d2 <- fit_dual$d_full
   602→  expect_true(length(d1) > 0 && length(d2) > 0)
   603→  L <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
   604→  ratio_sv <- d2[1:L] / pmax(1e-12, d1[1:L])
   605→  # We just want them within a modest ratio
   606→  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.3 & ratio_sv < 3.0))
   607→  
   608→  # A helper to measure cross-column correlation
   609→  # allowing sign flips or different order
   610→  col_cors <- function(matA, matB) {
   611→    # returns min of max correlation per column in matA
   612→    # ignoring sign or col permutations
   613→    stopifnot(ncol(matA) > 0, ncol(matB) > 0)
   614→    out <- numeric(ncol(matA))
   615→    for (j in seq_len(ncol(matA))) {
   616→      # correlate matA col j with *all* columns in matB, take max of absolute
   617→      cors <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(matB)), function(k) {
   618→        cc <- cor(matA[, j], matB[, k])
   619→        if (is.na(cc)) 0 else abs(cc)
   620→      })
   621→      out[j] <- max(cors)
   622→    }
   623→    mean(out)
   624→  }
   625→  
   626→  # Compare factor scores if available
   627→  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Tx) && !is.null(fit_dual$Tx)) {
   628→    score_cor_x <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Tx, fit_dual$Tx)
   629→    expect_true(score_cor_x > 0.5) 
   630→  }
   631→  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Ty) && !is.null(fit_dual$Ty)) {
   632→    score_cor_y <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Ty, fit_dual$Ty)
   633→    expect_true(score_cor_y > 0.5) 
   634→  }
   635→  
   636→  # Compare loadings in original domain
   637→  expect_equal(nrow(fit_nodual$vx), p)
   638→  expect_equal(nrow(fit_dual$vx), p)
   639→  if (ncol(fit_nodual$vx) > 0 && ncol(fit_dual$vx) > 0) {
   640→    cor_vx <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vx, fit_dual$vx)
   641→    expect_true(cor_vx > 0.5)
   642→  }
   643→  
   644→  expect_equal(nrow(fit_nodual$vy), q)
   645→  expect_equal(nrow(fit_dual$vy), q)
   646→  if (ncol(fit_nodual$vy) > 0 && ncol(fit_dual$vy) > 0) {
   647→    cor_vy <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vy, fit_dual$vy)
   648→    expect_true(cor_vy > 0.5)
   649→  }
   650→})
   651→

   653→test_that("genplscorr2 handles low-rank data + constraints consistently", {
   654→  set.seed(999)  # for reproducibility
   655→  
   656→  #### 1) Generate low-rank data (X, Y) + noise ####
   657→  n <- 30
   658→  p <- 20
   659→  q <- 15
   660→  
   661→  # We assume rank ~ 4 for the "true" latent structure.
   662→  rank_true <- 4
   663→  
   664→  # (a) Build a latent factor matrix for X: shape n x rank_true
   665→  #     plus a separate factor for Y.
   666→  #     Then create X, Y by (FactorX * LoadingsX) + noise, etc.
   667→  
   668→  # "true" latent factor scores for X, dimension n x rank_true
   669→  Fx <- matrix(rnorm(n * rank_true, sd=1.0), n, rank_true)
   670→  # loadings for X in p-d space
   671→  Lx <- matrix(rnorm(p * rank_true, sd=1.0), p, rank_true)
   672→  
   673→  # "true" latent factor for Y, dimension n x rank_true
   674→  # optionally correlated with X's factor: e.g. let's take Fx plus small random
   675→  # or you can do a separate factor. We'll do correlated for demonstration:
   676→  Fy <- Fx + matrix(rnorm(n * rank_true, sd=0.2), n, rank_true)
   677→  Ly <- matrix(rnorm(q * rank_true, sd=1.0), q, rank_true)
   678→  
   679→  # Construct X, Y (rank ~ rank_true) + random noise
   680→  Xbase <- Fx %*% t(Lx)   # shape n x p
   681→  Ybase <- Fy %*% t(Ly)   # shape n x q
   682→  
   683→  noiseX <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd=0.5), n, p)
   684→  noiseY <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, sd=0.5), n, q)
   685→  
   686→  X <- Xbase + noiseX
   687→  Y <- Ybase + noiseY
   688→  
   689→  # Optionally center columns (typical for PLS):
   690→  # We'll rely on genplscorr2's own 'preproc_x=pass()' or we can do:
   691→  # X <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
   692→  # Y <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
   693→  
   694→  #### 2) Build low-rank constraints for Mx, Ax, My, Ay ####
   695→  # Let's produce, e.g., rank 5 expansions for each, plus small noise => shape n x n or p x p, etc.
   696→  # We'll do something like U diag(...) V^T, with rank=5. Then we can add a dash of noise.
   697→  
   698→  make_lowrank_psd <- function(dimsize, r=5, noise_sd=0.01) {
   699→    # generate a random U, V => shape dimsize x r
   700→    U <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*r), dimsize, r)
   701→    V <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*r), dimsize, r)
   702→    # build rank-r mat => U diag(...) V^T
   703→    # simplest: let diag(...) be positives (random?)
   704→    diagvals <- runif(r, min=0.5, max=2.0)
   705→    lowrank <- U %*% (diag(diagvals)) %*% t(V)  # shape dimsize x dimsize
   706→    # symmetrize
   707→    lowrank <- 0.5 * (lowrank + t(lowrank))
   708→    # add small noise
   709→    noise <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*dimsize, sd=noise_sd), dimsize, dimsize)
   710→    out <- lowrank + noise
   711→    # make sure PSD => do a quick shift or crossprod approach
   712→    # to ensure non-negative eigenvalues
   713→    # simplest: crossprod => or we can do an eigen-cleaning
   714→    out <- 0.5*(out + t(out))  # sym
   715→    # We'll do a small "eigen-truncation" approach:
   716→    eout <- eigen(out, symmetric=TRUE)
   717→    lampos <- pmax(eout$values, 1e-5)
   718→    out_psd <- eout$vectors %*% diag(lampos) %*% t(eout$vectors)
   719→    out_psd
   720→  }
   721→  
   722→  # Mx, My => n x n
   723→  # Ax => p x p,   Ay => q x q
   724→  Mx_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(n, r=5)
   725→  My_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(n, r=5)
   726→  Ax_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(p, r=5)
   727→  Ay_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(q, r=5)
   728→  
   729→  #### 3) Run genplscorr2 => dual=FALSE vs. dual=TRUE ####
   730→  # We'll request ncomp=6, see how many we actually get
   731→  K <- 6
   732→  
   733→  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
   734→    X, Y,
   735→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   736→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   737→    ncomp=K,
   738→    dual=FALSE,
   739→    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
   740→    var_threshold=.9,
   741→    max_k=100,
   742→    verbose=FALSE
   743→  )
   744→  
   745→  fit_dual <- genplsc(
   746→    X, Y,
   747→    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
   748→    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
   749→    ncomp=K,
   750→    dual=TRUE,
   751→    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
   752→    var_threshold=.9,
   753→    max_k=100,
   754→    verbose=FALSE
   755→  )
   756→  
   757→  #### 4) Basic checks ####
   758→  # Check that each fit has # of components <= K
   759→  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
   760→  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp   <= K)
   761→  
   762→  # Confirm we have loadings of appropriate dimension
   763→  expect_equal(dim(fit_nodual$vx)[1], p)  # p x ncomp
   764→  expect_equal(dim(fit_dual$vx)[1],   p)
   765→  
   766→  # 5) Compare first few directions 
   767→  # They may differ due to partial expansions, sign flips, etc.
   768→  # We can do a rough check on correlation among the first 2-3 columns of vx or vy
   769→  # e.g., the absolute correlation => should be fairly large if they're capturing similar directions.
   770→  # but won't be identical => we just check if there's some overlap. 
   771→  library(stats)
   772→  ncomp_compare <- min(fit_nodual$ncomp, fit_dual$ncomp, 3)
   773→  cor_thresh <- 0.5  # somewhat arbitrary
   774→  for(j in seq_len(ncomp_compare)) {
   775→    # correlation of loadings on X
   776→    cval_x <- cor(fit_nodual$vx[,j], fit_dual$vx[,j])
   777→    # correlation of loadings on Y
   778→    cval_y <- cor(fit_nodual$vy[,j], fit_dual$vy[,j])
   779→    # Just expect they're not trivial => we won't do expect_* but we can check 
   780→    # they aren't negligible
   781→    msg_x <- paste("component", j, "X-loadings correlation is non-trivial")
   782→    msg_y <- paste("component", j, "Y-loadings correlation is non-trivial")
   783→    testthat::expect_true(abs(cval_x) > 0.2, info = msg_x)
   784→    testthat::expect_true(abs(cval_y) > 0.2, info = msg_y)
   785→  }
   786→  
   787→  # You might also check factor scores or singular values, etc.
   788→  # The point is: in a low-rank scenario, we'd see that both approaches 
   789→  # pick up somewhat similar directions, though not identical.
   790→})
   791→
   792→test_that("genplscorr dual=TRUE row likeness returns correct dimensions", {
   793→  set.seed(42)
   794→  n <- 7; p <- 5; q <- 4
   795→  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
   796→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
   797→
   798→  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, dual = TRUE,
   799→                 force_row_likeness = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
   800→
   801→  expect_s3_class(fit, c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"))
   802→  expect_equal(dim(fit$Tx), c(n, fit$ncomp))
   803→  expect_equal(dim(fit$Ty), c(n, fit$ncomp))
   804→})
   805→

## tests/testthat/test_genplscor.R

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

test_that("genplscor works with identity constraints (basic usage)", {
  set.seed(123)
  n <- 10
  p <- 5
  q <- 3
  
  # Generate random data X, Y
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # Typically, we center for PLS
  Xc <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  Yc <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  
  # Identity constraints => Mx=I_n, Ax=I_p, My=I_n, Ay=I_q
  # We'll do no constraints => default is NULL => identity
  fit_id <- genplsc(Xc, Yc, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
  
  expect_s3_class(fit_id, c("genplscorr","cross_projector","projector"))
  # check dimension of loadings
  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vx), c(p, 2))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_id$vy), c(q, 2))
  
  # project X -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
  Zx <- project(fit_id, Xc, source="X")
  expect_equal(dim(Zx), c(n, 2))
  
  # project Y -> latent => dimension n x ncomp
  Zy <- project(fit_id, Yc, source="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Zy), c(n, 2))
  
  # transfer X->Y => dimension n x q
  Yhat <- transfer(fit_id, Xc, source="X", target="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Yhat), c(n, q))
})


test_that("genplscor works with diagonal col constraints for X, Y", {
  set.seed(999)
  n <- 12
  p <- 4
  q <- 3
  
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  # Let's define col constraints: Ax=diag(1, p) => identity? Let's do a random diag
  Ax_vals <- c(1.0, 2.5, 0.5, 4.0) # just random positive
  Ax <- Diagonal(x=Ax_vals)
  # Similarly for Y
  Ay_vals <- c(2.0, 1.5, 3.2)
  Ay <- Diagonal(x=Ay_vals)
  
  # row constraints => identity
  # pass them as NULL => identity
  fit_diag <- genplsc(X, Y, Ax=Ax, Ay=Ay, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
  
  expect_s3_class(fit_diag, c("genplscorr","cross_projector","projector"))
  # dimension checks
  expect_equal(dim(fit_diag$vx), c(p, 2))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_diag$vy), c(q, 2))
  
  # project
  Zx <- project(fit_diag, X, source="X")
  expect_equal(dim(Zx), c(n, 2))
  
  Zy <- project(fit_diag, Y, source="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Zy), c(n, 2))
})

test_that("genplscor with random PSD row adjacency constraints for X, Y", {
  skip_on_cran()  # skip for CRAN if bigger
  
  set.seed(234)
  n <- 15
  p <- 6
  q <- 5
  
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  # row constraints => random adjacency => crossprod => PSD
  Mx_tmp <- rsparsematrix(n, n, density=0.2)
  Mx_tmp <- crossprod(Mx_tmp)
  Mx_tmp <- forceSymmetric(Mx_tmp)
  Mx <- as(Mx_tmp, "dsCMatrix")
  
  My_tmp <- rsparsematrix(n, n, density=0.2)
  My_tmp <- crossprod(My_tmp)
  My_tmp <- forceSymmetric(My_tmp)
  My <- as(My_tmp, "dsCMatrix")
  
  # col constraints => identity => pass as NULL
  fit_adj <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, My=My, ncomp=3, verbose=FALSE)
  
  expect_s3_class(fit_adj, c("genplscorr","cross_projector","projector"))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_adj$vx), c(p, 3))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_adj$vy), c(q, 3))
  
  # project => see if we can do it
  Zx <- project(fit_adj, X, source="X")
  Zy <- project(fit_adj, Y, source="Y")
  expect_equal(dim(Zx), c(n, 3))
  expect_equal(dim(Zy), c(n, 3))
})
