test_that("genplscor internal: correlation of loadings with direct SVD approach", {
  skip_on_cran()
  
  set.seed(456)
  n <- 8
  p <- 4
  q <- 3
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  # let us define a quick "classical PLS correlation" approach:
  # 1) center => Xc, Yc
  # 2) crossprod => do SVD => top ncomp
  Xc <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  Yc <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  # direct "pls correlation" => Mx=I,Ax=I, My=I,Ay=I
  # => genplscor
  fit_gcor <- genplsc(Xc, Yc, ncomp=2, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # as a naive check, let's do crossprod(Xc, Yc) => SVD => top2
  C_mat <- crossprod(Xc, Yc) # shape (p x q)
  sres <- svd(C_mat)
  # top 2
  d_naive <- sres$d[1:2]
  u_naive <- sres$u[,1:2, drop=FALSE]
  v_naive <- sres$v[,1:2, drop=FALSE]
  
  # compare fit_gcor$vx => p x 2 vs u_naive in correlation sense
  # because they can differ by sign or scale
  # flatten & cor
  vx_vec <- as.numeric(fit_gcor$vx)
  u_vec  <- as.numeric(u_naive)
  if(sd(vx_vec)<1e-15 || sd(u_vec)<1e-15) {
    # check difference
    dval <- sum(abs(vx_vec - u_vec))
    expect_lt(dval, 1e-6)
  } else {
    cor_val <- cor(vx_vec, u_vec)
    # we expect correlation > 0.9 or so
    expect_gt(cor_val, 0.9)
  }
})


test_that("genplscorr dual=FALSE vs dual=TRUE yield similar results on small data", {
  set.seed(123)
  n <- 8
  p <- 5
  q <- 4
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # (1) No constraints => identity
  fit_no_dual <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp=3, dual=FALSE, verbose=FALSE)
  fit_yes_dual<- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp=3, dual=TRUE,  verbose=FALSE)
  
  # We'll compare some basic items:
  #  - singular values (these are d_full in each fit)
  d_nodual <- fit_no_dual$d_full
  d_dual   <- fit_yes_dual$d_full
  
  # Because there's an inherent "sign or orientation" ambiguity in SVD/PLS loadings,
  # we can't expect exact equality. We'll check that they're not drastically different.
  # We'll do a length check first:
  expect_true(abs(length(d_nodual) - length(d_dual)) <= 1)
  
  # Then compare first 2 or 3 singular values if they exist:
  K <- min(length(d_nodual), length(d_dual))
  expect_true(K >= 2)
  
  ratio_sv <- d_dual[1:K] / d_nodual[1:K]
  # ratio should be close to 1 if they're truly aligned
  # We'll allow a tolerance:
  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.7 & ratio_sv < 1.4)) 
  
  # Compare factor scores in embedded domain: Tx, Ty
  # We'll do correlation check between the columns from each approach.
  # Because sign flips can happen, we do an absolute correlation check.
  # We'll define a function to compare columns via max corr:
  col_cors <- function(mat1, mat2, tol=0.7) {
    # For each col in mat1, pick best correlation in mat2
    # Return min of best cor across columns
    stopifnot(ncol(mat1) == ncol(mat2))
    out <- numeric(ncol(mat1))
    for(j in seq_len(ncol(mat1))) {
      corr_vals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(mat2)), function(k) {
        cor(mat1[, j], mat2[, k])
      })
      out[j] <- max(abs(corr_vals), na.rm=TRUE)
    }
    min(out)
  }
  
  score_corX <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Tx, fit_yes_dual$Tx)
  score_corY <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Ty, fit_yes_dual$Ty)
  expect_true(score_corX > 0.7)  # somewhat aligned
  expect_true(score_corY > 0.7)
  
  # Compare loadings in original space
  loading_corX <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vx, fit_yes_dual$vx)
  loading_corY <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vy, fit_yes_dual$vy)
  expect_true(loading_corX > 0.7)
  expect_true(loading_corY > 0.7)
})

test_that("genplscorr dual=FALSE vs dual=TRUE with constraints yield similar results", {
  set.seed(456)
  n <- 10
  p <- 6
  q <- 5
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # Let's define some basic PSD constraints, e.g. random cov for columns:
  # We'll do it for Ax only, for demonstration
  Ax_0 <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p * p, sd=0.2), p, p))
  # reduce rank => set small values below threshold
  Ax_0[Ax_0 < 0.15] <- 0
  # ensure symmetry
  Ax_0 <- 0.5 * (Ax_0 + t(Ax_0))
  
  # For Mx, My => identity, Ay => identity
  # to keep test simpler:
  Mx_0 <- diag(n)
  My_0 <- diag(n)
  Ay_0 <- diag(q)
  
  fit_no_dual <- genplsc(X, Y,
                            Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
                            My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
                            ncomp=3, dual=FALSE, verbose=TRUE)
  fit_yes_dual<- genplsc(X, Y,
                            Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
                            My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
                            ncomp=3, dual=TRUE,  verbose=TRUE)
  
  # do the same checks: singular values, factor scores, loadings
  d1 <- fit_no_dual$d_full
  d2 <- fit_yes_dual$d_full
  K  <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
  ratio_sv <- d2[1:K] / pmax(d1[1:K], 1e-9)
  # loosen tolerance as constraints can shift results more
  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.5 & ratio_sv < 2.0))
  
  # factor scores correlation
  col_cors <- function(mat1, mat2, tol=0.5) {
    stopifnot(ncol(mat1) == ncol(mat2))
    out <- numeric(ncol(mat1))
    for(j in seq_len(ncol(mat1))) {
      corr_vals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(mat2)), function(k) {
        cor(mat1[, j], mat2[, k])
      })
      out[j] <- max(abs(corr_vals), na.rm=TRUE)
    }
    min(out)
  }
  
  scX_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Tx, fit_yes_dual$Tx)
  scY_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$Ty, fit_yes_dual$Ty)
  expect_true(scX_cor > 0.5)
  expect_true(scY_cor > 0.5)
  
  ldX_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vx, fit_yes_dual$vx)
  ldY_cor <- col_cors(fit_no_dual$vy, fit_yes_dual$vy)
  expect_true(ldX_cor > 0.5)
  expect_true(ldY_cor > 0.5)
})

test_that("genplscorr handles ncomp > rank gracefully", {
  set.seed(999)
  n <- 8
  p <- 5
  q <- 4
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # Basic constraints (identity)
  # We'll choose ncomp= larger than min(n,p,q)
  # min(n,p,q) = 4, so let's pick ncomp=6
  ncomp_too_big <- 6
  
  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    ncomp = ncomp_too_big,
    dual = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  fit_dual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    ncomp = ncomp_too_big,
    dual = TRUE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  
  # Because ncomp > rank, each method might produce fewer actual components 
  # or produce degenerate columns in factor scores.
  # We'll check that the code didn't crash and that the final number of 
  # components is <= the requested ncomp.
  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= ncomp_too_big)
  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= ncomp_too_big)
  
  # Also check that the singular values are not huge or NA
  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_nodual$d_full)))
  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_dual$d_full)))
  
  # Quick check that at least something non-degenerate emerged
  # We'll ensure they produce at least 1 component if the data isn't rank-0
  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp >= 1)
  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp >= 1)
  
})

test_that("genplscorr dual=TRUE works with random sparse PSD constraints for X and Y", {
  set.seed(1001)
  n <- 12
  p <- 10
  q <- 8
  
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd=0.5), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, sd=0.5), n, q)
  
  # Build random sparse PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My.
  # We'll do something like:
  # Ax0: a random p x p, then threshold to sparse; symmetrize => PSD approx
  # Then refine by multiplying with t(...) if needed.
  # A real PSD approach might do crossprod( ) then zero out small entries.
  
  random_psd_sparse <- function(dimsize=10, threshold=0.3) {
    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=0.2), dimsize, dimsize)
    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
    # symmetrize
    mat <- 0.5 * (mat + t(mat))
    # crossprod to ensure PSD
    # for a small example, crossprod is enough
    psd <- crossprod(mat)
    # keep it sparse
    psd <- Matrix::Matrix(psd, sparse=TRUE)
    psd
  }
  
  Ax_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=p, threshold=0.25)
  Ay_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=q, threshold=0.2)
  Mx_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=n, threshold=0.1)
  My_sp <- random_psd_sparse(dimsize=n, threshold=0.1)
  
  # Just do a small number of components
  K <- 3
  
  # We'll try dual=TRUE 
  fit_sp_dual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_sp, Ax=Ax_sp,
    My=My_sp, Ay=Ay_sp,
    ncomp=K,
    dual=TRUE,
    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL, # => adaptive expansions
    var_threshold=0.9,  # let's capture 90% for speed
    max_k=5,           # small partial expansions
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  # If all is well, we won't crash or produce obviously degenerate results.
  expect_true(fit_sp_dual$ncomp <= K)
  expect_true(all(!is.na(fit_sp_dual$d_full)))
  expect_true(length(fit_sp_dual$d_full) >= 1)
  
  # Check that the embedding factor scores, etc., are of correct dimension
  # Factor scores Tx => n x (some #), loadings vx => p x (some #)
  expect_equal(dim(fit_sp_dual$Tx), c(n, fit_sp_dual$ncomp))
  expect_equal(dim(fit_sp_dual$vx), c(p, fit_sp_dual$ncomp))
  
  # Not easily verifiable if the decomposition is "right," but 
  # at least we confirm the code handles random sparse PSD constraints and 
  # the partial expansions didn't blow up. 
  # In real usage, you'd do deeper validations or compare with a known solution on smaller data.
  
  # Additional sanity checks:
  # The d_full typically won't be NA or infinite
  expect_true(all(is.finite(fit_sp_dual$d_full)))
  # Some measure that the loadings aren't all zero
  loadnormsX <- apply(fit_sp_dual$vx, 2, function(cc) sqrt(sum(cc^2)))
  expect_true(all(loadnormsX > 1e-8))
})


test_that("genplscorr: dual=TRUE vs dual=FALSE produce closely equivalent results on moderate data", {
  set.seed(222)
  n <- 12
  p <- 7
  q <- 6
  
  # Generate moderately sized data
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, mean=0, sd=0.6), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, mean=1, sd=0.8), n, q)
  
  # Let's define modest PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My:
  # We'll do a crossprod approach for each, plus a small threshold to ensure some sparsity.
  
  make_psd <- function(dimsize, threshold=0.1, sdev=0.2) {
    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=sdev), dimsize, dimsize)
    # zero out small absolute entries
    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
    # symmetrize
    mat <- 0.5*(mat + t(mat))
    # crossprod => ensure PSD
    out <- crossprod(mat)
    # small dimension => we can keep as is for demonstration
    # optionally convert to sparse
    # out <- Matrix::Matrix(out, sparse=TRUE)
    out
  }
  
  # Let n=12 => Mx, My are 12x12. p=7 => Ax is 7x7. q=6 => Ay is 6x6
  Mx_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
  My_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
  Ax_0 <- make_psd(p, threshold=0.07)
  Ay_0 <- make_psd(q, threshold=0.07)
  
  # We choose a small-ish rank expansions but let them be adaptive:
  # We'll do an example ncomp=4
  K <- 4
  
  # dual=FALSE
  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp = K,
    dual = FALSE,
    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
    var_threshold=0.95,
    max_k=10,  # partial expansions up to 10 
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  # dual=TRUE
  fit_dual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp = K,
    dual = TRUE,
    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
    var_threshold=0.95,
    max_k=10,
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= K)
  
  # 1) Compare singular values
  d1 <- fit_nodual$d_full
  d2 <- fit_dual$d_full
  expect_true(length(d1) > 0 && length(d2) > 0)
  
  # We'll compare up to min of lengths
  L <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
  ratio_sv <- d2[1:L] / pmax(d1[1:L], 1e-12)
  # Because constraints can shift them somewhat, we just expect no wild mismatch:
  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.5 & ratio_sv < 2.0))
  
  # 2) Compare factor scores or loadings
  # We'll define a helper again for correlation across columns:
  col_cors <- function(matA, matB) {
    stopifnot(ncol(matA) > 0, ncol(matB) > 0)
    K <- min(ncol(matA), ncol(matB))
    out <- numeric(K)
    for (j in seq_len(K)) {
      # best correlation with any col in matB
      cvals <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(matB)), function(k) {
        cor(matA[, j], matB[, k])
      })
      out[j] <- max(abs(cvals))
    }
    min(out)
  }
  
  # Factor scores Tx
  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Tx) && !is.null(fit_dual$Tx)) {
    scX_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Tx, fit_dual$Tx)
    expect_true(scX_cor > 0.5)
  }
  
  # Factor scores Ty
  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Ty) && !is.null(fit_dual$Ty)) {
    scY_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Ty, fit_dual$Ty)
    expect_true(scY_cor > 0.5)
  }
  
  # Loadings vx, vy in original domain
  ldX_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vx, fit_dual$vx)
  ldY_cor <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vy, fit_dual$vy)
  # We'll want these to be decently correlated
  expect_true(ldX_cor > 0.5)
  expect_true(ldY_cor > 0.5)
})



test_that("genplscorr2 dual=TRUE vs dual=FALSE produce similar results for moderate data and PSD constraints", {
  set.seed(227)
  n <- 34
  p <- 16
  q <- 15
  
  # Generate moderately sized data
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, mean=0, sd=0.6), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, mean=1, sd=0.8), n, q)
  
  # Let's define modest PSD constraints for Ax, Ay, Mx, My:
  # We'll do a crossprod approach for each, plus a small threshold to ensure some sparsity.
  make_psd <- function(dimsize, threshold=0.1, sdev=0.2) {
    mat <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize^2, sd=sdev), dimsize, dimsize)
    # zero out small entries
    mat[abs(mat) < threshold] <- 0
    # symmetrize
    mat <- 0.5 * (mat + t(mat))
    # ensure PSD by crossprod
    out <- crossprod(mat)
    out
  }
  
  # Let n=12 => Mx, My are 12x12. p=7 => Ax=7x7, q=6 => Ay=6x6
  Mx_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
  My_0 <- make_psd(n, threshold=0.08)
  Ax_0 <- make_psd(p, threshold=0.07)
  Ay_0 <- make_psd(q, threshold=0.07)
  
  # We'll do an example with ncomp=4
  K <- 4
  
  # 1) dual=FALSE
  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp = K,
    dual = FALSE,
    rank_Mx=3L, rank_Ax=3, rank_My=3, rank_Ay=3,
    var_threshold=1,
    max_k=100,  # partial expansions up to 10 
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  # 2) dual=TRUE
  fit_dual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp = K,
    dual = TRUE,
    rank_Mx=3, rank_Ax=3, rank_My=3, rank_Ay=3,
    var_threshold=1,
    max_k=100,
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  # Basic checks
  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp <= K)
  expect_s3_class(fit_nodual, "cross_projector")
  expect_s3_class(fit_dual, "cross_projector")
  
  # Compare singular values (d_full)
  d1 <- fit_nodual$d_full
  d2 <- fit_dual$d_full
  expect_true(length(d1) > 0 && length(d2) > 0)
  L <- min(length(d1), length(d2))
  ratio_sv <- d2[1:L] / pmax(1e-12, d1[1:L])
  # We just want them within a modest ratio
  expect_true(all(ratio_sv > 0.3 & ratio_sv < 3.0))
  
  # A helper to measure cross-column correlation
  # allowing sign flips or different order
  col_cors <- function(matA, matB) {
    # returns min of max correlation per column in matA
    # ignoring sign or col permutations
    stopifnot(ncol(matA) > 0, ncol(matB) > 0)
    out <- numeric(ncol(matA))
    for (j in seq_len(ncol(matA))) {
      # correlate matA col j with *all* columns in matB, take max of absolute
      cors <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(matB)), function(k) {
        cc <- cor(matA[, j], matB[, k])
        if (is.na(cc)) 0 else abs(cc)
      })
      out[j] <- max(cors)
    }
    mean(out)
  }
  
  # Compare factor scores if available
  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Tx) && !is.null(fit_dual$Tx)) {
    score_cor_x <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Tx, fit_dual$Tx)
    expect_true(score_cor_x > 0.5) 
  }
  if (!is.null(fit_nodual$Ty) && !is.null(fit_dual$Ty)) {
    score_cor_y <- col_cors(fit_nodual$Ty, fit_dual$Ty)
    expect_true(score_cor_y > 0.5) 
  }
  
  # Compare loadings in original domain
  expect_equal(nrow(fit_nodual$vx), p)
  expect_equal(nrow(fit_dual$vx), p)
  if (ncol(fit_nodual$vx) > 0 && ncol(fit_dual$vx) > 0) {
    cor_vx <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vx, fit_dual$vx)
    expect_true(cor_vx > 0.5)
  }
  
  expect_equal(nrow(fit_nodual$vy), q)
  expect_equal(nrow(fit_dual$vy), q)
  if (ncol(fit_nodual$vy) > 0 && ncol(fit_dual$vy) > 0) {
    cor_vy <- col_cors(fit_nodual$vy, fit_dual$vy)
    expect_true(cor_vy > 0.5)
  }
})


test_that("genplscorr2 handles low-rank data + constraints consistently", {
  set.seed(999)  # for reproducibility
  
  #### 1) Generate low-rank data (X, Y) + noise ####
  n <- 30
  p <- 20
  q <- 15
  
  # We assume rank ~ 4 for the "true" latent structure.
  rank_true <- 4
  
  # (a) Build a latent factor matrix for X: shape n x rank_true
  #     plus a separate factor for Y.
  #     Then create X, Y by (FactorX * LoadingsX) + noise, etc.
  
  # "true" latent factor scores for X, dimension n x rank_true
  Fx <- matrix(rnorm(n * rank_true, sd=1.0), n, rank_true)
  # loadings for X in p-d space
  Lx <- matrix(rnorm(p * rank_true, sd=1.0), p, rank_true)
  
  # "true" latent factor for Y, dimension n x rank_true
  # optionally correlated with X's factor: e.g. let's take Fx plus small random
  # or you can do a separate factor. We'll do correlated for demonstration:
  Fy <- Fx + matrix(rnorm(n * rank_true, sd=0.2), n, rank_true)
  Ly <- matrix(rnorm(q * rank_true, sd=1.0), q, rank_true)
  
  # Construct X, Y (rank ~ rank_true) + random noise
  Xbase <- Fx %*% t(Lx)   # shape n x p
  Ybase <- Fy %*% t(Ly)   # shape n x q
  
  noiseX <- matrix(rnorm(n * p, sd=0.5), n, p)
  noiseY <- matrix(rnorm(n * q, sd=0.5), n, q)
  
  X <- Xbase + noiseX
  Y <- Ybase + noiseY
  
  # Optionally center columns (typical for PLS):
  # We'll rely on genplscorr2's own 'preproc_x=pass()' or we can do:
  # X <- scale(X, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  # Y <- scale(Y, center=TRUE, scale=FALSE)
  
  #### 2) Build low-rank constraints for Mx, Ax, My, Ay ####
  # Let's produce, e.g., rank 5 expansions for each, plus small noise => shape n x n or p x p, etc.
  # We'll do something like U diag(...) V^T, with rank=5. Then we can add a dash of noise.
  
  make_lowrank_psd <- function(dimsize, r=5, noise_sd=0.01) {
    # generate a random U, V => shape dimsize x r
    U <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*r), dimsize, r)
    V <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*r), dimsize, r)
    # build rank-r mat => U diag(...) V^T
    # simplest: let diag(...) be positives (random?)
    diagvals <- runif(r, min=0.5, max=2.0)
    lowrank <- U %*% (diag(diagvals)) %*% t(V)  # shape dimsize x dimsize
    # symmetrize
    lowrank <- 0.5 * (lowrank + t(lowrank))
    # add small noise
    noise <- matrix(rnorm(dimsize*dimsize, sd=noise_sd), dimsize, dimsize)
    out <- lowrank + noise
    # make sure PSD => do a quick shift or crossprod approach
    # to ensure non-negative eigenvalues
    # simplest: crossprod => or we can do an eigen-cleaning
    out <- 0.5*(out + t(out))  # sym
    # We'll do a small "eigen-truncation" approach:
    eout <- eigen(out, symmetric=TRUE)
    lampos <- pmax(eout$values, 1e-5)
    out_psd <- eout$vectors %*% diag(lampos) %*% t(eout$vectors)
    out_psd
  }
  
  # Mx, My => n x n
  # Ax => p x p,   Ay => q x q
  Mx_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(n, r=5)
  My_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(n, r=5)
  Ax_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(p, r=5)
  Ay_0 <- make_lowrank_psd(q, r=5)
  
  #### 3) Run genplscorr2 => dual=FALSE vs. dual=TRUE ####
  # We'll request ncomp=6, see how many we actually get
  K <- 6
  
  fit_nodual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp=K,
    dual=FALSE,
    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
    var_threshold=.9,
    max_k=100,
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  fit_dual <- genplsc(
    X, Y,
    Mx=Mx_0, Ax=Ax_0,
    My=My_0, Ay=Ay_0,
    ncomp=K,
    dual=TRUE,
    rank_Mx=NULL, rank_Ax=NULL, rank_My=NULL, rank_Ay=NULL,
    var_threshold=.9,
    max_k=100,
    verbose=FALSE
  )
  
  #### 4) Basic checks ####
  # Check that each fit has # of components <= K
  expect_true(fit_nodual$ncomp <= K)
  expect_true(fit_dual$ncomp   <= K)
  
  # Confirm we have loadings of appropriate dimension
  expect_equal(dim(fit_nodual$vx)[1], p)  # p x ncomp
  expect_equal(dim(fit_dual$vx)[1],   p)
  
  # 5) Compare first few directions 
  # They may differ due to partial expansions, sign flips, etc.
  # We can do a rough check on correlation among the first 2-3 columns of vx or vy
  # e.g., the absolute correlation => should be fairly large if they're capturing similar directions.
  # but won't be identical => we just check if there's some overlap. 
  library(stats)
  ncomp_compare <- min(fit_nodual$ncomp, fit_dual$ncomp, 3)
  cor_thresh <- 0.5  # somewhat arbitrary
  for(j in seq_len(ncomp_compare)) {
    # correlation of loadings on X
    cval_x <- cor(fit_nodual$vx[,j], fit_dual$vx[,j])
    # correlation of loadings on Y
    cval_y <- cor(fit_nodual$vy[,j], fit_dual$vy[,j])
    # Just expect they're not trivial => we won't do expect_* but we can check 
    # they aren't negligible
    msg_x <- paste("component", j, "X-loadings correlation is non-trivial")
    msg_y <- paste("component", j, "Y-loadings correlation is non-trivial")
    testthat::expect_true(abs(cval_x) > 0.2, info = msg_x)
    testthat::expect_true(abs(cval_y) > 0.2, info = msg_y)
  }
  
  # You might also check factor scores or singular values, etc.
  # The point is: in a low-rank scenario, we'd see that both approaches 
  # pick up somewhat similar directions, though not identical.
})

test_that("genplscorr dual=TRUE row likeness returns correct dimensions", {
  set.seed(42)
  n <- 7; p <- 5; q <- 4
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, dual = TRUE,
                 force_row_likeness = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)

  expect_s3_class(fit, c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"))
  expect_equal(dim(fit$Tx), c(n, fit$ncomp))
  expect_equal(dim(fit$Ty), c(n, fit$ncomp))
})

1→#' @export
     2→#'
     3→#' @title Generalized partial least squares "regression decomposition" (GPLSREG)
     4→#'
     5→#' @description Computes generalized partial least squares "regression decomposition" between two data matrices.
     6→#' GPLSREG allows for the use of left (row) and right (column) weights for each data matrix.
     7→#'
     8→#' @param X Data matrix with \emph{I} rows and \emph{J} columns
     9→#' @param Y Data matrix with \emph{I} rows and \emph{K} columns
    10→#' @param XLW An \emph{I} by \emph{I} matrix of row weights for \code{X}. Default is \code{diag(nrow(X))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
    11→#' @param YLW An \emph{I} by \emph{I} matrix of row weights for \code{Y}. Default is \code{diag(nrow(Y))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
    12→#' @param XRW A \emph{J} by \emph{J} matrix of row weights for \code{X}. Default is \code{diag(ncol(X))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
    13→#' @param YRW A \emph{K} by \emph{K} matrix of row weights for \code{Y}. Default is \code{diag(ncol(Y))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
    14→#' @param components The number of components to return. If < 1 then the maximum components will be returned. Default = 0.
    15→#' @param tol default is .Machine$double.eps. A parameter to pass through to \code{\link[GSVD]{gplssvd}}; eliminates singular values that are effectively zero (and thus drops null components).
    16→#'
    17→#'
    18→#' @return A list of outputs
    19→#' \item{d}{A vector containing the singular values from each iteration.}
    20→#' \item{u}{Left (rows) singular vectors.}
    21→#' \item{v}{Right (columns) singular vectors. In GPLSREG sometimes called "weight matrix".}
    22→#' \item{lx}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{X}}
    23→#' \item{ly}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{Y}}
    24→#' \item{p}{Left (rows) generalized singular vectors.}
    25→#' \item{q}{Right (columns) generalized singular vectors.}
    26→#' \item{fi}{Left (rows) component scores.}
    27→#' \item{fj}{Right (columns) component scores.}
    28→#' \item{tx}{X "Latent vectors": A normed version of \code{lx} for use in rebuilding \code{X} data}
    29→#' \item{u_hat}{X "Loading matrix": A "predicted" version of \code{u} for use in rebuilding \code{X} data}
    30→#' \item{betas}{"Regression weights": Akin to betas for use in rebuilding \code{Y}}
    31→#' \item{X_reconstructeds}{A version of \code{X} reconstructed for each iteration (i.e., latent variable/component)}
    32→#' \item{Y_reconstructeds}{A version of \code{Y} reconstructed for each iteration (i.e., latent variable/component)}
    33→#' \item{X_residuals}{The residualized (i.e., \code{X - X_reconstructeds}) version of \code{X} for each iteration (i.e., latent variable/component)}
    34→#' \item{Y_residuals}{The residualized (i.e., \code{Y - Y_reconstructeds}) version of \code{Y} for each iteration (i.e., latent variable/component)}
    35→#' \item{r2_x}{Proportion of explained variance from \code{X} to each latent variable/component.}
    36→#' \item{r2_y}{Proportion of explained variance from \code{Y} to each latent variable/component.}
    37→#' \item{Y_reconstructed}{A version of \code{Y} reconstructed from all iterations (i.e., latent variables/components); see \code{components}.}
    38→#' \item{Y_residual}{The residualized (i.e., \code{Y - Y_reconstructed} from all iterations (i.e., latent variables/components); see \code{components}.}
    39→#'
    40→#' @seealso \code{\link{gpls_can}} \code{\link{gpls_cor}} \code{\link{pls_reg}} \code{\link{plsca_reg}} \code{\link[GSVD]{gplssvd}}
    41→#'
    42→#' @references
    43→#' Beaton, D., ADNI, Saporta, G., Abdi, H. (2019). A generalization of partial least squares regression and correspondence analysis for categorical and mixed data: An application with the ADNI data. \emph{bioRxiv}, 598888.
    44→#'
    45→#' @examples
    46→#'
    47→#'  \dontrun{
    48→#'  library(GSVD)
    49→#'  data("wine", package = "GSVD")
    50→#'  X <- scale(wine$objective)
    51→#'  Y <- scale(wine$subjective)
    52→#'
    53→#'
    54→#'  ## standard partial least squares "regression decomposition"
    55→#'  #### the first latent variable from reg & cor & can are identical in all PLSs.
    56→#'  gplsreg_pls_optimization <- gpls_reg(X, Y)
    57→#'
    58→#'  ## partial least squares "regression decomposition"
    59→#'  ### but with the optimization per latent variable of CCA
    60→#'  #### because of optimization, this ends up identical to
    61→#'  #### cca(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
    62→#'  gplsreg_cca_optimization <- gpls_reg( t(MASS::ginv(X)), t(MASS::ginv(Y)),
    63→#'       XRW = crossprod(X), YRW = crossprod(Y))
    64→#'
    65→#'  ## partial least squares "regression decomposition"
    66→#'  ### but with the optimization per latent variable of RRR/RDA
    67→#'  #### because of optimization, this ends up identical to
    68→#'  #### rrr(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
    69→#'  #### or rda(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
    70→#'  gplsreg_rrr_optimization <- gpls_reg( t(MASS::ginv(X)), Y, XRW = crossprod(X))
    71→#'
    72→#'  rm(X)
    73→#'  rm(Y)
    74→#'
    75→#'  ## partial least squares-correspondence analysis "regression decomposition"
    76→#'  #### the first latent variable from reg & cor & can are identical in all PLSs.
    77→#'  data("snps.druguse", package = "GSVD")
    78→#'  X <- make_data_disjunctive(snps.druguse$DATA1)
    79→#'  Y <- make_data_disjunctive(snps.druguse$DATA2)
    80→#'
    81→#'  X_ca_preproc <- ca_preproc(X)
    82→#'  Y_ca_preproc <- ca_preproc(Y)
    83→#'
    84→#'  gplsreg_plsca <- gpls_reg( X = X_ca_preproc$Z, Y = Y_ca_preproc$Z,
    85→#'      XLW = diag(1/X_ca_preproc$m), YLW = diag(1/Y_ca_preproc$m),
    86→#'      XRW = diag(1/X_ca_preproc$w), YRW = diag(1/Y_ca_preproc$w)
    87→#'  )
    88→#'  }
    89→#'
    90→#' @keywords multivariate, diagonalization, partial least squares
    91→#' @importFrom MASS ginv
    92→#' @importFrom GSVD invsqrt_psd_matrix sqrt_psd_matrix
    93→
    94→gpls_reg <- function(X, Y,
    95→                     XLW = diag(nrow(X)), YLW = diag(nrow(Y)),
    96→                     XRW = diag(ncol(X)), YRW = diag(ncol(Y)),
    97→                     components = 0, tol = .Machine$double.eps){
    98→
    99→
   100→  X_gsvd <- gsvd(X, XLW, XRW)
   101→    X_rank <- length(X_gsvd$d)
   102→    X_trace <- sum(X_gsvd$d^2)
   103→  rm(X_gsvd)
   104→
   105→  Y_gsvd <- gsvd(Y, YLW, YRW)
   106→    Y_rank <- length(Y_gsvd$d)
   107→    Y_trace <- sum(Y_gsvd$d^2)
   108→  rm(Y_gsvd)
   109→
   110→  stopped_early <- F
   111→
   112→  if((components > X_rank) | (components < 1)){
   113→    components <- X_rank
   114→  }
   115→
   116→  lx <- tx <- matrix(NA,nrow(X),components)
   117→  ly <- matrix(NA,nrow(Y),components)
   118→
   119→  u_hat <- fi <- p <- u <- matrix(NA,ncol(X),components)
   120→  fj <- q <- v <- matrix(NA,ncol(Y),components)
   121→
   122→  r2_x_cumulative <- r2_y_cumulative <- d <- betas <- rep(NA, components)
   123→
   124→  X_reconstructeds <- X_residuals <- array(NA,dim=c(nrow(X),ncol(X),components))
   125→  Y_reconstructeds <- Y_residuals <- array(NA,dim=c(nrow(Y),ncol(Y),components))
   126→
   127→  X_deflate <- X
   128→  Y_deflate <- Y
   129→
   130→  for(i in 1:components){
   131→
   132→    gplssvd_results <- gpls_cor(X_deflate, Y_deflate,
   133→                                XRW = XRW, YRW = YRW,
   134→                                XLW = XLW, YLW = YLW,
   135→                                components = 1, tol = tol)
   136→
   137→    u[,i] <- gplssvd_results$u
   138→    p[,i] <- gplssvd_results$p
   139→    fi[,i] <- gplssvd_results$fi
   140→    v[,i] <- gplssvd_results$v
   141→    q[,i] <- gplssvd_results$q
   142→    fj[,i] <- gplssvd_results$fj
   143→    d[i] <- gplssvd_results$d
   144→    lx[,i] <- gplssvd_results$lx
   145→    ly[,i] <- gplssvd_results$ly
   146→
   147→    tx[,i] <- lx[,i] / sqrt(sum(lx[,i]^2))
   148→    betas[i] <- t(ly[,i]) %*% tx[,i]
   149→    u_hat[,i] <- t(tx[,i]) %*% ( GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(XLW) %*% X_deflate %*% GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(XRW) )
   150→
   151→
   152→    X_reconstructeds[,,i] <- GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(XLW) %*% (tx[,i] %o% u_hat[,i]) %*% GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(XRW)
   153→      X_reconstructeds[abs(X_reconstructeds) < tol] <- 0
   154→    Y_reconstructeds[,,i] <- GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(YLW) %*% ((tx[,i] * betas[i]) %o% v[,i]) %*% GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(YRW)
   155→      Y_reconstructeds[abs(Y_reconstructeds) < tol] <- 0
   156→
   157→
   158→    X_residuals[,,i] <- (X_deflate - X_reconstructeds[,,i])
   159→      X_residuals[abs(X_residuals) < tol] <- 0
   160→    Y_residuals[,,i] <- (Y_deflate - Y_reconstructeds[,,i])
   161→      Y_residuals[abs(Y_residuals) < tol] <- 0
   162→
   163→    X_deflate <- X_residuals[,,i]
   164→    Y_deflate <- Y_residuals[,,i]
   165→
   166→
   167→    r2_x_cumulative[i] <- (X_trace-sum( ( GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(XLW) %*%  X_deflate %*% GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(XRW) ) ^2)) / X_trace
   168→    r2_y_cumulative[i] <- (Y_trace-sum( ( GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(YLW) %*%  Y_deflate %*% GSVD::sqrt_psd_matrix(YRW) ) ^2)) / Y_trace
   169→
   170→
   171→    if( (sum(Y_deflate^2) < tol) & (i < components) ){
   172→
   173→      stopped_early <- T
   174→      warning("gpls_reg: Y is fully deflated. Stopping early.")
   175→
   176→    }
   177→    if( (sum(X_deflate^2) < tol) & (i < components) ){
   178→
   179→      stopped_early <- T
   180→      warning("gpls_reg: X is fully deflated. Stopping early.")
   181→
   182→    }
   183→
   184→    if(stopped_early){
   185→      break
   186→    }
   187→
   188→  }
   189→
   190→  if(stopped_early){
   191→    u <- u[,1:i]
   192→    p <- p[,1:i]
   193→    fi <- fi[,1:i]
   194→    v <- v[,1:i]
   195→    q <- q[,1:i]
   196→    fj <- fj[,1:i]
   197→    d <- d[1:i]
   198→    lx <- lx[,1:i]
   199→    ly <- ly[,1:i]
   200→
   201→    tx <- tx[,1:i]
   202→    betas <- betas[1:i]
   203→    u_hat <- u_hat[,1:i]
   204→
   205→    X_reconstructeds <- X_reconstructeds[,,1:i]
   206→    Y_reconstructeds <- Y_reconstructeds[,,1:i]
   207→    X_residuals <- X_residuals[,,1:i]
   208→    Y_residuals <- Y_residuals[,,1:i]
   209→
   210→    r2_x_cumulative <- r2_x_cumulative[1:i]
   211→    r2_y_cumulative <- r2_y_cumulative[1:i]
   212→  }
   213→
   214→
   215→  Y_reconstructed <- GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(YLW) %*% (tx %*% diag(betas,length(betas),length(betas)) %*% t(v)) %*% GSVD::invsqrt_psd_matrix(YRW)
   216→    Y_reconstructed[abs(Y_reconstructed) < tol] <- 0
   217→  Y_residual <- Y - Y_reconstructed
   218→
   219→
   220→
   221→  rownames(tx) <- rownames(lx) <- rownames(X_reconstructeds) <- rownames(X_residuals) <- rownames(X)
   222→  rownames(fi) <- rownames(u) <- rownames(p) <- rownames(u_hat) <- colnames(X_reconstructeds) <- colnames(X_residuals) <- colnames(X)
   223→  rownames(Y_reconstructed) <- rownames(Y_residual) <- rownames(Y_reconstructeds) <- rownames(Y_residuals) <- rownames(Y)
   224→  rownames(fj) <- rownames(v) <- rownames(q) <- colnames(Y_reconstructed) <- colnames(Y_residual) <- colnames(Y_reconstructeds) <- colnames(Y_residuals) <- colnames(Y)
   225→
   226→
   227→  return( list(
   228→    d = d, u = u, v = v, lx = lx, ly = ly,
   229→    p = p, q = q, fi = fi, fj = fj,
   230→    tx = tx, u_hat = u_hat, betas = betas,
   231→    X_reconstructeds = X_reconstructeds, X_residuals = X_residuals,
   232→    Y_reconstructeds = Y_reconstructeds, Y_residuals = Y_residuals,
   233→    r2_x = diff(c(0,r2_x_cumulative)), r2_y = diff(c(0,r2_y_cumulative)),
   234→    Y_reconstructed = Y_reconstructed, Y_residual = Y_residual
   235→  ) )
   236→
   237→
   238→}
   239→
   240→#' @export
   241→#'
   242→#' @title Generalized partial least squares "correlation decomposition" (GPLSCOR)
   243→#'
   244→#' @description Computes generalized partial least squares "correlation decomposition" between two data matrices.
   245→#' GPLSCOR allows for the use of left (row) and right (column) weights for each data matrix.
   246→#'
   247→#' @param X Data matrix with \emph{I} rows and \emph{J} columns
   248→#' @param Y Data matrix with \emph{I} rows and \emph{K} columns
   249→#' @param XLW An \emph{I} by \emph{I} matrix of row weights for \code{X}. Default is \code{diag(nrow(X))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
   250→#' @param YLW An \emph{I} by \emph{I} matrix of row weights for \code{Y}. Default is \code{diag(nrow(Y))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
   251→#' @param XRW A \emph{J} by \emph{J} matrix of row weights for \code{X}. Default is \code{diag(ncol(X))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
   252→#' @param YRW A \emph{K} by \emph{K} matrix of row weights for \code{Y}. Default is \code{diag(ncol(Y))} (i.e., all ones on the diagonal; zeros off-diagonal).
   253→#' @param components The number of components to return. If < 1 then the maximum components will be returned. Default = 0.
   254→#' @param tol default is .Machine$double.eps. A parameter to pass through to \code{\link[GSVD]{gplssvd}}; eliminates singular values that are effectively zero (and thus drops null components).
   255→#'
   256→#'
   257→#' @return a list of outputs; see also \code{\link[GSVD]{gplssvd}}
   258→#' \item{d.orig}{A vector containing the singular values of DAT above the tolerance threshold (based on eigenvalues).}
   259→#' \item{l.orig}{A vector containing the eigen values of DAT above the tolerance threshold (\code{tol}).}
   260→#' \item{tau}{A vector that contains the (original) explained variance per component (via eigenvalues: \code{$l.orig}.}
   261→#' \item{d}{A vector of length \code{min(length(d.orig), k)} containing the retained singular values}
   262→#' \item{l}{A vector of length \code{min(length(l.orig), k)} containing the retained eigen values}
   263→#' \item{u}{Left (rows) singular vectors.}
   264→#' \item{p}{Left (rows) generalized singular vectors.}
   265→#' \item{fi}{Left (rows) component scores.}
   266→#' \item{lx}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{X}}
   267→#' \item{v}{Right (columns) singular vectors.}
   268→#' \item{q}{Right (columns) generalized singular vectors.}
   269→#' \item{fj}{Right (columns) component scores.}
   270→#' \item{ly}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{Y}}
   271→#'
   272→#' @seealso \code{\link{gpls_reg}} \code{\link{gpls_can}} \code{\link{pls_cor}} \code{\link{plsca_cor}} \code{\link{cca}} \code{\link{rrr}} \code{\link{rda}} \code{\link[GSVD]{gplssvd}}
   273→#'
   274→#' @references
   275→#' Beaton, D., ADNI, Saporta, G., Abdi, H. (2019). A generalization of partial least squares regression and correspondence analysis for categorical and mixed data: An application with the ADNI data. \emph{bioRxiv}, 598888.
   276→#' Beaton, D., Dunlop, J., & Abdi, H. (2016). Partial least squares correspondence analysis: A framework to simultaneously analyze behavioral and genetic data. \emph{Psychological methods}, \bold{21} (4), 621.
   277→#'
   278→#' @examples
   279→#'
   280→#'  \dontrun{
   281→#'  library(GSVD)
   282→#'  data("wine", package = "GSVD")
   283→#'  X <- scale(wine$objective)
   284→#'  Y <- scale(wine$subjective)
   285→#'
   286→#'  ## partial least squares "correlation decomposition"
   287→#'  #### the first latent variable from reg & cor & can are identical in all PLSs.
   288→#'  #### this is pls_cor(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
   289→#'  gplscor_pls_optimization <- gpls_cor(X, Y)
   290→#'
   291→#'  ## partial least squares "correlation decomposition"
   292→#'  ### but with the optimization per latent variable of CCA
   293→#'  #### this is cca(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
   294→#'  gplscor_cca_optimization <- gpls_cor( t(MASS::ginv(X)), t(MASS::ginv(Y)),
   295→#'       XRW = crossprod(X), YRW = crossprod(Y))
   296→#'
   297→#'  ## partial least squares "correlation decomposition"
   298→#'  ### but with the optimization per latent variable of RRR/RDA
   299→#'  #### this is rrr(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F) or
   300→#'  #### rda(X, Y, center_X = F, center_Y = F, scale_X = F, scale_Y = F)
   301→#'  gplscor_rrr_optimization <- gpls_cor( t(MASS::ginv(X)), Y, XRW = crossprod(X))
   302→#'
   303→#'
   304→#'  rm(X)
   305→#'  rm(Y)
   306→#'
   307→#'  ## partial least squares-correspondence analysis "correlation decomposition"
   308→#'  #### the first latent variable from reg & cor & can are identical in all PLSs.
   309→#'  data("snps.druguse", package = "GSVD")
   310→#'  X <- make_data_disjunctive(snps.druguse$DATA1)
   311→#'  Y <- make_data_disjunctive(snps.druguse$DATA2)
   312→#'
   313→#'  X_ca_preproc <- ca_preproc(X)
   314→#'  Y_ca_preproc <- ca_preproc(Y)
   315→#'
   316→#'  #### this is plsca_cor(X, Y)
   317→#'  gplscor_plsca <- gpls_cor( X = X_ca_preproc$Z, Y = Y_ca_preproc$Z,
   318→#'      XLW = diag(1/X_ca_preproc$m), YLW = diag(1/Y_ca_preproc$m),
   319→#'      XRW = diag(1/X_ca_preproc$w), YRW = diag(1/Y_ca_preproc$w)
   320→#'  )
   321→#'
   322→#'  }
   323→#'
   324→#' @keywords multivariate, diagonalization, partial least squares
   325→#' @importFrom MASS ginv
   326→
   327→gpls_cor <- function(X, Y,
   328→                  XLW = diag(nrow(X)), YLW = diag(nrow(Y)),
   329→                  XRW = diag(ncol(X)), YRW = diag(ncol(Y)),
   330→                  components = 0, tol = .Machine$double.eps){
   331→
   332→    ## that's it.
   333→    gplssvd(X, Y, XLW = XLW, YLW = YLW, XRW = XRW, YRW = YRW, k = components, tol = tol)
   334→
   335→}
   336→
   337→' @export
   338→#'
   339→#' @title Generalized partial least squares singular value decomposition
   340→#'
   341→#' @description
   342→#' \code{gplssvd} takes in left (\code{XLW}, \code{YLW}) and right (\code{XRW}, \code{YRW}) constraints (usually diagonal matrices, but any positive semi-definite matrix is fine) that are applied to each data matrix (\code{X} and \code{Y}), respectively.
   343→#'   Right constraints for each data matrix are used for the orthogonality conditions.
   344→#'
   345→#' @param X a data matrix
   346→#' @param Y a data matrix
   347→#' @param XLW \bold{X}'s \bold{L}eft \bold{W}eights -- the constraints applied to the left side (rows) of the \code{X} matrix and thus left singular vectors.
   348→#' @param YLW \bold{Y}'s \bold{L}eft \bold{W}eights -- the constraints applied to the left side (rows) of the \code{Y} matrix and thus left singular vectors.
   349→#' @param XRW \bold{X}'s \bold{R}ight \bold{W}eights -- the constraints applied to the right side (columns) of the \code{X} matrix and thus right singular vectors.
   350→#' @param YRW \bold{Y}'s \bold{R}ight \bold{W}eights -- the constraints applied to the right side (columns) of the \code{Y} matrix and thus right singular vectors.
   351→#' @param k total number of components to return though the full variance will still be returned (see \code{d_full}). If 0, the full set of components are returned.
   352→#' @param tol default is .Machine$double.eps. A tolerance level for eliminating effectively zero (small variance), negative, imaginary eigen/singular value components.
   353→#'
   354→#' @return A list with thirteen elements:
   355→#' \item{d_full}{A vector containing the singular values of DAT above the tolerance threshold (based on eigenvalues).}
   356→#' \item{l_full}{A vector containing the eigen values of DAT above the tolerance threshold (\code{tol}).}
   357→#' \item{d}{A vector of length \code{min(length(d_full), k)} containing the retained singular values}
   358→#' \item{l}{A vector of length \code{min(length(l_full), k)} containing the retained eigen values}
   359→#' \item{u}{Left (rows) singular vectors. Dimensions are \code{nrow(DAT)} by k.}
   360→#' \item{p}{Left (rows) generalized singular vectors.}
   361→#' \item{fi}{Left (rows) component scores.}
   362→#' \item{lx}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{X}}
   363→#' \item{v}{Right (columns) singular vectors.}
   364→#' \item{q}{Right (columns) generalized singular vectors.}
   365→#' \item{fj}{Right (columns) component scores.}
   366→#' \item{ly}{Latent variable scores for rows of \code{Y}}
   367→#'
   368→#' @seealso \code{\link{tolerance_svd}}, \code{\link{geigen}} and \code{\link{gsvd}}
   369→#'
   370→#' @examples
   371→#'
   372→#'  # Three "two-table" technique examples
   373→#'  data(wine)
   374→#'  X <- scale(wine$objective)
   375→#'  Y <- scale(wine$subjective)
   376→#'
   377→#'  ## Partial least squares (correlation)
   378→#'  pls.res <- gplssvd(X, Y)
   379→#'
   380→#'
   381→#'  ## Canonical correlation analysis (CCA)
   382→#'  ### NOTE:
   383→#'  #### This is not "traditional" CCA because of the generalized inverse.
   384→#'  #### However the results are the same as standard CCA when data are not rank deficient.
   385→#'  #### and this particular version uses tricks to minimize memory & computation
   386→#'  cca.res <- gplssvd(
   387→#'      X = MASS::ginv(t(X)),
   388→#'      Y = MASS::ginv(t(Y)),
   389→#'      XRW=crossprod(X),
   390→#'      YRW=crossprod(Y)
   391→#'  )
   392→#'
   393→#'
   394→#'  ## Reduced rank regression (RRR) a.k.a. redundancy analysis (RDA)
   395→#'  ### NOTE:
   396→#'  #### This is not "traditional" RRR because of the generalized inverse.
   397→#'  #### However the results are the same as standard RRR when data are not rank deficient.
   398→#'  rrr.res <- gplssvd(
   399→#'      X = MASS::ginv(t(X)),
   400→#'      Y = Y,
   401→#'      XRW=crossprod(X)
   402→#'  )
   403→#'
   404→#' @author Derek Beaton
   405→#' @keywords multivariate
   406→
   407→gplssvd <- function(X, Y, XLW, YLW, XRW, YRW, k = 0, tol = .Machine$double.eps){
   408→
   409→  # preliminaries
   410→  X_dimensions <- dim(X)
   411→  ## stolen from MASS::ginv()
   412→  if (length(X_dimensions) > 2 || !(is.numeric(X) || is.complex(X))){
   413→    stop("gplssvd: 'X' must be a numeric or complex matrix")
   414→  }
   415→  if (!is.matrix(X)){
   416→    X <- as.matrix(X)
   417→  }
   418→
   419→  # preliminaries
   420→  Y_dimensions <- dim(Y)
   421→  ## stolen from MASS::ginv()
   422→  if (length(Y_dimensions) > 2 || !(is.numeric(Y) || is.complex(Y))){
   423→    stop("gplssvd: 'Y' must be a numeric or complex matrix")
   424→  }
   425→  if (!is.matrix(Y)){
   426→    Y <- as.matrix(Y)
   427→  }
   428→
   429→  # check that row dimensions match
   430→  if(X_dimensions[1] != Y_dimensions[1]){
   431→    stop("gplssvd: X and Y must have the same number of rows")
   432→  }
   433→
   434→  # a few things about XLW for stopping conditions
   435→  XLW_is_missing <- missing(XLW)
   436→  if(!XLW_is_missing){
   437→
   438→    XLW_is_vector <- is.vector(XLW)
   439→
   440→    if(!XLW_is_vector){
   441→
   442→      if( nrow(XLW) != ncol(XLW) | nrow(XLW) != X_dimensions[1] ){
   443→        stop("gplssvd: nrow(XLW) does not equal ncol(XLW) or nrow(X)")
   444→      }
   445→
   446→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   447→      if(is_empty_matrix(XLW)){
   448→        stop("gplssvd: XLW is empty (i.e., all 0s")
   449→      }
   450→    }
   451→
   452→    if(XLW_is_vector){
   453→      if(length(XLW)!=X_dimensions[1]){
   454→        stop("gplssvd: length(XLW) does not equal nrow(X)")
   455→      }
   456→
   457→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   458→      # if(all(abs(XLW)<=tol)){
   459→      if(!are_all_values_positive(XLW)){
   460→        stop("gplssvd: XLW is not strictly positive values")
   461→      }
   462→    }
   463→  }
   464→
   465→  # a few things about XRW for stopping conditions
   466→  XRW_is_missing <- missing(XRW)
   467→  if(!XRW_is_missing){
   468→
   469→    XRW_is_vector <- is.vector(XRW)
   470→
   471→    if(!XRW_is_vector){
   472→
   473→      if( nrow(XRW) != ncol(XRW) | nrow(XRW) != X_dimensions[2] ){
   474→        stop("gplssvd: nrow(XRW) does not equal ncol(XRW) or ncol(X)")
   475→      }
   476→
   477→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   478→      if(is_empty_matrix(XRW)){
   479→        stop("gplssvd: XRW is empty (i.e., all 0s")
   480→      }
   481→    }
   482→
   483→    if(XRW_is_vector){
   484→      if(length(XRW)!=X_dimensions[2]){
   485→        stop("gplssvd: length(XRW) does not equal ncol(X)")
   486→      }
   487→
   488→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   489→      # if(all(abs(XRW)<=tol)){
   490→      if(!are_all_values_positive(XRW)){
   491→        stop("gplssvd: XRW is not strictly positive values")
   492→      }
   493→    }
   494→  }
   495→
   496→  # a few things about YLW for stopping conditions
   497→  YLW_is_missing <- missing(YLW)
   498→  if(!YLW_is_missing){
   499→
   500→    YLW_is_vector <- is.vector(YLW)
   501→
   502→    if(!YLW_is_vector){
   503→
   504→      if( nrow(YLW) != ncol(YLW) | nrow(YLW) != Y_dimensions[1] ){
   505→        stop("gplssvd: nrow(YLW) does not equal ncol(YLW) or nrow(Y)")
   506→      }
   507→
   508→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   509→      if(is_empty_matrix(YLW)){
   510→        stop("gplssvd: YLW is empty (i.e., all 0s")
   511→      }
   512→    }
   513→
   514→    if(YLW_is_vector){
   515→      if(length(YLW)!=Y_dimensions[1]){
   516→        stop("gplssvd: length(YLW) does not equal nrow(Y)")
   517→      }
   518→
   519→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   520→      # if(all(abs(YLW)<=tol)){
   521→      if(!are_all_values_positive(YLW)){
   522→        stop("gplssvd: YLW is not strictly positive values")
   523→      }
   524→    }
   525→  }
   526→
   527→  # a few things about YRW for stopping conditions
   528→  YRW_is_missing <- missing(YRW)
   529→  if(!YRW_is_missing){
   530→
   531→    YRW_is_vector <- is.vector(YRW)
   532→
   533→    if(!YRW_is_vector){
   534→
   535→      if( nrow(YRW) != ncol(YRW) | nrow(YRW) != Y_dimensions[2] ){
   536→        stop("gplssvd: nrow(YRW) does not equal ncol(YRW) or ncol(Y)")
   537→      }
   538→
   539→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   540→      if(is_empty_matrix(YRW)){
   541→        stop("gplssvd: YRW is empty (i.e., all 0s")
   542→      }
   543→    }
   544→
   545→    if(YRW_is_vector){
   546→      if(length(YRW)!=Y_dimensions[2]){
   547→        stop("gplssvd: length(YRW) does not equal ncol(Y)")
   548→      }
   549→
   550→      # if you gave me all zeros, I'm stopping.
   551→      # if(all(abs(YRW)<=tol)){
   552→      if(!are_all_values_positive(YRW)){
   553→        stop("gplssvd: YRW is not strictly positive values")
   554→      }
   555→    }
   556→  }
   557→
   558→  ## convenience checks *could* be removed* if problematic
   559→  # convenience checks & conversions; these are meant to minimize XLW's memory footprint
   560→  if(!XLW_is_missing){
   561→
   562→    ## this is a matrix call; THIS MAKES IT GO MISSING AND NEXT CHECKS FAIL.
   563→      ### technically, the following two checks actually handle this.
   564→      ### it is a diagonal, and then it's a vector that's all 1s
   565→    if( !XLW_is_vector ){
   566→
   567→      # if(is_identity_matrix(XLW) ){
   568→      #   XLW_is_missing <- T
   569→      #   XLW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   570→      # }
   571→
   572→      ## this is a matrix call
   573→      if( is_diagonal_matrix(XLW) ){
   574→        XLW <- diag(XLW)
   575→        XLW_is_vector <- T  # now it's a vector
   576→      }
   577→    }
   578→
   579→    ## this is a vector call
   580→    if( XLW_is_vector & all(XLW==1) ){
   581→      XLW_is_missing <- T
   582→      XLW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   583→    }
   584→  }
   585→
   586→  # convenience checks & conversions; these are meant to minimize XRW's memory footprint
   587→  if(!XRW_is_missing){
   588→    if( !XRW_is_vector ){
   589→
   590→      # if( is_identity_matrix(XRW) ){
   591→      #   XRW_is_missing <- T
   592→      #   XRW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   593→      # }
   594→
   595→      if( is_diagonal_matrix(XRW) ){
   596→        XRW <- diag(XRW)
   597→        XRW_is_vector <- T  # now it's a vector
   598→      }
   599→    }
   600→
   601→    if( XRW_is_vector & all(XRW==1) ){
   602→      XRW_is_missing <- T
   603→      XRW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   604→    }
   605→  }
   606→
   607→  # convenience checks & conversions; these are meant to minimize YLW's memory footprint
   608→  if(!YLW_is_missing){
   609→    if( !YLW_is_vector ){
   610→
   611→      # if( is_identity_matrix(YLW) ){
   612→      #   YLW_is_missing <- T
   613→      #   YLW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   614→      # }
   615→
   616→      if( is_diagonal_matrix(YLW) ){
   617→        YLW <- diag(YLW)
   618→        YLW_is_vector <- T  # now it's a vector
   619→      }
   620→
   621→    }
   622→
   623→    if( YLW_is_vector & all(YLW==1) ){
   624→      YLW_is_missing <- T
   625→      YLW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   626→    }
   627→  }
   628→
   629→  # convenience checks & conversions; these are meant to minimize YRW's memory footprint
   630→  if(!YRW_is_missing){
   631→    if( !YRW_is_vector ){
   632→
   633→      # if( is_identity_matrix(YRW) ){
   634→      #   YRW_is_missing <- T
   635→      #   YRW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   636→      # }
   637→
   638→      if( is_diagonal_matrix(YRW) ){
   639→        YRW <- diag(YRW)
   640→        YRW_is_vector <- T  # now it's a vector
   641→      }
   642→
   643→    }
   644→
   645→    if( YRW_is_vector & all(YRW==1) ){
   646→      YRW_is_missing <- T
   647→      YRW <- substitute() # neat! this makes it go missing
   648→    }
   649→  }
   650→
   651→
   652→  # this manipulates X as needed based on XLW
   653→  if(!XLW_is_missing){
   654→
   655→    if( XLW_is_vector ){
   656→      # X <- sweep(X,1,sqrt(XLW),"*") ## replace the sweep with * & t()
   657→      X <- X * sqrt(XLW)
   658→    }else{
   659→      XLW <- as.matrix(XLW)
   660→      # X <- (XLW %^% (1/2)) %*% X
   661→      X <- sqrt_psd_matrix(XLW) %*% X
   662→    }
   663→
   664→  }
   665→  # this manipulates X as needed based on XRW
   666→  if(!XRW_is_missing){
   667→
   668→    if( XRW_is_vector ){
   669→      sqrt_XRW <- sqrt(XRW)
   670→      # X <- sweep(X,2, sqrt_XRW,"*") ## replace the sweep with * & t()
   671→      X <- t(t(X) * sqrt_XRW)
   672→    }else{
   673→      XRW <- as.matrix(XRW)
   674→      # X <- X %*% (XRW %^% (1/2))
   675→      X <- X %*% sqrt_psd_matrix(XRW)
   676→    }
   677→
   678→  }
   679→  # this manipulates Y as needed based on YLW
   680→  if(!YLW_is_missing){
   681→
   682→    if( YLW_is_vector ){
   683→      # Y <- sweep(Y,1,sqrt(YLW),"*")  ## replace the sweep with * & t()
   684→      Y <- Y * sqrt(YLW)
   685→    }else{
   686→      YLW <- as.matrix(YLW)
   687→      Y <- sqrt_psd_matrix(YLW) %*% Y
   688→    }
   689→
   690→  }
   691→  # this manipulates Y as needed based on YRW
   692→  if(!YRW_is_missing){
   693→
   694→    if( YRW_is_vector ){
   695→      sqrt_YRW <- sqrt(YRW)
   696→      # Y <- sweep(Y,2,sqrt_YRW,"*")  ## replace the sweep with * & t()
   697→      Y <- t(t(Y) * sqrt_YRW)
   698→    }else{
   699→      YRW <- as.matrix(YRW)
   700→      # Y <- Y %*% (YRW %^% (1/2))
   701→      Y <- Y %*% sqrt_psd_matrix(YRW)
   702→    }
   703→
   704→  }
   705→
   706→
   707→  # all the decomposition things
   708→  if(k<=0){
   709→    k <- min(X_dimensions, Y_dimensions)
   710→  }
   711→
   712→  res <- tolerance_svd( t(X) %*% Y, nu=k, nv=k, tol=tol)
   713→  res$d_full <- res$d
   714→  res$l_full <- res$d_full^2
   715→  # res$tau <- (res$l_full/sum(res$l_full)) * 100
   716→  components.to.return <- min(length(res$d_full),k) #a safety check
   717→  res$d <- res$d_full[1:components.to.return]
   718→  res$l <- res$d^2
   719→  res$u <- res$u[,1:components.to.return, drop = FALSE]
   720→  res$v <- res$v[,1:components.to.return, drop = FALSE]
   721→
   722→  res$lx <- X %*% res$u
   723→  res$ly <- Y %*% res$v
   724→
   725→
   726→  # make scores according to weights
   727→  if(!XRW_is_missing){
   728→    if(XRW_is_vector){
   729→
   730→      # res$p <- sweep(res$u,1,1/sqrt_XRW,"*")
   731→      res$p <- res$u / sqrt_XRW
   732→      # res$fi <- sweep(sweep(res$p,1,XRW,"*"),2,res$d,"*")
   733→      res$fi <- t(t(res$p * XRW) * res$d)
   734→
   735→    }else{
   736→
   737→      # res$p <- (XRW %^% (-1/2)) %*% res$u
   738→      res$p <- invsqrt_psd_matrix(XRW) %*% res$u
   739→      # res$fi <- sweep((XRW %*% res$p),2,res$d,"*")
   740→      res$fi <- t(t(XRW %*% res$p) * res$d)
   741→
   742→    }
   743→  }else{
   744→
   745→    res$p <- res$u
   746→    # res$fi <- sweep(res$p,2,res$d,"*")
   747→    res$fi <- t(t(res$p) * res$d)
   748→
   749→  }
   750→
   751→  if(!YRW_is_missing){
   752→    if(YRW_is_vector){
   753→
   754→      # res$q <- sweep(res$v,1,1/sqrt_YRW,"*")
   755→      res$q <- res$v /sqrt_YRW
   756→      # res$fj <- sweep(sweep(res$q,1,YRW,"*"),2,res$d,"*")
   757→      res$fj <- t(t(res$q * YRW) * res$d)
   758→
   759→    }else{
   760→
   761→      # res$q <- (YRW %^% (-1/2)) %*% res$v
   762→      res$q <- invsqrt_psd_matrix(YRW) %*% res$v
   763→      # res$fj <- sweep((YRW %*% res$q),2,res$d,"*")
   764→      res$fj <- t(t(YRW %*% res$q) * res$d)
   765→
   766→    }
   767→  }else{
   768→
   769→    res$q <- res$v
   770→    # res$fj <- sweep(res$q,2,res$d,"*")
   771→    res$fj <- t(t(res$q) * res$d)
   772→
   773→  }
   774→
   775→  rownames(res$fi) <- rownames(res$u) <- rownames(res$p) <- colnames(X)
   776→  rownames(res$fj) <- rownames(res$v) <- rownames(res$q) <- colnames(Y)
   777→
   778→  rownames(res$lx) <- rownames(X)
   779→  rownames(res$ly) <- rownames(Y)
   780→
   781→  class(res) <- c("gplssvd", "GSVD", "list")
   782→  return(res)
   783→
   784→}
   785→
   786→
