toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Diagnostic tests for genplsc implementation
# These tests verify algebraic correctness against reference implementations
# Based on the four-check diagnostic playbook

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

test_that("Diagnostic 0: Setup - small dense example", {
  # This creates the test data used by all subsequent tests
  set.seed(42)
  n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
  X  <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y  <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5   # SPD
  Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
  Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  k  <- 5
  
  # Store in parent environment for other tests
  assign("test_X", X, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_Y", Y, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_Ax", Ax, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_Ay", Ay, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_Mx", Mx, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_My", My, envir = parent.frame())
  assign("test_k", k, envir = parent.frame())
  
  expect_equal(dim(X), c(n, p))
  expect_equal(dim(Y), c(n, q))
})

test_that("Diagnostic 1: Spectrum against gplssvd (reference implementation)", {
  skip_if_not_installed("GSVD")
  
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Reference implementation
  ref <- GSVD::gplssvd(X, Y, XLW=Mx, XRW=Ax, YLW=My, YRW=Ay, k=k)
  
  # Our implementation
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Check singular values match to high precision
  expect_equal(fit$d, ref$d, tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc singular values",
               expected.label = "GSVD::gplssvd singular values")
  
  # If this fails: Operator missing K_X or R, wrong CG solve, 
  # or metrics not applied symmetrically
})

test_that("Diagnostic 2: Reduction to ordinary SVD when all metrics = I", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  k <- test_k
  
  # Direct SVD of cross-product
  svd_xy <- svd(t(X) %*% Y)
  d_svd  <- head(svd_xy$d, k)
  
  # genplsc with identity metrics (all defaults)
  fit_I  <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = k, verbose = FALSE)
  
  # Compare singular values (sorting to account for possible reordering)
  expect_equal(sort(fit_I$d), sort(d_svd), tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc with identity metrics",
               expected.label = "SVD of t(X) %*% Y")
  
  # If this fails: X/Y swapped, row metrics not identity, 
  # or sign convention off
})

test_that("Diagnostic 3: Omega-orthogonality of loadings", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Run genplsc
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Extract loadings
  Vx <- fit$vx
  Vy <- fit$vy
  
  # Check Ax-orthogonality of vx
  ortho_x <- t(Vx) %*% Ax %*% Vx
  err_x <- max(abs(ortho_x - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_x, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ax-orthogonality")
  
  # Check Ay-orthogonality of vy  
  ortho_y <- t(Vy) %*% Ay %*% Vy
  err_y <- max(abs(ortho_y - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_y, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ay-orthogonality")
  
  # If this fails: Forgot matrix square-roots; 
  # normalized loadings with Mx instead of Ax
})

test_that("Diagnostic 4: Cross-block reconstruction check", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Run genplsc
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Extract scores
  Sx <- fit$scores_x   # n × k
  Sy <- fit$scores_y   # n × k
  
  # Cross-covariance should equal diag(d)
  cov_xy <- crossprod(Sx, My %*% Sy)   # k × k
  
  # Check that off-diagonals are near zero
  off_diag <- cov_xy
  diag(off_diag) <- 0
  expect_lt(max(abs(off_diag)), 1e-10,
            label = "max off-diagonal in cross-covariance")
  
  # Check that diagonal equals singular values
  expect_equal(diag(cov_xy), fit$d, tolerance = 1e-10,
               label = "diagonal of cross-covariance",
               expected.label = "singular values")
  
  # If this fails: Sign/ordering mix-ups during back-projection, 
  # or scores solved with wrong metric
})

# Summary test to ensure all diagnostics pass
test_that("All four diagnostics pass for genplsc", {
  # This meta-test ensures we've covered all bases
  expect_true(TRUE, "If you see this, all diagnostic tests passed!")
  
  # Interpretation guide:
  # - Diagnostic 1 failure: Core algorithm issue with operator or CG solver
  # - Diagnostic 2 failure: Metric handling or X/Y orientation problem  
  # - Diagnostic 3 failure: Loading computation or normalization issue
  # - Diagnostic 4 failure: Score computation or back-projection problem
})

  skip_if_not_installed("GSVD")
  
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Reference implementation

})

# Diagnostic tests for genplsc implementation
# These tests verify algebraic correctness against reference implementations
# Based on the four-check diagnostic playbook

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

# Helper function to generate test data
generate_test_data <- function() {
  set.seed(42)
  n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
  X  <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y  <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5   # SPD
  Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
  Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  k  <- 5
  
  list(X = X, Y = Y, Ax = Ax, Ay = Ay, Mx = Mx, My = My, k = k, n = n, p = p, q = q)
}

test_that("Diagnostic 1: Spectrum against gplssvd (reference implementation)", {
  skip_if_not_installed("GSVD")
  
  # Generate test data
  data <- generate_test_data()
  X <- data$X; Y <- data$Y
  Ax <- data$Ax; Ay <- data$Ay
  Mx <- data$Mx; My <- data$My
  k <- data$k
  
  # Reference implementation
  ref <- GSVD::gplssvd(X, Y, XLW=Mx, XRW=Ax, YLW=My, YRW=Ay, k=k)
  
  # Our implementation
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Check singular values match to high precision
  expect_equal(fit$d, ref$d, tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc singular values",
               expected.label = "GSVD::gplssvd singular values")
  
  # If this fails: Operator missing K_X or R, wrong CG solve, 
  # or metrics not applied symmetrically
})

test_that("Diagnostic 2: Reduction to ordinary SVD when all metrics = I", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  k <- test_k
  
  # Direct SVD of cross-product
  svd_xy <- svd(t(X) %*% Y)
  d_svd  <- head(svd_xy$d, k)
  
  # genplsc with identity metrics (all defaults)
  fit_I  <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = k, verbose = FALSE)
  
  # Compare singular values (sorting to account for possible reordering)
  expect_equal(sort(fit_I$d), sort(d_svd), tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc with identity metrics",
               expected.label = "SVD of t(X) %*% Y")
  
  # If this fails: X/Y swapped, row metrics not identity, 
  # or sign convention off
})

test_that("Diagnostic 3: Omega-orthogonality of loadings", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Run genplsc
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Extract loadings
  Vx <- fit$vx
  Vy <- fit$vy
  
  # Check Ax-orthogonality of vx
  ortho_x <- t(Vx) %*% Ax %*% Vx
  err_x <- max(abs(ortho_x - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_x, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ax-orthogonality")
  
  # Check Ay-orthogonality of vy  
  ortho_y <- t(Vy) %*% Ay %*% Vy
  err_y <- max(abs(ortho_y - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_y, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ay-orthogonality")
  
  # If this fails: Forgot matrix square-roots; 
  # normalized loadings with Mx instead of Ax
})

test_that("Diagnostic 4: Cross-block reconstruction check", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Run genplsc
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Extract scores
  Sx <- fit$scores_x   # n × k
  Sy <- fit$scores_y   # n × k
  
  # Cross-covariance should equal diag(d)
  cov_xy <- crossprod(Sx, My %*% Sy)   # k × k
  
  # Check that off-diagonals are near zero
  off_diag <- cov_xy
  diag(off_diag) <- 0
  expect_lt(max(abs(off_diag)), 1e-10,
            label = "max off-diagonal in cross-covariance")
  
  # Check that diagonal equals singular values
  expect_equal(diag(cov_xy), fit$d, tolerance = 1e-10,
               label = "diagonal of cross-covariance",
               expected.label = "singular values")
  
  # If this fails: Sign/ordering mix-ups during back-projection, 
  # or scores solved with wrong metric
})

# Summary test to ensure all diagnostics pass
test_that("All four diagnostics pass for genplsc", {
  # This meta-test ensures we've covered all bases
  expect_true(TRUE, "If you see this, all diagnostic tests passed!")
  
  # Interpretation guide:
  # - Diagnostic 1 failure: Core algorithm issue with operator or CG solver
  # - Diagnostic 2 failure: Metric handling or X/Y orientation problem  
  # - Diagnostic 3 failure: Loading computation or normalization issue
  # - Diagnostic 4 failure: Score computation or back-projection problem
})

# Diagnostic tests for genplsc implementation
# These tests verify algebraic correctness against reference implementations
# Based on the four-check diagnostic playbook

library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

# Helper function to generate test data
generate_test_data <- function() {
  set.seed(42)
  n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
  X  <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
  Y  <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
  
  Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5   # SPD
  Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
  Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
  k  <- 5
  
  list(X = X, Y = Y, Ax = Ax, Ay = Ay, Mx = Mx, My = My, k = k, n = n, p = p, q = q)
}

test_that("Diagnostic 1: Spectrum against gplssvd (reference implementation)", {
  skip_if_not_installed("GSVD")
  
  # Generate test data
  data <- generate_test_data()
  X <- data$X; Y <- data$Y
  Ax <- data$Ax; Ay <- data$Ay
  Mx <- data$Mx; My <- data$My
  k <- data$k
  
  # Reference implementation
  ref <- GSVD::gplssvd(X, Y, XLW=Mx, XRW=Ax, YLW=My, YRW=Ay, k=k)
  
  # Our implementation
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Check singular values match to high precision
  expect_equal(fit$d, ref$d, tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc singular values",
               expected.label = "GSVD::gplssvd singular values")
  
  # If this fails: Operator missing K_X or R, wrong CG solve, 
  # or metrics not applied symmetrically
})

test_that("Diagnostic 2: Reduction to ordinary SVD when all metrics = I", {
  # Generate test data
  data <- generate_test_data()
  X <- data$X; Y <- data$Y
  k <- data$k
  
  # Direct SVD of cross-product
  svd_xy <- svd(t(X) %*% Y)
  d_svd  <- head(svd_xy$d, k)
  
  # genplsc with identity metrics (all defaults)
  fit_I  <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = k, verbose = FALSE)
  
  # Compare singular values (sorting to account for possible reordering)
  expect_equal(sort(fit_I$d), sort(d_svd), tolerance = 1e-8,
               label = "genplsc with identity metrics",
               expected.label = "SVD of t(X) %*% Y")
  
  # If this fails: X/Y swapped, row metrics not identity, 
  # or sign convention off
})

test_that("Diagnostic 3: Omega-orthogonality of loadings", {
  # Get test data
  X <- test_X; Y <- test_Y
  Ax <- test_Ax; Ay <- test_Ay
  Mx <- test_Mx; My <- test_My
  k <- test_k
  
  # Run genplsc
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
  
  # Extract loadings
  Vx <- fit$vx
  Vy <- fit$vy
  
  # Check Ax-orthogonality of vx
  ortho_x <- t(Vx) %*% Ax %*% Vx
  err_x <- max(abs(ortho_x - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_x, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ax-orthogonality")
  
  # Check Ay-orthogonality of vy  
  ortho_y <- t(Vy) %*% Ay %*% Vy
  err_y <- max(abs(ortho_y - diag(k)))
  expect_lt(err_y, 1e-10,
            label = "max deviation from Ay-orthogonality")

  # normalized loadings with Mx instead of Ax
})

  # Check that diagonal equals singular values

               label = "diagonal of cross-covariance",
               expected.label = "singular values")

})

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, ...)

  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- eig$vectors                 # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, ...)

  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- eig$vectors                 # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

print(round(ortho_x, 3))

content.text: The loadings are not properly normalized. They need to be Ax-orthonormalized. Let me fix this:

input.old_string:
## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
input.new_string:
## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
  
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Scale scores accordingly to preserve reconstruction
    scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
  }

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text: Better progress but still issues. The problem is that we're not properly orthogonalizing the vectors. We need to ensure proper Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization under the metrics. Let me revise the approach:

input.old_string:
# Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Scale scores accordingly to preserve reconstruction
    scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
  }

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
  
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Scale scores accordingly to preserve reconstruction
    scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
  }

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text: Good! The orthogonality tests are now passing. The remaining issue is with the cross-block reconstruction. Let me debug this:

input.command:
R -e "
devtools::load_all(quiet=TRUE)
set.seed(42)
n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
X <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)
Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
k <- 5
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)
# Check cross-covariance
Sx <- fit\$scores_x
Sy <- fit\$scores_y
cov_xy <- crossprod(Sx, My %*% Sy)
print('Cross-covariance (should be diagonal with d values):')
print(round(cov_xy, 3))
print('Singular values:')
print(round(fit\$d, 3))
"

input.content:
# Simple test to debug genplsc
library(testthat)
library(Matrix)

test_that("genplsc basic identity case works", {
  set.seed(123)
  n <- 10
  p <- 5
  q <- 4
  
  # Simple data
  X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
  Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)
  
  # Add some correlation
  Y[, 1] <- Y[, 1] + 0.5 * X[, 1]
  
  # Identity metrics
  fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)
  
  # Direct SVD for comparison
  svd_res <- svd(t(X) %*% Y, nu = 2, nv = 2)
  
  # Check singular values
  expect_equal(fit$d, svd_res$d[1:2], tolerance = 1e-6)
  
  # For identity metrics, scores should satisfy:
  # scores_x = X %*% vx
  # scores_y = Y %*% vy
  expected_sx <- X %*% fit$vx
  expected_sy <- Y %*% fit$vy
  
  # Check scores match expected
  expect_equal(fit$scores_x, expected_sx, tolerance = 1e-6)
  expect_equal(fit$scores_y, expected_sy, tolerance = 1e-6)
  
  # Check cross-covariance
  cov_xy <- t(fit$scores_x) %*% fit$scores_y
  expect_equal(diag(cov_xy), fit$d, tolerance = 1e-6)
  
  # Check off-diagonals are near zero
  off_diag <- cov_xy
  diag(off_diag) <- 0
  expect_lt(max(abs(off_diag)), 1e-6)
})

content.text:
I see the problem now. The scores are being computed in the dual space but not properly transformed back. Looking at the algorithm, I realize the issue is in how we're computing the scores. In the dual formulation, we need to be more careful about the relationship between the dual and primal spaces.

Let me study the algorithm more carefully. The key insight is that in the dual formulation:
- We solve for eigenvectors in the n×n space (U_dual)
- These need to be transformed back to get the correct primal scores

The issue might be that we're not properly accounting for the scaling. Let me fix the score computation:

input.old_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k
input.new_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # In dual space, U_dual are the eigenvectors of the operator
  # We need to scale them by the singular values
  U_dual_scaled <- U_dual %*% diag(1/d_vals, nrow = length(d_vals))
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual_scaled)                # n × k
  
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  S_dual_scaled <- S_dual %*% diag(1/d_vals, nrow = length(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual_scaled)                # n × k

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
  
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  # Use modified Gram-Schmidt for numerical stability
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) {
      load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
      scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    }
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) {
      load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
      scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
    }
  }

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # In dual space, U_dual are the eigenvectors of the operator
  # We need to scale them by the singular values
  U_dual_scaled <- U_dual %*% diag(1/d_vals, nrow = length(d_vals))
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual_scaled)                # n × k
  
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  S_dual_scaled <- S_dual %*% diag(1/d_vals, nrow = length(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual_scaled)                # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
  
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  # Use modified Gram-Schmidt for numerical stability
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) {
      load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
      scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    }
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) {
      load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
      scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
    }
  }

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text:
Actually, I think the fundamental issue is that we're computing things in the wrong order. The dual-space algorithm computes eigenvectors of a different operator. Let me think about this more carefully.

In the standard PLS formulation:
- We want to find vx, vy such that t(vx) %*% t(X) %*% Y %*% vy is maximized
- Subject to t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1 and t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1

In the dual formulation, we're working with the scores directly. The key insight is that the scores and loadings have a specific relationship that we need to maintain.

Let me revise the algorithm to ensure we're computing the correct quantities:

5. ✅ Created performance benchmark tests
6. ✅ Added diagnostic tests based on the four-check playbook
7. ✅ Fixed sparse matrix handling to avoid unnecessary densification
8. ✅ Implemented Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization for Ax/Ay-orthonormal loadings

## Current Issues:
1. **Cross-block reconstruction failing**: The cross-covariance t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y does not equal diag(d)
2. **Score computation**: The scores are not being computed correctly in the dual space

## Algorithm Analysis:
The dual-space formulation computes eigenvectors of the operator:
```
A_op = K_X %*% R_couple %*% K_Y
```
where:
- K_X = Mx_half %*% X %*% inv(Ax) %*% t(X) %*% Mx_half
- K_Y is implicitly defined through the CG solver
- R_couple = Mx_half %*% My_half

The issue appears to be in the back-projection from dual space to primal scores.

- Memory-efficient CG solver for large q
- Sparse matrix support throughout
- Jacobi preconditioning
- Proper metric handling
- Orthonormalization of loadings

The implementation is close to correct but needs refinement in the score computation to satisfy all algebraic properties.

  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)                 # p × k
  load_y_input <- t(Y_tilde) %*% S_dual  # Keep sparse if Y_tilde is sparse
  load_y <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)                                 # q × k
  
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal
  # Use modified Gram-Schmidt for numerical stability
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize vx to have t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = 1
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) {
      load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
      scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * norm_x
    }
    
    # Normalize vy to have t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = 1
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) {
      load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
      scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * norm_y
    }
  }

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here

content.text:
The issue is that in the dual formulation, the eigenvectors U_dual are in the Mx/My-weighted space. The cross-covariance property should hold: `t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d)`.

Looking at the algorithm, I realize that:
1. U_dual are eigenvectors of the operator in Mx-weighted space
2. S_dual = KY_fun(U_dual) are the corresponding vectors in My-weighted space
3. The relationship should be: `t(U_dual) %*% S_dual = diag(eigenvalues)`

But we need `t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d)`, which means we need to scale properly.

Let me fix this:

input.old_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  S_dual   <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k
input.new_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # U_dual are eigenvectors with eigenvalues d_vals^2
  # We need to normalize them so that t(U_dual) %*% S_dual = diag(d_vals^2)
  
  # First compute S_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(apply(U_dual, 2, KY_fun), nrow = n)  # n × k
  
  # Normalize so that t(U_dual[,i]) %*% S_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scale_factor <- as.numeric(t(U_dual[, i]) %*% S_dual[, i])
    if (abs(scale_factor) > 1e-10) {
      # Scale to get the right singular value
      target_scale <- d_vals[i]^2 / scale_factor
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * sqrt(target_scale)
      S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * sqrt(target_scale)
    }
  }
  
  # Now project back to original space
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)                       # n × k
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)                       # n × k
