content.content:
Applied 1 edit to /Users/bbuchsbaum/code/genpca/R/genplsc.R:
1. Replaced "  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@metho..." with "  # Check if preprocessing densified sparse matric..."

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py@method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y_temp <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scores_y_temp[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
  }
  scores_y <- scores_y_temp  # n × k
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i])
    scores_y[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text: I see the issue. We're always calling `as.matrix()` on sparse matrices before preprocessing, which defeats the purpose of sparse matrix support. Let me fix this to avoid densification when using pass-through preprocessing:

edits.old_string:

  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices

  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  # Check if preprocessing densified sparse matrices
  # Note: multivarious uses S3 not S4, so we check the method field directly
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px$method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py$method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")

  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  # Note: multivarious currently requires regular matrices
  # For pass-through preprocessing, this won't densify
  # For centering/scaling sparse data, consider sparse-aware alternatives
  X_for_prep <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
  Y_for_prep <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
  Xp  <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_for_prep)   # n × p
  Yp  <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_for_prep)   # n × q
  # Convert back to sparse if original was sparse and transform preserved sparsity
  # Check if preprocessing densified sparse matrices
  # Note: multivarious uses S3 not S4, so we check the method field directly
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix") && !identical(px$method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix") && !identical(py$method, "pass")) {
    warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y may have densified it")
  }
  if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix(Xp)
  if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix(Yp)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y_temp <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scores_y_temp[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
  }
  scores_y <- scores_y_temp  # n × k
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i])
    scores_y[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

Let me update the genplsc implementation to follow this pattern and avoid unnecessary densification:

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Only densify if preprocessing is not pass-through
  # This preserves sparse matrices when no preprocessing is needed
  if (identical(px$method, "pass")) {
    Xp <- X
  } else {
    # multivarious requires regular matrices for non-pass preprocessing
    X_dense <- if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) as.matrix(X) else X
    Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X_dense)
    if (inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X requires densification")
    }
  }
  
  if (identical(py$method, "pass")) {
    Yp <- Y
  } else {
    # multivarious requires regular matrices for non-pass preprocessing
    Y_dense <- if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(Y) else Y
    Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y_dense)
    if (inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y requires densification")
    }
  }

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y_temp <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scores_y_temp[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
  }
  scores_y <- scores_y_temp  # n × k
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i])
    scores_y[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = as.matrix(load_x),
    vy = as.matrix(load_y),
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = as.matrix(scores_x),
    scores_y  = as.matrix(scores_y),
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

lines: -  # Only densify if preprocessing is not pass-through
-  # This preserves sparse matrices when no preprocessing is needed
-    # multivarious requires regular matrices for non-pass preprocessing
-      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X requires densification")
+  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
+  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
+  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
+    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
+      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
-    # multivarious requires regular matrices for non-pass preprocessing
-      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y requires densification")
+    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
+      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
+  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
-  # Note: cross_projector expects regular matrices, so we convert here
-  # This is acceptable since loadings (p×k, q×k) and scores (n×k) are typically small
+  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)

    scores_y[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])

    scores_x[, i] <- as.vector(Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i, drop = FALSE]))
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y_temp <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scores_y_temp[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
  }
  scores_y <- scores_y_temp  # n × k
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i])
    scores_y[, i] <- Yp %*% solve_Ay(load_y[, i])
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text:
You're correct! Looking at line 118-119, the code is creating a dense matrix with `diag(diag(Ay_reg))`. This creates:
1. `diag(Ay_reg)` extracts the diagonal as a vector
2. `diag(...)` creates a dense diagonal matrix from that vector

This unnecessarily densifies what should remain sparse. Additionally, looking at the Rlinsolve documentation would help understand the proper way to pass preconditioners.

Let me also check if there are other places where we might be inadvertently densifying sparse matrices:

               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)

content.text:
I found two issues:

1. **Line 119**: `diag(diag(Ay_reg))` creates a dense matrix
2. **Line 119**: `diag(q)` also creates a dense identity matrix

Based on your suggestion and the sparse matrix best practices, here's my plan to fix these issues:

input.plan:
## Plan to Fix Sparse Matrix Densification Issues

### 1. Fix Jacobi Preconditioner (lines 118-119)
Replace:
```r
Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
           diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
```

With:
```r
Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi") 
           Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
```

This change:
- Uses `Matrix::Diagonal()` to create a sparse diagonal matrix for the Jacobi preconditioner
- Returns `NULL` for no preconditioning (lsolve.cg treats NULL as identity)
- Avoids creating any dense matrices

### 2. Update lsolve.cg call (line 127)
Change the preconditioner parameter to handle the new format:
```r
preconditioner = Mprec,
```
The lsolve.cg function should handle both sparse Matrix objects and NULL appropriately.

### 3. Benefits
- Maintains sparsity throughout the computation
- Reduces memory usage for large sparse problems
- Improves performance by avoiding unnecessary dense matrix operations

This fix ensures that the sparse matrix support is preserved throughout the iterative solver, which is critical for the memory-efficient implementation that was the original goal of this genplsc function.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, just extract diagonal (works for sparse)
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               diag(diag(Ay_reg)) else diag(q)
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
  }
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- as.vector(Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i, drop = FALSE]))
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text: The Jacobi preconditioner fix is working, but we still have the cross-block reconstruction issue. Let me run a more detailed debug to understand what's happening:

input.content:
# Debug cross-block reconstruction issue in detail

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

# Use the same test data as diagnostic test 4
set.seed(42)
n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
X  <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
Y  <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)

Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5   # SPD
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
k  <- 5

# Run genplsc
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)

# Extract components
Sx <- fit$scores_x   # n × k
Sy <- fit$scores_y   # n × k
Vx <- fit$vx         # p × k  
Vy <- fit$vy         # q × k

# Check the fundamental relationships
cat("=== Checking fundamental genPLS relationships ===\n\n")

# 1. Check if scores match the definition: sx = X * Ax^{-1} * vx
cat("1. Score computation check:\n")
expected_sx <- X %*% solve(Ax) %*% Vx
score_diff_x <- max(abs(Sx - expected_sx))
cat("   max|scores_x - X*Ax^{-1}*vx| =", score_diff_x, "\n")

expected_sy <- Y %*% solve(Ay) %*% Vy
score_diff_y <- max(abs(Sy - expected_sy))
cat("   max|scores_y - Y*Ay^{-1}*vy| =", score_diff_y, "\n\n")

# 2. Check orthogonality of loadings
cat("2. Loading orthogonality check:\n")
ortho_x <- t(Vx) %*% Ax %*% Vx
cat("   max|t(vx)*Ax*vx - I| =", max(abs(ortho_x - diag(k))), "\n")

ortho_y <- t(Vy) %*% Ay %*% Vy
cat("   max|t(vy)*Ay*vy - I| =", max(abs(ortho_y - diag(k))), "\n\n")

# 3. Check cross-block reconstruction
cat("3. Cross-block reconstruction:\n")
cov_xy <- t(Sx) %*% My %*% Sy   # k × k
cat("   Cross-covariance matrix:\n")
print(round(cov_xy, 4))
cat("\n   Expected (singular values):\n")
print(fit$d)
cat("\n   Diagonal of cross-cov:\n")
print(diag(cov_xy))
cat("\n   max|diag(cov_xy) - d| =", max(abs(diag(cov_xy) - fit$d)), "\n")
cat("   max off-diagonal =", max(abs(cov_xy - diag(diag(cov_xy)))), "\n\n")

# 4. Let's check the generalized singular value decomposition property
# X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
cat("4. Generalized SVD property check:\n")
for (i in 1:min(2, k)) {
  lhs <- t(X) %*% My %*% Y %*% Vy[, i]
  rhs <- Ax %*% Vx[, i] * fit$d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": max|X'*My*Y*vy - Ax*vx*d| / |rhs| =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# 5. Check if the issue is in the scaling
cat("\n5. Investigating scaling issue:\n")
# What if we compute the cross-covariance without the My metric?
cov_xy_no_metric <- t(Sx) %*% Sy
cat("   Cross-cov without My metric:\n")
print(round(diag(cov_xy_no_metric), 4))

# What's the ratio?

# Debug cross-block reconstruction issue in detail

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

# Use the same test data as diagnostic test 4
set.seed(42)
n <- 30; p <- 8; q <- 10
X  <- matrix(rnorm(n*p), n, p)
Y  <- matrix(rnorm(n*q), n, q)

Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p)) + diag(p) * 0.5   # SPD
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q)) + diag(q) * 0.5
Mx <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
My <- diag(runif(n, .5, 1.5))
k  <- 5

# Run genplsc
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx=Mx, Ax=Ax, My=My, Ay=Ay, ncomp=k, verbose=FALSE)

# Extract components
Sx <- fit$scores_x   # n × k
Sy <- fit$scores_y   # n × k
Vx <- fit$vx         # p × k  
Vy <- fit$vy         # q × k

# Check the fundamental relationships
cat("=== Checking fundamental genPLS relationships ===\n\n")

# 1. Check if scores match the definition: sx = X * Ax^{-1} * vx
cat("1. Score computation check:\n")
expected_sx <- X %*% solve(Ax) %*% Vx
score_diff_x <- max(abs(Sx - expected_sx))
cat("   max|scores_x - X*Ax^{-1}*vx| =", score_diff_x, "\n")

expected_sy <- Y %*% solve(Ay) %*% Vy
score_diff_y <- max(abs(Sy - expected_sy))
cat("   max|scores_y - Y*Ay^{-1}*vy| =", score_diff_y, "\n\n")

# 2. Check orthogonality of loadings
cat("2. Loading orthogonality check:\n")
ortho_x <- t(Vx) %*% Ax %*% Vx
cat("   max|t(vx)*Ax*vx - I| =", max(abs(ortho_x - diag(k))), "\n")

ortho_y <- t(Vy) %*% Ay %*% Vy
cat("   max|t(vy)*Ay*vy - I| =", max(abs(ortho_y - diag(k))), "\n\n")

# 3. Check cross-block reconstruction
cat("3. Cross-block reconstruction:\n")
cov_xy <- t(Sx) %*% My %*% Sy   # k × k
cat("   Cross-covariance matrix:\n")
print(round(cov_xy, 4))
cat("\n   Expected (singular values):\n")
print(fit$d)
cat("\n   Diagonal of cross-cov:\n")
print(diag(cov_xy))
cat("\n   max|diag(cov_xy) - d| =", max(abs(diag(cov_xy) - fit$d)), "\n")
cat("   max off-diagonal =", max(abs(cov_xy - diag(diag(cov_xy)))), "\n\n")

# 4. Let's check the generalized singular value decomposition property
# X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
cat("4. Generalized SVD property check:\n")
for (i in 1:min(2, k)) {
  lhs <- t(X) %*% My %*% Y %*% Vy[, i]
  rhs <- Ax %*% Vx[, i] * fit$d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": max|X'*My*Y*vy - Ax*vx*d| / |rhs| =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# 5. Check if the issue is in the scaling
cat("\n5. Investigating scaling issue:\n")
# What if we compute the cross-covariance without the My metric?
cov_xy_no_metric <- t(Sx) %*% Sy
cat("   Cross-cov without My metric:\n")
print(round(diag(cov_xy_no_metric), 4))

# What's the ratio?
cat("\n   Ratio of cross-cov with/without My:\n")
print(round(diag(cov_xy) / diag(cov_xy_no_metric), 4))

input.plan:
## Plan to Fix Dual to Primal Scaling

### Problem Analysis
The current implementation has a convoluted approach to scaling that:
- Normalizes U_dual to unit Euclidean norm (which loses important scaling information)
- Uses complex Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization loops
- Tries to retrofit the cross-covariance relationship after the fact
- Breaks sparsity by using many scalar operations
- Is numerically fragile

### Proposed Solution
Replace the entire back-projection and normalization sections with the mathematically correct and elegant approach:

```r
## ---- 8. back-project to primal scores --------------------------------
# The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
# We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores

# Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
for (i in 1:ncomp) {
  S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
}

# Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
# This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

## ---- 9. compute loadings from scores ----------------------------------
# vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x (normalized under Ax)
# vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y (normalized under Ay)

load_x <- matrix(0, p, ncomp)
load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)

for (i in 1:ncomp) {
  # Compute loadings for component i
  vx_unnorm <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x[, i])
  vy_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% scores_y[, i])
  vy_unnorm <- solve_Ay(vy_input)
  
  # Normalize under respective metrics
  norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vx_unnorm) %*% Ax %*% vx_unnorm))
  norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vy_unnorm) %*% Ay %*% vy_unnorm))
  
  load_x[, i] <- if (norm_x > 1e-10) vx_unnorm / norm_x else vx_unnorm
  load_y[, i] <- if (norm_y > 1e-10) vy_unnorm / norm_y else vy_unnorm
}
```

### Benefits
1. **Mathematically correct**: The scaling with `diag(sqrt(d_vals))` and `diag(1/sqrt(d_vals))` guarantees the cross-covariance relationship
2. **Preserves sparsity**: Uses matrix operations instead of element-wise loops
3. **Numerically stable**: No iterative adjustments or complex orthogonalization
4. **Simpler code**: Removes ~40 lines of complex normalization logic
5. **Guaranteed to satisfy all three identities**:
   - `t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx = I`
   - `t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy = I`  
   - `t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)`

### Implementation Steps
1. Remove the normalization of U_dual (lines 165-171)
2. Replace the entire section 8 with the clean scaling approach
3. Simplify section 9 to compute loadings from the correctly scaled scores
4. Delete the entire section 11 (normalize and scale) as it's no longer needed
5. Update section 10 to remove redundant score computation

  }

  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)

        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]

    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
