1. Replaced "  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores ------..." with "  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores ------..."
2. Replaced "  ## ---- 9.  loadings ---------------------------..." with "  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores -------..."
3. Replaced "  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ---------------------..." with "  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ---------------------..."

edits.new_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

edits.old_string:
## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
  }
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- as.vector(Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i, drop = FALSE]))
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }
edits.new_string:
## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x (normalized under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y (normalized under Ay)
  
  load_x <- matrix(0, p, ncomp)
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Compute loadings for component i
    vx_unnorm <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x[, i])
    vy_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% scores_y[, i])
    vy_unnorm <- solve_Ay(vy_input)
    
    # Normalize under respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vx_unnorm) %*% Ax %*% vx_unnorm))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vy_unnorm) %*% Ay %*% vy_unnorm))
    
    load_x[, i] <- if (norm_x > 1e-10) as.vector(vx_unnorm / norm_x) else as.vector(vx_unnorm)
    load_y[, i] <- if (norm_y > 1e-10) as.vector(vy_unnorm / norm_y) else as.vector(vy_unnorm)
  }

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * R_couple * K_Y * U_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2 * U_dual[,i]
  
  # First normalize the dual eigenvectors
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_u <- sqrt(sum(U_dual[, i]^2))
    if (norm_u > 1e-10) {
      U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] / norm_u
    }
  }
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform back from the metric-weighted space
  u_orig <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)  # n × k
  s_orig <- solve(My_half, S_dual)  # n × k

  ## ---- 9.  loadings ------------------------------------------------------
  # Compute loadings from the transformed vectors
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * u_orig (then normalize under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * s_orig (then normalize under Ay)
  
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% u_orig)  # p × k
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    load_y_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% s_orig[, i])
    load_y[, i] <- solve_Ay(load_y_input)
  }
  
  ## ---- 10. compute scores from loadings ---------------------------------
  # Now compute the actual scores
  # For generalized PLS: scores_x = X * Ax^{-1} * vx (not just X * vx)
  # and scores_y = Y * Ay^{-1} * vy (not just Y * vy)
  
  scores_x <- Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x)  # n × k  
  scores_y <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
  }
  
  ## ---- 11. normalize and scale ------------------------------------------
  # Normalize loadings to be Ax/Ay-orthonormal and ensure cross-block reconstruction
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Orthogonalize against previous components (Gram-Schmidt)
    if (i > 1) {
      for (j in 1:(i-1)) {
        # Project out component j from component i under Ax metric
        proj_x <- as.numeric(t(load_x[, j]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i])
        load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] - proj_x * load_x[, j]
        
        # Project out component j from component i under Ay metric
        proj_y <- as.numeric(t(load_y[, j]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i])
        load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] - proj_y * load_y[, j]
      }
    }
    
    # Normalize loadings to have unit norm under their respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_x[, i]) %*% Ax %*% load_x[, i]))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(load_y[, i]) %*% Ay %*% load_y[, i]))
    
    if (norm_x > 1e-10) load_x[, i] <- load_x[, i] / norm_x
    if (norm_y > 1e-10) load_y[, i] <- load_y[, i] / norm_y
    
    # Recompute scores with normalized loadings
    scores_x[, i] <- as.vector(Xp %*% (inv_Ax %*% load_x[, i, drop = FALSE]))
    ay_inv_vy <- solve_Ay(load_y[, i, drop = FALSE])
    scores_y[, i] <- as.vector(Yp %*% ay_inv_vy)
    
    # Scale scores to ensure t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = d[i]
    current_cov <- as.numeric(t(scores_x[, i]) %*% My %*% scores_y[, i])
    if (abs(current_cov) > 1e-10 && d_vals[i] > 1e-10) {
      scale_factor <- d_vals[i] / current_cov
      if (scale_factor > 0) {
        sqrt_scale <- sqrt(abs(scale_factor))
        scores_x[, i] <- scores_x[, i] * sqrt_scale
        scores_y[, i] <- scores_y[, i] * sqrt_scale * sign(scale_factor)
      }
    }
  }

  ## ---- 12.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text:
I see two issues:

1. **Redundant CG solver calls**: We're calling `solve_Ay` again in the loadings computation (line 187), even though we already have the scores. This is wasteful.

2. **Normalization breaking orthogonality**: The normalization in lines 189-194 is applied component-by-component without ensuring orthogonality between components.

The correct approach with the D^(1/2) scaling should automatically give us the right relationships. Let me create a plan to fix this:

input.plan:
## Plan to Fix Loadings Computation and Remove Redundancy

### Issues Identified
1. **Redundant CG solver calls**: Computing `vy_unnorm <- solve_Ay(vy_input)` when we already have scores_y
2. **Breaking orthogonality**: The per-component normalization doesn't preserve Ax/Ay-orthogonality between components
3. **Missing the mathematical relationship**: With proper dual-space scaling, loadings should emerge naturally

### Mathematical Insight
In the dual-space formulation with correct scaling:
- The scores are already computed: `scores_x` and `scores_y` 
- The loadings should satisfy: `X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * d` and `Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * d`
- This means: `vx = (1/d) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y` and `vy = (1/d) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x`

### Proposed Solution

```r
## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
# The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
# X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
# Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
#
# Therefore:
# vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
# vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x

# Compute vx directly (no normalization needed)
XtMy_sy <- t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)  # p × k
load_x <- inv_Ax %*% XtMy_sy %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals)

# Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)
YtMx_sx <- t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)  # q × k
load_y <- solve_Ay(YtMx_sx %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals))
```

### Benefits
1. **No redundant CG calls**: We compute `load_y` in one batch operation instead of k individual calls
2. **Automatic orthogonality**: The loadings computed this way are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal by construction
3. **Simpler code**: No normalization loops needed
4. **Preserves sparsity**: Matrix operations throughout
5. **Mathematically correct**: Directly implements the generalized SVD relationships

### Verification
With this approach:
- `t(load_x) %*% Ax %*% load_x = I` (by construction)
- `t(load_y) %*% Ay %*% load_y = I` (by construction)
- `t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)` (from the scaling in step 8)

All three key relationships are satisfied without any post-hoc adjustments!

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x (normalized under Ax)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y (normalized under Ay)
  
  load_x <- matrix(0, p, ncomp)
  load_y <- matrix(0, q, ncomp)
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    # Compute loadings for component i
    vx_unnorm <- inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x[, i])
    vy_input <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% scores_y[, i])
    vy_unnorm <- solve_Ay(vy_input)
    
    # Normalize under respective metrics
    norm_x <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vx_unnorm) %*% Ax %*% vx_unnorm))
    norm_y <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(vy_unnorm) %*% Ay %*% vy_unnorm))
    
    load_x[, i] <- if (norm_x > 1e-10) as.vector(vx_unnorm / norm_x) else as.vector(vx_unnorm)
    load_y[, i] <- if (norm_y > 1e-10) as.vector(vy_unnorm / norm_y) else as.vector(vy_unnorm)
  }

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

lines: -  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x (normalized under Ax)
-  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y (normalized under Ay)
+  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
+  # X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
+  # Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
+  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
+  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x
-    load_x[, i] <- if (norm_x > 1e-10) as.vector(vx_unnorm / norm_x) else as.vector(vx_unnorm)
-    load_y[, i] <- if (norm_y > 1e-10) as.vector(vy_unnorm / norm_y) else as.vector(vy_unnorm)
+  # Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)

content.text:
I see the issue! The loadings are not orthonormal - they have norm around 0.577 instead of 1. Also, the cross-covariance diagonal values are wrong. It looks like the relationship between scores and loadings is not correctly implemented.

Looking at the output:
- The singular values are correct (11.73, 5.86)
- But the cross-covariance diagonal shows (6.77, 3.38) - roughly 0.577 times the expected values
- The loadings have norm 0.577 instead of 1

This suggests that the formula for computing loadings from scores might be incorrect. Let me think about this more carefully. In the generalized PLS formulation, we need to ensure that the loadings computed from the scores satisfy the correct relationships.

Let me write a more detailed debug script to understand what's happening:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # Keep as is
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
  # Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
  #
  # Therefore:
  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x
  
  # Compute vx directly (no normalization needed)
  XtMy_sy <- t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)  # p × k
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% XtMy_sy %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals)
  
  # Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)
  YtMx_sx <- t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)  # q × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(YtMx_sx %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals))

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       b = b,       # lowercase b
                       x0 = if (is.null(prev_u)) NULL else prev_u,
                       M = Mprec,   # preconditioner matrix
                       tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
  # Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
  #
  # Therefore:
  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x
  
  # Compute vx directly (no normalization needed)
  XtMy_sy <- t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)  # p × k
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% XtMy_sy %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals)
  
  # Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)
  YtMx_sx <- t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)  # q × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(YtMx_sx %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals))

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)

#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       b = b,       # lowercase b
                       x0 = if (is.null(prev_u)) NULL else prev_u,
                       M = Mprec,   # preconditioner matrix
                       tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
  # Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
  #
  # Therefore:
  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x
  
  # Compute vx directly (no normalization needed)
  XtMx_sx <- t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)  # p × k
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% XtMx_sx %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals)
  
  # Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)
  YtMy_sy <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)  # q × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(YtMy_sy %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals))

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

lines: # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
-  # X^T * My * scores_y = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
-  # Y^T * Mx * scores_x = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
+  # X^T * Mx * scores_x = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
+  # Y^T * My * scores_y = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
-  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y
-  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x
+  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x
+  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       b = b,       # lowercase b
                       x0 = if (is.null(prev_u)) NULL else prev_u,
                       M = Mprec,   # preconditioner matrix
                       tol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy the eigenvalue problem
  # We need to scale them correctly to get the primal scores
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores with correct scaling
  # This ensures t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(d_vals))
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual) %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/sqrt(d_vals))

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * Mx * scores_x = Ax * vx * diag(d_vals)
  # Y^T * My * scores_y = Ay * vy * diag(d_vals)
  #
  # Therefore:
  # vx = diag(1/d_vals) * Ax^{-1} * X^T * Mx * scores_x
  # vy = diag(1/d_vals) * Ay^{-1} * Y^T * My * scores_y
  
  # Compute vx directly (no normalization needed)
  XtMx_sx <- t(Xp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x)  # p × k
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% XtMx_sx %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals)
  
  # Compute vy using the CG solver (batch all columns at once)
  YtMy_sy <- t(Yp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)  # q × k
  load_y <- solve_Ay(YtMy_sy %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1/d_vals))

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text:
We're still failing the orthogonality and cross-block reconstruction tests. Let me check what the actual mathematical relationships should be. I think the issue might be that the relationship between scores and loadings isn't what I implemented.

Let me debug with the simple identity case to understand what's happening:

content.text:
The loadings are not orthonormal and the scores don't match `X %*% vx`. This suggests that the formulas I'm using are incorrect for the loadings computation.

Let me think about this more carefully. In generalized PLS:
- We have the eigenvalue problem in the dual space
- The eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual are in the transformed space
- We need to recover the loadings vx and vy that satisfy the generalized SVD relationships

Looking at Beaton's formulation, after getting the dual space eigenvectors, the correct approach might be different. Let me check if we need to compute the loadings differently.

input.content:
# Check the mathematical relationships in genplsc

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

# Simple test case
set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Identity metrics
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

# Extract components
Sx <- fit$scores_x
Sy <- fit$scores_y
Vx <- fit$vx
Vy <- fit$vy
d <- fit$d

# For identity metrics, the relationships should be:
# 1. X^T * Y * vy = vx * d
# 2. Y^T * X * vx = vy * d
# 3. sx = X * vx
# 4. sy = Y * vy
# 5. t(sx) * sy = diag(d)
# 6. t(vx) * vx = I
# 7. t(vy) * vy = I

cat("=== Checking generalized PLS relationships (identity metrics) ===\n\n")

# Check 1: X^T * Y * vy = vx * d
cat("1. X^T * Y * vy = vx * d:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- t(X) %*% Y %*% Vy[, i]
  rhs <- Vx[, i] * d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": relative error =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# Check 2: Y^T * X * vx = vy * d
cat("\n2. Y^T * X * vx = vy * d:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- t(Y) %*% X %*% Vx[, i]
  rhs <- Vy[, i] * d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": relative error =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# Check relationships 3-7
cat("\n3. sx = X * vx: max diff =", max(abs(Sx - X %*% Vx)), "\n")
cat("4. sy = Y * vy: max diff =", max(abs(Sy - Y %*% Vy)), "\n")
cat("5. t(sx) * sy = diag(d): max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Sx) %*% Sy - diag(d))), "\n")
cat("6. t(vx) * vx = I: max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Vx) %*% Vx - diag(2))), "\n")
cat("7. t(vy) * vy = I: max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Vy) %*% Vy - diag(2))), "\n")

# What if we compute loadings from the primal scores differently?
cat("\n=== Alternative loading computation ===\n")
# If sx and sy are given with the correct cross-covariance,
# then vx and vy should satisfy:
# vx = (1/d) * X^T * sy  (normalized)
# vy = (1/d) * Y^T * sx  (normalized)

vx_alt <- matrix(0, p, 2)
vy_alt <- matrix(0, q, 2)
for (i in 1:2) {
  vx_alt[, i] <- t(X) %*% Sy[, i] / d[i]
  vx_alt[, i] <- vx_alt[, i] / sqrt(sum(vx_alt[, i]^2))
  
  vy_alt[, i] <- t(Y) %*% Sx[, i] / d[i]
  vy_alt[, i] <- vy_alt[, i] / sqrt(sum(vy_alt[, i]^2))
}

cat("\nAlternative loadings orthogonality:\n")
cat("t(vx_alt) * vx_alt:\n")
print(round(t(vx_alt) %*% vx_alt, 6))
cat("\nt(vy_alt) * vy_alt:\n")
print(round(t(vy_alt) %*% vy_alt, 6))

# Check the mathematical relationships in genplsc

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

# Simple test case
set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Identity metrics
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

# Extract components
Sx <- fit$scores_x
Sy <- fit$scores_y
Vx <- fit$vx
Vy <- fit$vy
d <- fit$d

# For identity metrics, the relationships should be:
# 1. X^T * Y * vy = vx * d
# 2. Y^T * X * vx = vy * d
# 3. sx = X * vx
# 4. sy = Y * vy
# 5. t(sx) * sy = diag(d)
# 6. t(vx) * vx = I
# 7. t(vy) * vy = I

cat("=== Checking generalized PLS relationships (identity metrics) ===\n\n")

# Check 1: X^T * Y * vy = vx * d
cat("1. X^T * Y * vy = vx * d:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- t(X) %*% Y %*% Vy[, i]
  rhs <- Vx[, i] * d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": relative error =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# Check 2: Y^T * X * vx = vy * d
cat("\n2. Y^T * X * vx = vy * d:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- t(Y) %*% X %*% Vx[, i]
  rhs <- Vy[, i] * d[i]
  rel_error <- max(abs(lhs - rhs)) / max(abs(rhs))
  cat("   Component", i, ": relative error =", rel_error, "\n")
}

# Check relationships 3-7
cat("\n3. sx = X * vx: max diff =", max(abs(Sx - X %*% Vx)), "\n")
cat("4. sy = Y * vy: max diff =", max(abs(Sy - Y %*% Vy)), "\n")
cat("5. t(sx) * sy = diag(d): max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Sx) %*% Sy - diag(d))), "\n")
cat("6. t(vx) * vx = I: max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Vx) %*% Vx - diag(2))), "\n")
cat("7. t(vy) * vy = I: max off-diag =", max(abs(t(Vy) %*% Vy - diag(2))), "\n")

# What if we compute loadings from the primal scores differently?
cat("\n=== Alternative loading computation ===\n")
# If sx and sy are given with the correct cross-covariance,
# then vx and vy should satisfy:
# vx = (1/d) * X^T * sy  (normalized)
# vy = (1/d) * Y^T * sx  (normalized)

vx_alt <- matrix(0, p, 2)
vy_alt <- matrix(0, q, 2)
for (i in 1:2) {
  vx_alt[, i] <- t(X) %*% Sy[, i] / d[i]
  vx_alt[, i] <- vx_alt[, i] / sqrt(sum(vx_alt[, i]^2))
  
  vy_alt[, i] <- t(Y) %*% Sx[, i] / d[i]
  vy_alt[, i] <- vy_alt[, i] / sqrt(sum(vy_alt[, i]^2))
}

cat("\nAlternative loadings orthogonality:\n")
cat("t(vx_alt) * vx_alt:\n")
print(round(t(vx_alt) %*% vx_alt, 6))
cat("\nt(vy_alt) * vy_alt:\n")
print(round(t(vy_alt) %*% vy_alt, 6))

I see! The relationships are not satisfied with the current implementation. The alternative computation at the end shows that for the identity metric case, the loadings should be:
- `vx = (1/d) * X^T * sy` (normalized)
- `vy = (1/d) * Y^T * sx` (normalized)

This is the cross-loading relationship, not the same-side relationship I implemented. Let me fix this:
