toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # uppercase B
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # Extract singular values and vectors from augmented system
  # Eigenvalues come in pairs ±d, we take the positive ones
  d_vals <- Re(eig$values)
  
  # Extract left and right singular vectors from eigenvectors
  # For eigenvalue +d, eigenvector is [u; v] where u and v are the left and right vectors
  U_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp) 
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ev <- Re(eig$vectors[, i])
    U_dual[, i] <- ev[1:n]          # Left singular vectors
    S_dual[, i] <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]  # Right singular vectors
    
    # Normalize so that U^T * S = d
    inner_prod <- sum(U_dual[, i] * S_dual[, i])
    if (inner_prod < 0) {
      # Flip sign if needed
      S_dual[, i] <- -S_dual[, i]
      inner_prod <- -inner_prod
    }
    # Scale to get correct normalization
    scale <- sqrt(d_vals[i] / inner_prod)
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * scale
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The eigenvectors from RSpectra have unit L2 norm: ||U_dual||_2 = 1
  # But for generalized PLS, we need: t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(lambda)
  # Currently we have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] * ||U_dual[,i]||^2 = lambda[i]
  # So our eigenvectors already have the correct relationship!
  
  # However, the primal scores need different scaling to satisfy:
  # t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals) where d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # First normalize U_dual and S_dual by sqrt(sqrt(lambda)) each
  # This will give us t(U_norm) * S_norm = diag(sqrt(lambda)) = diag(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scale_factor <- (d_vals[i])^(1/4)  # Fourth root of eigenvalue
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / scale_factor
  }
  
  # Now transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
  # Y^T * Mx * X * vx = Ay * vy * d
  #
  # Given scores with t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals),
  # the loadings are:
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y * diag(1/d_vals)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x * diag(1/d_vals)
  
  # The loadings are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal when computed from 
  # the properly scaled scores
  # Check for zero singular values to avoid division by zero
  if (any(d_vals < 1e-10)) {
    warning("Near-zero singular values detected. Using threshold 1e-10.")
    d_vals[d_vals < 1e-10] <- 1e-10
  }
  Dinv <- Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1 / d_vals)
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% (t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)) %*% Dinv   # p × k
  
  # Batch CG solve for all k columns at once
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x) %*% Dinv)      # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

# Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The eigenvectors from RSpectra have unit L2 norm: ||U_dual||_2 = 1
  # But for generalized PLS, we need: t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(lambda)
  # Currently we have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] * ||U_dual[,i]||^2 = lambda[i]
  # So our eigenvectors already have the correct relationship!
  
  # However, the primal scores need different scaling to satisfy:
  # t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals) where d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # First normalize U_dual and S_dual by sqrt(sqrt(lambda)) each
  # This will give us t(U_norm) * S_norm = diag(sqrt(lambda)) = diag(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scale_factor <- (d_vals[i])^(1/4)  # Fourth root of eigenvalue
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / scale_factor
  }
  
  # Now transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
  # Y^T * Mx * X * vx = Ay * vy * d
  #
  # Given scores with t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals),
  # the loadings are:
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y * diag(1/d_vals)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x * diag(1/d_vals)
  
  # The loadings are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal when computed from 
  # the properly scaled scores
  # Check for zero singular values to avoid division by zero
  if (any(d_vals < 1e-10)) {
    warning("Near-zero singular values detected. Using threshold 1e-10.")
    d_vals[d_vals < 1e-10] <- 1e-10
  }
  Dinv <- Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1 / d_vals)
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% (t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)) %*% Dinv   # p × k
  
  # Batch CG solve for all k columns at once
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x) %*% Dinv)      # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # uppercase B
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  
  # Use augmented symmetric formulation for SVD
  # Define H([u; v]) = [0 A^T; A 0][u; v] = [A^T v; A u]
  # where A is the operator K_X * R_couple * K_Y
  # This gives a symmetric 2n x 2n problem
  
  A_op_forward <- function(v) {
    # A * v = K_X * R_couple * K_Y * v
    as.numeric(K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v)))
  }
  
  A_op_transpose <- function(u) {
    # A^T * u = K_Y^T * R_couple^T * K_X^T * u
    # Since K_X, K_Y, R_couple are symmetric: A^T * u = K_Y * R_couple * K_X * u
    # First apply K_X
    w1 <- K_X %*% u
    # Then R_couple (symmetric)
    w2 <- R_couple %*% w1
    # Finally K_Y
    as.numeric(KY_fun(w2))
  }
  
  # Augmented symmetric operator
  H_op <- function(w, ...) {
    # w is a 2n vector [u; v]
    u <- w[1:n]
    v <- w[(n+1):(2*n)]
    
    # H([u; v]) = [A^T v; A u]
    top <- A_op_transpose(v)
    bottom <- A_op_forward(u)
    
    c(top, bottom)
  }
  
  # Solve for k largest eigenvalues of symmetric H
  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(H_op, k = ncomp, n = 2*n, which = "LM", ...)
  
  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # Extract singular values and vectors from augmented system
  # Eigenvalues come in pairs ±d, we take the positive ones
  d_vals <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  
  # Extract left and right singular vectors from eigenvectors
  # For eigenvalue +d, eigenvector is [u; v] where u and v are the left and right vectors
  U_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  V_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp) 
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ev <- Re(eig$vectors[, i])
    U_dual[, i] <- ev[1:n]          # Left singular vectors  
    V_dual[, i] <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]  # Right singular vectors
    
    # Normalize so that U^T * V = 1 (bi-orthogonal)
    inner_prod <- sum(U_dual[, i] * V_dual[, i])
    if (abs(inner_prod) < 1e-10) {
      warning("Near-zero inner product in singular vector normalization")
      inner_prod <- 1e-10
    }
    if (inner_prod < 0) {
      # Flip sign if needed
      V_dual[, i] <- -V_dual[, i]
      inner_prod <- -inner_prod
    }
    # Scale to get bi-orthogonal vectors
    scale <- 1 / sqrt(inner_prod)
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale
    V_dual[, i] <- V_dual[, i] * scale
  }
  
  # For dual space, U_dual are in the K_X space and V_dual in the K_Y space
  # We need S_dual = K_Y * U_dual for the cross-block relationship
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Scale to get correct cross-covariance
  # We want t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  # Currently t(U_dual) * S_dual should equal diag(d_vals^2) due to eigenvalue relation
  # So we scale by 1/sqrt(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * sqrt(d_vals[i])
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / sqrt(d_vals[i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores  
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy:
  # K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The eigenvectors from RSpectra have unit L2 norm: ||U_dual||_2 = 1
  # But for generalized PLS, we need: t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(lambda)
  # Currently we have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] * ||U_dual[,i]||^2 = lambda[i]
  # So our eigenvectors already have the correct relationship!
  
  # However, the primal scores need different scaling to satisfy:
  # t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals) where d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # First normalize U_dual and S_dual by sqrt(sqrt(lambda)) each
  # This will give us t(U_norm) * S_norm = diag(sqrt(lambda)) = diag(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scale_factor <- (d_vals[i])^(1/4)  # Fourth root of eigenvalue
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / scale_factor
  }
  
  # Now transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
  # Y^T * Mx * X * vx = Ay * vy * d
  #
  # Given scores with t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals),
  # the loadings are:
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y * diag(1/d_vals)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x * diag(1/d_vals)
  
  # The loadings are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal when computed from 
  # the properly scaled scores
  # Check for zero singular values to avoid division by zero
  if (any(d_vals < 1e-10)) {
    warning("Near-zero singular values detected. Using threshold 1e-10.")
    d_vals[d_vals < 1e-10] <- 1e-10
  }
  Dinv <- Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1 / d_vals)
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% (t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)) %*% Dinv   # p × k
  
  # Batch CG solve for all k columns at once
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x) %*% Dinv)      # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

-  # The dual-space eigenvectors U_dual and S_dual = K_Y * U_dual satisfy:
-  # The eigenvectors from RSpectra have unit L2 norm: ||U_dual||_2 = 1
-  # But for generalized PLS, we need: t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(lambda)
-  # Currently we have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] * ||U_dual[,i]||^2 = lambda[i]

-  # However, the primal scores need different scaling to satisfy:
-  # t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals) where d_vals = sqrt(lambda)

-  # This will give us t(U_norm) * S_norm = diag(sqrt(lambda)) = diag(d_vals)

content.text: We're getting NaN values, which suggests the augmented system is not working correctly. The singular values are also completely wrong. Let me debug this:

input.content:
# Debug the augmented symmetric operator

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Direct SVD for comparison
svd_xy <- svd(t(X) %*% Y)
cat("Direct SVD singular values:", svd_xy$d[1:2], "\n\n")

# Build operators manually
Xp <- Matrix(X, sparse = FALSE)
Yp <- Matrix(Y, sparse = FALSE)
K_X <- Xp %*% t(Xp)
K_Y <- Yp %*% t(Yp)

# Test the augmented formulation
# For identity metrics, A = K_X * K_Y
A <- K_X %*% K_Y

# Build augmented matrix H = [0 A^T; A 0]
H <- rbind(cbind(matrix(0, n, n), t(A)),
           cbind(A, matrix(0, n, n)))

# Get eigenvalues of H
eig_H <- eigen(H)
cat("Eigenvalues of augmented matrix H:\n")
print(eig_H$values[1:10])

# The eigenvalues should come in pairs ±d
# where d are the singular values of the cross-covariance
cat("\nPositive eigenvalues (should match SVD):\n")
pos_eigs <- eig_H$values[eig_H$values > 1e-10]
print(sqrt(pos_eigs[1:2]))

# Check eigenvectors
cat("\nChecking eigenvector structure for first positive eigenvalue:\n")
idx <- which.max(eig_H$values)
ev <- eig_H$vectors[, idx]
u <- ev[1:n]
v <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]

# Check if H * [u; v] = lambda * [u; v]
Hev <- H %*% ev
lambda_check <- eig_H$values[idx]
cat("||H*ev - lambda*ev|| =", norm(Hev - lambda_check * ev, "2"), "\n")

# Check if A*u = lambda*v and A^T*v = lambda*u
Au <- A %*% u
ATv <- t(A) %*% v

# Debug the augmented symmetric operator

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Direct SVD for comparison
svd_xy <- svd(t(X) %*% Y)
cat("Direct SVD singular values:", svd_xy$d[1:2], "\n\n")

# Build operators manually
Xp <- Matrix(X, sparse = FALSE)
Yp <- Matrix(Y, sparse = FALSE)
K_X <- Xp %*% t(Xp)
K_Y <- Yp %*% t(Yp)

# Test the augmented formulation
# For identity metrics, A = K_X * K_Y
A <- K_X %*% K_Y

# Build augmented matrix H = [0 A^T; A 0]
H <- rbind(cbind(matrix(0, n, n), t(A)),
           cbind(A, matrix(0, n, n)))

# Get eigenvalues of H
eig_H <- eigen(H)
cat("Eigenvalues of augmented matrix H:\n")
print(eig_H$values[1:10])

# The eigenvalues should come in pairs ±d
# where d are the singular values of the cross-covariance
cat("\nPositive eigenvalues (should match SVD):\n")
pos_eigs <- eig_H$values[eig_H$values > 1e-10]
print(sqrt(pos_eigs[1:2]))

# Check eigenvectors
cat("\nChecking eigenvector structure for first positive eigenvalue:\n")
idx <- which.max(eig_H$values)
ev <- eig_H$vectors[, idx]
u <- ev[1:n]
v <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]

# Check if H * [u; v] = lambda * [u; v]
Hev <- H %*% ev
lambda_check <- eig_H$values[idx]
cat("||H*ev - lambda*ev|| =", norm(Hev - lambda_check * ev, "2"), "\n")

# Check if A*u = lambda*v and A^T*v = lambda*u
Au <- A %*% u
ATv <- t(A) %*% v
cat("||A*u - lambda*v|| =", norm(Au - lambda_check * v, "2"), "\n")
cat("||A^T*v - lambda*u|| =", norm(ATv - lambda_check * u, "2"), "\n")

content.text:
I see the issue! The eigenvalues of the augmented matrix H are NOT the singular values directly. They are 114.58, 62.27, 11.84, 2.10... and their square roots are 10.70, 7.89... which don't match our expected singular values 8.12, 6.42.

The problem is that for the augmented matrix formulation to give us the SVD of t(X) %*% Y, we need to use the operator that corresponds to that matrix, not K_X * K_Y. Let me reconsider the approach.

Actually, looking back at the feedback, they mentioned that the eigenvalues of K_X * K_Y are the squared singular values of the cross-covariance. So √65.975 ≈ 8.123 and √41.176 ≈ 6.417, which matches!

So our original non-symmetric formulation was actually correct for the singular values. The issue is just with the eigenvector orthogonality. Let me go back to the simpler approach and use the suggestion about using left and right eigenvectors:

edits.old_string:
## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  
  # Use augmented symmetric formulation for SVD
  # Define H([u; v]) = [0 A^T; A 0][u; v] = [A^T v; A u]
  # where A is the operator K_X * R_couple * K_Y
  # This gives a symmetric 2n x 2n problem
  
  A_op_forward <- function(v) {
    # A * v = K_X * R_couple * K_Y * v
    as.numeric(K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v)))
  }
  
  A_op_transpose <- function(u) {
    # A^T * u = K_Y^T * R_couple^T * K_X^T * u
    # Since K_X, K_Y, R_couple are symmetric: A^T * u = K_Y * R_couple * K_X * u
    # First apply K_X
    w1 <- K_X %*% u
    # Then R_couple (symmetric)
    w2 <- R_couple %*% w1
    # Finally K_Y
    as.numeric(KY_fun(w2))
  }
  
  # Augmented symmetric operator
  H_op <- function(w, ...) {
    # w is a 2n vector [u; v]
    u <- w[1:n]
    v <- w[(n+1):(2*n)]
    
    # H([u; v]) = [A^T v; A u]
    top <- A_op_transpose(v)
    bottom <- A_op_forward(u)
    
    c(top, bottom)
  }
  
  # Solve for k largest eigenvalues of symmetric H
  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(H_op, k = ncomp, n = 2*n, which = "LM", ...)
  
  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # Extract singular values and vectors from augmented system
  # Eigenvalues come in pairs ±d, we take the positive ones
  d_vals <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  
  # Extract left and right singular vectors from eigenvectors
  # For eigenvalue +d, eigenvector is [u; v] where u and v are the left and right vectors
  U_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  V_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp) 
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ev <- Re(eig$vectors[, i])
    U_dual[, i] <- ev[1:n]          # Left singular vectors  
    V_dual[, i] <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]  # Right singular vectors
    
    # Normalize so that U^T * V = 1 (bi-orthogonal)
    inner_prod <- sum(U_dual[, i] * V_dual[, i])
    if (abs(inner_prod) < 1e-10) {
      warning("Near-zero inner product in singular vector normalization")
      inner_prod <- 1e-10
    }
    if (inner_prod < 0) {
      # Flip sign if needed
      V_dual[, i] <- -V_dual[, i]
      inner_prod <- -inner_prod
    }
    # Scale to get bi-orthogonal vectors
    scale <- 1 / sqrt(inner_prod)
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale
    V_dual[, i] <- V_dual[, i] * scale
  }
  
  # For dual space, U_dual are in the K_X space and V_dual in the K_Y space
  # We need S_dual = K_Y * U_dual for the cross-block relationship
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Scale to get correct cross-covariance
  # We want t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  # Currently t(U_dual) * S_dual should equal diag(d_vals^2) due to eigenvalue relation
  # So we scale by 1/sqrt(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * sqrt(d_vals[i])
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / sqrt(d_vals[i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores  
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)
edits.new_string:
## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors satisfy: K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues and d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The key insight from the feedback: for non-symmetric operators,
  # RSpectra returns unit-norm eigenvectors that are not bi-orthogonal.
  # We have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] (eigenvalue)
  # But we need: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  
  # Normalize to get the correct cross-block relationship
  # Currently: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2
  # We want: t(U_norm[,i]) * S_norm[,i] = d_vals[i]
  # So scale each by 1/sqrt(d_vals[i])
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_factor <- 1 / sqrt(d_vals[i])
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * norm_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * norm_factor
  }
  
  # Now t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(d_vals)
  # Transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # uppercase B
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  
  # Use augmented symmetric formulation for SVD
  # Define H([u; v]) = [0 A^T; A 0][u; v] = [A^T v; A u]
  # where A is the operator K_X * R_couple * K_Y
  # This gives a symmetric 2n x 2n problem
  
  A_op_forward <- function(v) {
    # A * v = K_X * R_couple * K_Y * v
    as.numeric(K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v)))
  }
  
  A_op_transpose <- function(u) {
    # A^T * u = K_Y^T * R_couple^T * K_X^T * u
    # Since K_X, K_Y, R_couple are symmetric: A^T * u = K_Y * R_couple * K_X * u
    # First apply K_X
    w1 <- K_X %*% u
    # Then R_couple (symmetric)
    w2 <- R_couple %*% w1
    # Finally K_Y
    as.numeric(KY_fun(w2))
  }
  
  # Augmented symmetric operator
  H_op <- function(w, ...) {
    # w is a 2n vector [u; v]
    u <- w[1:n]
    v <- w[(n+1):(2*n)]
    
    # H([u; v]) = [A^T v; A u]
    top <- A_op_transpose(v)
    bottom <- A_op_forward(u)
    
    c(top, bottom)
  }
  
  # Solve for k largest eigenvalues of symmetric H
  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(H_op, k = ncomp, n = 2*n, which = "LM", ...)
  
  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # Extract singular values and vectors from augmented system
  # Eigenvalues come in pairs ±d, we take the positive ones
  d_vals <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  
  # Extract left and right singular vectors from eigenvectors
  # For eigenvalue +d, eigenvector is [u; v] where u and v are the left and right vectors
  U_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  V_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp) 
  
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    ev <- Re(eig$vectors[, i])
    U_dual[, i] <- ev[1:n]          # Left singular vectors  
    V_dual[, i] <- ev[(n+1):(2*n)]  # Right singular vectors
    
    # Normalize so that U^T * V = 1 (bi-orthogonal)
    inner_prod <- sum(U_dual[, i] * V_dual[, i])
    if (abs(inner_prod) < 1e-10) {
      warning("Near-zero inner product in singular vector normalization")
      inner_prod <- 1e-10
    }
    if (inner_prod < 0) {
      # Flip sign if needed
      V_dual[, i] <- -V_dual[, i]
      inner_prod <- -inner_prod
    }
    # Scale to get bi-orthogonal vectors
    scale <- 1 / sqrt(inner_prod)
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale
    V_dual[, i] <- V_dual[, i] * scale
  }
  
  # For dual space, U_dual are in the K_X space and V_dual in the K_Y space
  # We need S_dual = K_Y * U_dual for the cross-block relationship
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Scale to get correct cross-covariance
  # We want t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  # Currently t(U_dual) * S_dual should equal diag(d_vals^2) due to eigenvalue relation
  # So we scale by 1/sqrt(d_vals)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * sqrt(d_vals[i])
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] / sqrt(d_vals[i])
  }
  
  # Transform to primal scores  
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)


  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
  # Y^T * Mx * X * vx = Ay * vy * d
  #
  # Given scores with t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals),
  # the loadings are:
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y * diag(1/d_vals)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x * diag(1/d_vals)
  
  # The loadings are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal when computed from 
  # the properly scaled scores
  # Check for zero singular values to avoid division by zero
  if (any(d_vals < 1e-10)) {
    warning("Near-zero singular values detected. Using threshold 1e-10.")
    d_vals[d_vals < 1e-10] <- 1e-10
  }
  Dinv <- Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1 / d_vals)
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% (t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)) %*% Dinv   # p × k
  
  # Batch CG solve for all k columns at once
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x) %*% Dinv)      # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

content.text: Progress! We're back to having the correct singular values (tests 1 and 2 pass), but still failing orthogonality and cross-block reconstruction. Let me debug what's happening:

input.content:
# Debug the normalization issue

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 20; p <- 10; q <- 12
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Add some structure to make it more interesting
X[,1] <- X[,1] + 2 * Y[,1]
Y[,2] <- Y[,2] + 1.5 * X[,2]

# Create SPD metrics
Mx <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n)) + diag(n)
My <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n)) + diag(n)
Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p * p), p, p)) + diag(p)
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q * q), q, q)) + diag(q)

# Run genplsc
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx = Mx, Ax = Ax, My = My, Ay = Ay, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

cat("Singular values from genplsc:", fit$d, "\n\n")

# Check cross-block reconstruction
Sx <- fit$scores_x
Sy <- fit$scores_y
cov_xy <- t(Sx) %*% My %*% Sy
cat("Cross-block covariance:\n")
print(round(cov_xy, 6))
cat("\nExpected diagonal:", fit$d, "\n")
cat("Actual diagonal:", diag(cov_xy), "\n")
cat("Max off-diagonal:", max(abs(cov_xy[row(cov_xy) != col(cov_xy)])), "\n\n")

# Check Omega-orthogonality
Omega_x <- t(fit$vx) %*% Ax %*% fit$vx
Omega_y <- t(fit$vy) %*% Ay %*% fit$vy

cat("Ax-orthogonality (should be identity):\n")
print(round(Omega_x, 6))

cat("\nAy-orthogonality (should be identity):\n")
print(round(Omega_y, 6))

# Let's trace through what's happening
# The issue is that for non-identity metrics, the dual-space eigenvectors
# don't directly give us the correct normalization

# What we have: K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
# where K_X and K_Y incorporate the metrics

# The loadings are computed as:
# vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y / d
# vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x / d

# For these to be Omega-orthonormal, we need:
# t(vx) * Ax * vx = I
# t(vy) * Ay * vy = I

# Let's check the intermediate calculations
cat("\n\nChecking intermediate steps:\n")
cat("Shape of scores_x:", dim(Sx), "\n")
cat("Shape of scores_y:", dim(Sy), "\n")

# The cross-covariance should satisfy:
# t(Sx) * My * Sy = diag(d)
# This is what's failing

# Let's manually compute what the scores should be
# to satisfy this relationship

content.text:
The issue is clear now. The cross-covariance diagonal is [0.0849, 0.1029] but should be [1487.713, 890.830]. The loadings Omega-orthogonality is also completely wrong (near zero instead of identity).

This suggests our scaling is way off. Looking at the feedback again, they mentioned that the eigenvalues of K_X * K_Y are the squared singular values. So our d_vals = sqrt(eigenvalues) is correct. But our normalization of U_dual and S_dual is wrong.

Let me trace through the math more carefully:

input.content:
# Trace through the dual-space mathematics carefully

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# For simplicity, use identity metrics first
Mx <- Diagonal(n)
My <- Diagonal(n)
Ax <- Diagonal(p)
Ay <- Diagonal(q)

# Build operators
K_X <- X %*% t(X)  # n x n
K_Y <- Y %*% t(Y)  # n x n
A_op <- K_X %*% K_Y

# Get eigenvalues/vectors
eig <- eigen(A_op)
lambda <- eig$values[1:2]
U_dual_raw <- eig$vectors[, 1:2]

cat("Eigenvalues (lambda):", lambda, "\n")
cat("Singular values (d = sqrt(lambda)):", sqrt(lambda), "\n\n")

# Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
S_dual_raw <- K_Y %*% U_dual_raw

# Check the eigenvalue equation: K_X * K_Y * u = lambda * u
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- A_op %*% U_dual_raw[, i]
  rhs <- lambda[i] * U_dual_raw[, i]
  cat("Component", i, ": ||A*u - lambda*u|| =", norm(lhs - rhs, "2"), "\n")
}

# Check inner products
cat("\nInner products t(U) * S:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  ip <- t(U_dual_raw[, i]) %*% S_dual_raw[, i]
  cat("Component", i, ": t(U) * S =", ip, "= lambda\n")
}

# So we have: t(U_dual_raw) * S_dual_raw = diag(lambda)
# But we want: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d) where d = sqrt(lambda)

# Since My = I in this case, we need:
# t(scores_x) * scores_y = diag(sqrt(lambda))

# If scores_x = alpha * U_dual and scores_y = beta * S_dual, then:
# t(scores_x) * scores_y = alpha * beta * t(U_dual) * S_dual = alpha * beta * lambda

# We want: alpha * beta * lambda = sqrt(lambda)
# So: alpha * beta = 1/sqrt(lambda)

# A natural choice is: alpha = beta = 1/lambda^(1/4)

cat("\n\nApplying scaling:\n")
scores_x <- U_dual_raw %*% diag(1/lambda^(1/4))
scores_y <- S_dual_raw %*% diag(1/lambda^(1/4))

# Check cross-covariance
cross_cov <- t(scores_x) %*% scores_y
cat("Cross-covariance diagonal:", diag(cross_cov), "\n")
cat("Expected (d = sqrt(lambda)):", sqrt(lambda), "\n")

# Now compute loadings
# vx = X^T * scores_y / d
# vy = Y^T * scores_x / d
load_x <- t(X) %*% scores_y %*% diag(1/sqrt(lambda))
load_y <- t(Y) %*% scores_x %*% diag(1/sqrt(lambda))

# Check orthogonality
cat("\nOrthogonality t(vx) * vx:\n")
print(round(t(load_x) %*% load_x, 6))

cat("\nOrthogonality t(vy) * vy:\n")
print(round(t(load_y) %*% load_y, 6))

# Trace through the dual-space mathematics carefully

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# For simplicity, use identity metrics first
Mx <- Diagonal(n)
My <- Diagonal(n)
Ax <- Diagonal(p)
Ay <- Diagonal(q)

# Build operators
K_X <- X %*% t(X)  # n x n
K_Y <- Y %*% t(Y)  # n x n
A_op <- K_X %*% K_Y

# Get eigenvalues/vectors
eig <- eigen(A_op)
lambda <- eig$values[1:2]
U_dual_raw <- eig$vectors[, 1:2]

cat("Eigenvalues (lambda):", lambda, "\n")
cat("Singular values (d = sqrt(lambda)):", sqrt(lambda), "\n\n")

# Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
S_dual_raw <- K_Y %*% U_dual_raw

# Check the eigenvalue equation: K_X * K_Y * u = lambda * u
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- A_op %*% U_dual_raw[, i]
  rhs <- lambda[i] * U_dual_raw[, i]
  cat("Component", i, ": ||A*u - lambda*u|| =", norm(lhs - rhs, "2"), "\n")
}

# Check inner products
cat("\nInner products t(U) * S:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  ip <- t(U_dual_raw[, i]) %*% S_dual_raw[, i]
  cat("Component", i, ": t(U) * S =", ip, "= lambda\n")
}

# So we have: t(U_dual_raw) * S_dual_raw = diag(lambda)
# But we want: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d) where d = sqrt(lambda)

# Since My = I in this case, we need:
# t(scores_x) * scores_y = diag(sqrt(lambda))

# If scores_x = alpha * U_dual and scores_y = beta * S_dual, then:
# t(scores_x) * scores_y = alpha * beta * t(U_dual) * S_dual = alpha * beta * lambda

# We want: alpha * beta * lambda = sqrt(lambda)
# So: alpha * beta = 1/sqrt(lambda)

# A natural choice is: alpha = beta = 1/lambda^(1/4)

cat("\n\nApplying scaling:\n")
scores_x <- U_dual_raw %*% diag(1/lambda^(1/4))
scores_y <- S_dual_raw %*% diag(1/lambda^(1/4))

# Check cross-covariance
cross_cov <- t(scores_x) %*% scores_y
cat("Cross-covariance diagonal:", diag(cross_cov), "\n")
cat("Expected (d = sqrt(lambda)):", sqrt(lambda), "\n")

# Now compute loadings
# vx = X^T * scores_y / d
# vy = Y^T * scores_x / d
load_x <- t(X) %*% scores_y %*% diag(1/sqrt(lambda))
load_y <- t(Y) %*% scores_x %*% diag(1/sqrt(lambda))

# Check orthogonality
cat("\nOrthogonality t(vx) * vx:\n")
print(round(t(load_x) %*% load_x, 6))

cat("\nOrthogonality t(vy) * vy:\n")
print(round(t(load_y) %*% load_y, 6))

Interesting! The inner products are NOT equal to lambda as I expected. They're much smaller:
- Component 1: t(U) * S = 3.33 (but lambda = 65.98)
- Component 2: t(U) * S = 4.02 (but lambda = 41.18)

This suggests the eigenvectors from eigen() are not normalized the way I thought. Let me check their norms:

input.content:
# Check eigenvector normalization

library(Matrix)

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

K_X <- X %*% t(X)
K_Y <- Y %*% t(Y)
A_op <- K_X %*% K_Y

# Get eigenvalues/vectors
eig <- eigen(A_op)
lambda <- eig$values[1:2]
U_dual <- eig$vectors[, 1:2]

# Check norms
cat("Eigenvector norms:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  cat("||U[,", i, "]||^2 =", sum(U_dual[,i]^2), "\n")
}

# Ah! The eigenvectors are unit norm
# So we have: ||U||^2 = 1

# Now check S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
S_dual <- K_Y %*% U_dual

cat("\nS_dual norms:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  cat("||S[,", i, "]||^2 =", sum(S_dual[,i]^2), "\n")
}

# Check the relationship
cat("\nChecking t(U) * S:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  ip <- t(U_dual[,i]) %*% S_dual[,i]
  cat("t(U[,", i, "]) * S[,", i, "] =", Re(ip), "\n")
  
  # Also check t(U) * K_Y * U
  ip2 <- t(U_dual[,i]) %*% K_Y %*% U_dual[,i]
  cat("t(U[,", i, "]) * K_Y * U[,", i, "] =", Re(ip2), "\n\n")
}

# So the issue is that eigen() returns unit-norm eigenvectors
# But we have: K_X * K_Y * u = lambda * u
# And: S = K_Y * u
# So: t(u) * S = t(u) * K_Y * u

# This is NOT lambda unless the eigenvectors are normalized differently

# Let's check if we can recover the expected relationship
cat("Checking if t(U) * K_X * S = lambda * t(U) * U:\n")
for (i in 1:2) {
  lhs <- t(U_dual[,i]) %*% K_X %*% S_dual[,i]
  rhs <- lambda[i] * sum(U_dual[,i]^2)
  cat("Component", i, ": LHS =", Re(lhs), ", RHS =", Re(rhs), "\n")
}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I understand. The relationship is:
- t(U) * K_X * S = lambda (this verifies the eigenvalue equation)
- t(U) * S = t(U) * K_Y * U ≈ 3.33 and 4.02 (NOT lambda)

So the issue in our current implementation is that we're normalizing based on the wrong assumption. Let me fix the implementation with the correct understanding:

edits.old_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors satisfy: K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues and d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The key insight from the feedback: for non-symmetric operators,
  # RSpectra returns unit-norm eigenvectors that are not bi-orthogonal.
  # We have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] (eigenvalue)
  # But we need: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  
  # Normalize to get the correct cross-block relationship
  # Currently: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2
  # We want: t(U_norm[,i]) * S_norm[,i] = d_vals[i]
  # So scale each by 1/sqrt(d_vals[i])
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_factor <- 1 / sqrt(d_vals[i])
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * norm_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * norm_factor
  }
  
  # Now t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(d_vals)
  # Transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)
edits.new_string:
## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors satisfy: K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues and d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # Key relationships:
  # - RSpectra returns unit-norm eigenvectors: ||U_dual||_2 = 1
  # - We have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = t(U_dual[,i]) * K_Y * U_dual[,i]
  # - This is NOT equal to lambda[i] in general
  # - We need to measure the actual inner products and scale accordingly
  
  # Compute actual inner products
  inner_prods <- numeric(ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    inner_prods[i] <- Re(sum(U_dual[, i] * S_dual[, i]))
  }
  
  # Scale to get the correct cross-block relationship
  # We want: t(U_norm) * S_norm = diag(d_vals)
  # Currently: t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(inner_prods)
  # So we need to scale by sqrt(d_vals[i] / inner_prods[i])
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    scale_factor <- sqrt(d_vals[i] / inner_prods[i])
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * scale_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * scale_factor
  }
  
  # Now t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(d_vals)
  # Transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)

  # where lambda are the eigenvalues and d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual

toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
#' Generalised Partial‑Least‑Squares Correlation (gPLS‑C)
#'
#' A memory‑efficient implementation of the GPLSSVD formulation described in
#' Beaton (2020).  It supports arbitrary row‑ and column‑side metrics, uses a
#' matrix‑free dual‑space Lanczos solver when `q >> n`, and delegates the
#' expensive Ay⁻¹ solves to an iterative conjugate‑gradient routine with an
#' optional Jacobi preconditioner.
#'
#' @param X,Y          Numeric or sparse `Matrix`.  Same number of rows `n`.
#' @param Mx,Ax,My,Ay  SPD row / column metrics; if `NULL` → identity.
#' @param ncomp        Number of latent pairs (default 2).
#' @param preproc_x,y  `multivarious` pre‑processors (default: pass‑through).
#' @param dual         `"auto"` (default), `"true"`, `"false"`.  `"false"`
#'                     (primal path) is not yet implemented.
#' @param tol_cg,maxit_cg  Convergence criteria for CG.
#' @param preconditioner   `"jacobi"` (default) or `"none"`.
#' @param verbose      Logical.
#' @param ...          Extra arguments forwarded to `RSpectra::eigs()`.
#' @return             A `cross_projector` object with loadings, scores,
#'                     singular values and CG diagnostics.
#'
#' @export
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix Diagonal solve chol isDiagonal
#' @importFrom multivarious prep init_transform pass cross_projector
#' @importFrom RSpectra eigs
#' @importFrom Rlinsolve lsolve.cg
genplsc <- function(X, Y,
                    Mx = NULL, Ax = NULL, My = NULL, Ay = NULL,
                    ncomp       = 2,
                    preproc_x   = multivarious::pass(),
                    preproc_y   = multivarious::pass(),
                    dual        = c("auto", "true", "false"),
                    tol_cg      = 1e-6,
                    maxit_cg    = 1000,
                    preconditioner = c("jacobi", "none"),
                    verbose     = FALSE,
                    ...) {

  ## ---- 1.  input checks --------------------------------------------------
  dual           <- tolower(match.arg(dual))
  preconditioner <- match.arg(preconditioner)

  if (!is.numeric(X) && !inherits(X, "Matrix"))
    stop("X must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (!is.numeric(Y) && !inherits(Y, "Matrix"))
    stop("Y must be a numeric or Matrix object.")
  if (nrow(X) != nrow(Y))
    stop("X and Y must have the same number of rows.")

  n <- nrow(X);                 p <- ncol(X); q <- ncol(Y)
  use_dual <- (dual == "true") || (dual == "auto" && q > n)
  if (dual == "false")
    stop("Primal path (dual = \"false\") not implemented yet.")

  ## ---- 2.  preprocessing -------------------------------------------------
  px  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_x)
  py  <- multivarious::prep(preproc_y)
  
  # Apply preprocessing
  # For pass-through, init_transform should preserve the input type
  Xp <- multivarious::init_transform(px, X)
  Yp <- multivarious::init_transform(py, Y)
  
  # Ensure matrices are in Matrix format if sparse
  # This follows the pattern from gpca.R
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix") && inherits(X, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it
    if (!identical(px$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix X resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix") && inherits(Y, "Matrix")) {
    # If input was sparse but preprocessing densified it  
    if (!identical(py$method, "pass")) {
      warning("Preprocessing sparse matrix Y resulted in densification")
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to Matrix format for consistency (sparse if possible)
  if (!is(Xp, "Matrix")) Xp <- Matrix::Matrix(Xp, sparse = FALSE)
  if (!is(Yp, "Matrix")) Yp <- Matrix::Matrix(Yp, sparse = FALSE)

  if (is.null(Mx)) Mx <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(My)) My <- Matrix::Diagonal(n)
  if (is.null(Ax)) Ax <- Matrix::Diagonal(p)
  if (is.null(Ay)) Ay <- Matrix::Diagonal(q)

  Ax_reg <- Ax + Matrix::Diagonal(p, 1e-8)
  Ay_reg <- Ay + Matrix::Diagonal(q, 1e-8)

  ## ---- 3.  helpers -------------------------------------------------------
  mat_sqrt <- function(M) {
    if (Matrix::isDiagonal(M)) {
      return(Matrix::Diagonal(x = sqrt(diag(M))))
    }
    ev <- eigen(M, symmetric = TRUE)
    if (any(ev$values < -1e-8))
      stop("Metric matrix not positive semi‑definite.")
    ev$vectors %*% Diagonal(x = sqrt(pmax(ev$values, 0))) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  }

  ## ---- 4.  metric square roots & Gram matrices --------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Preparing metric square roots ...")
  Mx_half <- mat_sqrt(Mx)
  My_half <- mat_sqrt(My)

  inv_Ax   <- solve(Ax_reg)                 # p × p  (small)
  K_X      <- Mx_half %*% Xp %*% inv_Ax %*% t(Xp) %*% Mx_half   # n × n
  R_couple <- Mx_half %*% My_half                              # n × n

  ## ---- 5.  CG solver wrapper --------------------------------------------
  prev_u <- NULL                    # warm‑start across columns & Lanczos calls
  cg_diags <- list()

  solve_Ay <- function(B) {
    if (!is.matrix(B) && !inherits(B, "Matrix")) B <- matrix(B, ncol = 1)
    # For Jacobi preconditioner, create sparse diagonal matrix
    Mprec <- if (preconditioner == "jacobi")
               Matrix::Diagonal(x = diag(Ay_reg)) else NULL
    out <- apply(B, 2, function(b) {
      # lsolve.cg can handle sparse matrices directly
      res <- lsolve.cg(A = Ay_reg,  # Keep sparse if it is
                       B = b,       # uppercase B
                       xinit = if (is.null(prev_u)) NA else prev_u,
                       reltol = tol_cg,
                       maxiter = maxit_cg,
                       preconditioner = Mprec,
                       adjsym = FALSE,
                       verbose = verbose)
      prev_u <<- as.numeric(res$x)                # update warm‑start
      cg_diags[[length(cg_diags)+1]] <<- list(iter = res$iter,
                                              relres = res$reltol)
      as.numeric(res$x)
    })
    matrix(out, nrow = q, ncol = ncol(B))
  }

  ## ---- 6.  K_Y operator --------------------------------------------------
  Y_tilde <- My_half %*% Yp                # n × q
  KY_fun  <- function(v) {
    # Ensure v is numeric/real
    v <- as.numeric(v)
    z <- t(Y_tilde) %*% v                 # q × 1
    u <- solve_Ay(z)                      # q × 1
    as.numeric(Y_tilde %*% u)             # n × 1
  }

  ## ---- 7.  dual‑space operator ------------------------------------------
  if (verbose) message("Running Lanczos eigensolver ...")
  A_op <- function(v, ...) {
    result <- K_X %*% (R_couple %*% KY_fun(v))
    as.numeric(result)
  }

  eig <- RSpectra::eigs(A_op, k = ncomp, n = n, which = "LM", ...)

  # Handle potentially complex eigenvalues
  d_vals    <- sqrt(pmax(Re(eig$values), 0))
  U_dual    <- Re(eig$vectors)             # n × k, Mx‑/My‑weighted space

  ## ---- 8.  back‑project to primal scores --------------------------------
  # The dual-space eigenvectors satisfy: K_X * K_Y * U_dual = lambda * U_dual
  # where lambda are the eigenvalues and d_vals = sqrt(lambda)
  
  # Compute S_dual = K_Y * U_dual
  S_dual <- matrix(0, n, ncomp)
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    S_dual[, i] <- KY_fun(U_dual[, i])
  }
  
  # The key insight from the feedback: for non-symmetric operators,
  # RSpectra returns unit-norm eigenvectors that are not bi-orthogonal.
  # We have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = lambda[i] (eigenvalue)
  # But we need: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
  
  # Normalize to get the correct cross-block relationship
  # Currently: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = d_vals[i]^2
  # We want: t(U_norm[,i]) * S_norm[,i] = d_vals[i]
  # So scale each by 1/sqrt(d_vals[i])
  for (i in 1:ncomp) {
    norm_factor <- 1 / sqrt(d_vals[i])
    U_dual[, i] <- U_dual[, i] * norm_factor
    S_dual[, i] <- S_dual[, i] * norm_factor
  }
  
  # Now t(U_dual) * S_dual = diag(d_vals)
  # Transform to primal scores
  scores_x <- solve(Mx_half, U_dual)
  scores_y <- solve(My_half, S_dual)


  ## ---- 9.  compute loadings from scores ---------------------------------
  # The loadings satisfy the generalized SVD relationships:
  # X^T * My * Y * vy = Ax * vx * d
  # Y^T * Mx * X * vx = Ay * vy * d
  #
  # Given scores with t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals),
  # the loadings are:
  # vx = Ax^{-1} * X^T * My * scores_y * diag(1/d_vals)
  # vy = Ay^{-1} * Y^T * Mx * scores_x * diag(1/d_vals)
  
  # The loadings are automatically Ax/Ay-orthonormal when computed from 
  # the properly scaled scores
  # Check for zero singular values to avoid division by zero
  if (any(d_vals < 1e-10)) {
    warning("Near-zero singular values detected. Using threshold 1e-10.")
    d_vals[d_vals < 1e-10] <- 1e-10
  }
  Dinv <- Matrix::Diagonal(x = 1 / d_vals)
  load_x <- inv_Ax %*% (t(Xp) %*% (My %*% scores_y)) %*% Dinv   # p × k
  
  # Batch CG solve for all k columns at once
  load_y <- solve_Ay(t(Yp) %*% (Mx %*% scores_x) %*% Dinv)      # q × k

  ## ---- 10.  tidy & return ----------------------------------------------
  comp_names <- paste0("comp", seq_len(ncomp))
  colnames(load_x) <- colnames(load_y) <- comp_names
  colnames(scores_x) <- colnames(scores_y) <- comp_names

  # cross_projector expects regular matrices
  # Convert only if necessary (loadings and scores are typically small)
  load_x_final <- if (is(load_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_x) else load_x
  load_y_final <- if (is(load_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(load_y) else load_y
  scores_x_final <- if (is(scores_x, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_x) else scores_x
  scores_y_final <- if (is(scores_y, "Matrix")) as.matrix(scores_y) else scores_y
  
  ret <- multivarious::cross_projector(
    vx = load_x_final,
    vy = load_y_final,
    d  = d_vals,
    preproc_x = px, preproc_y = py,
    scores_x  = scores_x_final,
    scores_y  = scores_y_final,
    ncomp     = ncomp,
    classes   = c("genplscorr", "cross_projector", "projector"),
    solver_info = list(path = "dual",
                       cg_tol = tol_cg,
                       cg_maxit = maxit_cg,
                       cg_diags = cg_diags)
  )
  if (verbose) message("Done.")
  ret
}

lines: -  # The key insight from the feedback: for non-symmetric operators,
-  # RSpectra returns unit-norm eigenvectors that are not bi-orthogonal.
-  # But we need: t(scores_x) * My * scores_y = diag(d_vals)
+  # - We have: t(U_dual[,i]) * S_dual[,i] = t(U_dual[,i]) * K_Y * U_dual[,i]
+  # - This is NOT equal to lambda[i] in general
+  # - We need to measure the actual inner products and scale accordingly
-  # Normalize to get the correct cross-block relationship
+  # Scale to get the correct cross-block relationship
+  # So we need to scale by sqrt(d_vals[i] / inner_prods[i])

# Final debug - check what genplsc is producing

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Run with identity metrics
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

cat("Singular values:", fit$d, "\n\n")

# Check cross-block reconstruction
cov_xy <- t(fit$scores_x) %*% fit$scores_y
cat("Cross-block covariance:\n")

# Check loadings orthogonality

print(round(t(fit$vx) %*% fit$vx, 4))

cat("\nLoadings orthogonality t(vy) * vy:\n")

# Direct check - what should the loadings be?
# For identity metrics: vx = X^T * scores_y / d
cat("\n\nDirect computation of loadings:\n")
vx_direct <- t(X) %*% fit$scores_y %*% diag(1/fit$d)
vy_direct <- t(Y) %*% fit$scores_x %*% diag(1/fit$d)

cat("Direct vx orthogonality:\n")
print(round(t(vx_direct) %*% vx_direct, 4))

cat("\nDirect vy orthogonality:\n")  
print(round(t(vy_direct) %*% vy_direct, 4))

# Compare with fit loadings
cat("\n\nMax difference between computed and direct loadings:\n")
cat("vx:", max(abs(fit$vx - vx_direct)), "\n")
cat("vy:", max(abs(fit$vy - vy_direct)), "\n")

content.text:
Great! The cross-block reconstruction is now perfect (diagonal = [8.1225, 6.4169] matching the singular values). However, the loadings orthogonality is wrong - we're getting the singular values instead of the identity matrix.

This tells me that the loadings are NOT Omega-orthonormal. They satisfy a different relationship. Let me check what the correct normalization should be for generalized PLS:

todos.content: Fix loadings normalization to ensure Omega-orthogonality

content.text:
Looking at the diagnostic tests, I see that for generalized PLS, the loadings should be Omega-orthonormal:
- t(vx) * Ax * vx = I
- t(vy) * Ay * vy = I

But we're getting t(vx) * vx = diag(d) instead. This suggests we need to normalize the loadings differently. Let me check the GSVD relationships:

input.content:
# Fix loadings normalization for Omega-orthogonality

library(Matrix)
devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# For testing, use non-identity metrics
Mx <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n)
My <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n) 
Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p, p)) + diag(p)
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q, q)) + diag(q)

# Run genplsc
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx = Mx, Ax = Ax, My = My, Ay = Ay, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

# Check current state
cat("Cross-block reconstruction (should be diagonal):\n")
cov_xy <- t(fit$scores_x) %*% My %*% fit$scores_y
print(round(cov_xy, 4))

cat("\n\nCurrent loadings Omega-orthogonality:\n")
cat("t(vx) * Ax * vx:\n")
print(round(t(fit$vx) %*% Ax %*% fit$vx, 4))

cat("\nt(vy) * Ay * vy:\n")
print(round(t(fit$vy) %*% Ay %*% fit$vy, 4))

# The issue is that our loadings are not normalized correctly
# Let's renormalize them to be Omega-orthonormal

# For Omega-orthonormal loadings, we need:
# t(vx) * Ax * vx = I
# Currently we have some other diagonal matrix

# Extract current norms
Omega_x_diag <- diag(t(fit$vx) %*% Ax %*% fit$vx)
Omega_y_diag <- diag(t(fit$vy) %*% Ay %*% fit$vy)

cat("\n\nCurrent Omega norms:\n")
cat("Omega_x diagonal:", Omega_x_diag, "\n")
cat("Omega_y diagonal:", Omega_y_diag, "\n")

# Renormalize loadings
vx_norm <- fit$vx %*% diag(1/sqrt(Omega_x_diag))
vy_norm <- fit$vy %*% diag(1/sqrt(Omega_y_diag))

cat("\n\nAfter renormalization:\n")
cat("t(vx_norm) * Ax * vx_norm:\n")
print(round(t(vx_norm) %*% Ax %*% vx_norm, 6))

cat("\nt(vy_norm) * Ay * vy_norm:\n")
print(round(t(vy_norm) %*% Ay %*% vy_norm, 6))

# But wait - if we change the loadings, will the scores still satisfy
# the cross-block reconstruction?
# The answer is NO - we need to fix this in the algorithm itself

devtools::load_all()

set.seed(123)
n <- 10; p <- 5; q <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# For testing, use non-identity metrics
Mx <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n)
My <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n) 
Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p, p)) + diag(p)
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q, q)) + diag(q)

# Run genplsc
fit <- genplsc(X, Y, Mx = Mx, Ax = Ax, My = My, Ay = Ay, ncomp = 2, verbose = FALSE)

# Check current state
cat("Cross-block reconstruction (should be diagonal):\n")
cov_xy <- t(fit$scores_x) %*% My %*% fit$scores_y
print(round(cov_xy, 4))

cat("\n\nCurrent loadings Omega-orthogonality:\n")
cat("t(vx) * Ax * vx:\n")
print(round(t(fit$vx) %*% Ax %*% fit$vx, 4))

cat("\nt(vy) * Ay * vy:\n")
print(round(t(fit$vy) %*% Ay %*% fit$vy, 4))

# The issue is that our loadings are not normalized correctly
# Let's renormalize them to be Omega-orthonormal

# For Omega-orthonormal loadings, we need:
# t(vx) * Ax * vx = I
# Currently we have some other diagonal matrix

# Extract current norms
Omega_x_diag <- diag(t(fit$vx) %*% Ax %*% fit$vx)
Omega_y_diag <- diag(t(fit$vy) %*% Ay %*% fit$vy)

cat("\n\nCurrent Omega norms:\n")
cat("Omega_x diagonal:", Omega_x_diag, "\n")
cat("Omega_y diagonal:", Omega_y_diag, "\n")

# Renormalize loadings
vx_norm <- fit$vx %*% diag(1/sqrt(Omega_x_diag))
vy_norm <- fit$vy %*% diag(1/sqrt(Omega_y_diag))

cat("\n\nAfter renormalization:\n")
cat("t(vx_norm) * Ax * vx_norm:\n")
print(round(t(vx_norm) %*% Ax %*% vx_norm, 6))

cat("\nt(vy_norm) * Ay * vy_norm:\n")
print(round(t(vy_norm) %*% Ay %*% vy_norm, 6))

# But wait - if we change the loadings, will the scores still satisfy
# the cross-block reconstruction?
# The answer is NO - we need to fix this in the algorithm itself

content.text:
I see multiple issues:
1. The cross-block reconstruction is not diagonal (has off-diagonals of -34 and 41.7)
2. The loadings are not Omega-orthogonal (has off-diagonals after normalization)
3. The whole approach seems wrong for non-identity metrics

Looking back at the diagnostic test file and the feedback, I think the issue is more fundamental. For non-identity metrics, the dual-space operator K_X * R_couple * K_Y is not giving us the correct generalized PLS solution.

Let me reconsider the mathematical formulation. For generalized PLS, we want to find the SVD of:
Ax^{-1/2} * X^T * My * Y * Ay^{-1/2}

Let me check if there's a simpler primal formulation we should use instead:

input.content:
# Test primal formulation for small p, q

library(Matrix)
library(GSVD) # If available

set.seed(123)
n <- 20; p <- 10; q <- 12
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
Y <- matrix(rnorm(n * q), n, q)

# Non-identity metrics
Mx <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n)
My <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(n*n), n, n)) + diag(n)
Ax <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(p*p), p, p)) + diag(p)
Ay <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(q*q), q, q)) + diag(q)

# Primal formulation: compute generalized SVD directly
# We want the SVD of: Ax^{-1/2} * X^T * My * Y * Ay^{-1/2}

# Compute matrix square roots
Ax_half_inv <- solve(chol(Ax))  # Ax^{-1/2}
Ay_half_inv <- solve(chol(Ay))  # Ay^{-1/2}

# Form the matrix for SVD
C <- Ax_half_inv %*% t(X) %*% My %*% Y %*% Ay_half_inv

# Regular SVD
svd_C <- svd(C, nu = 2, nv = 2)

cat("Singular values from primal SVD:", svd_C$d[1:2], "\n\n")

# Extract components
# u are the Ax^{-1/2}-transformed X loadings
# v are the Ay^{-1/2}-transformed Y loadings
u_trans <- svd_C$u[, 1:2]
v_trans <- svd_C$v[, 1:2]
d_vals <- svd_C$d[1:2]

# Transform back to get actual loadings
vx <- Ax_half_inv %*% u_trans  # p x 2
vy <- Ay_half_inv %*% v_trans  # q x 2

# Check Omega-orthogonality
cat("Omega-orthogonality of loadings:\n")
cat("t(vx) * Ax * vx:\n")
print(round(t(vx) %*% Ax %*% vx, 6))

cat("\nt(vy) * Ay * vy:\n")
print(round(t(vy) %*% Ay %*% vy, 6))

# Compute scores
# From the GSVD relationships:
# scores_x = X * vx
# scores_y = Y * vy
scores_x <- X %*% vx
scores_y <- Y %*% vy

# Check cross-block reconstruction
cat("\n\nCross-block reconstruction:\n")
cov_xy <- t(scores_x) %*% My %*% scores_y
print(round(cov_xy, 6))
cat("\nExpected diagonal:", d_vals, "\n")

Ax_half_inv <- solve(chol(Ax))  # Ax^{-1/2}
Ay_half_inv <- solve(chol(Ay))  # Ay^{-1/2}

C <- Ax_half_inv %*% t(X) %*% My %*% Y %*% Ay_half_inv

svd_C <- svd(C, nu = 2, nv = 2)

v_trans <- svd_C$v[, 1:2]
